With these criteria in mind, we find three categories: well-constrained and promising (Ursa Minor, Sculptor and Draco), well-constrained but less promising (Carina, Fornax and Leo I), and poorly constrained (Sextans and Leo II).

sources.summary: The paper models Carina orbits and finds filamentary infall is only plausible for lower proper motions and high mass, though modified halo growth can raise its apocentre.

quotes.quote: Here we test this scenario by making use of the proper motions of the Fornax, Sculptor, Ursa Minor and Carina dwarf spheroidals, and trace their orbits back through several variations of the Milky Way's potential and account for dynamical friction.
One can see that by 12 Gyrs for the Carina dSph, it is possible to reach 1 Mpc from the Milky Way, even with a reasonable mass ( 10^8 ).
Ursa Minor and Carina would both require masses of the order of 10^ 10 and these masses come with the condition that the proper motions are at least 1 below the mean measurement.

sources.summary: The paper includes Carina among the classical Milky Way dwarf spheroidals and finds that, under isotropic Jeans modeling, it prefers a cored dark-matter halo.

quotes.quote: Interestingly, even in this limit, not all of the dSphs prefer large constant-density cores: the Sculptor dSph prefers a cusp while Carina, Draco and Leo I prefer cores.
We use a spherical Jeans analyses to constrain the density profile slopes of the eight classical MW dSphs: Carina, Draco, Fornax, Leo I, Leo II, Sculptor, Sextans, and Ursa Minor.
Three galaxies (Carina, Draco, and Leo I) demonstrate a preference for a core ( 0 ).

sources.summary: The paper reports H.E.S.S. observations of Carina as a dwarf spheroidal dark-matter target, finding no gamma-ray signal and deriving annihilation cross-section limits dependent on the halo model.

quotes.quote: The Sculptor and Carina dwarf spheroidal galaxies were observed with H.E.S.S. for 11.8 and 14.8 hours, respectively Viana 1036 . No gamma-ray signal was detected at the nominal positions of these galaxies above 220 GeV and 320 GeV, respectively. Constraints on the dark matter velocity-weighted annihilation cross section for both, the Sculptor and Carina dwarf galaxies range from v 10^ 21 down to 10^ 22 cm ^3 s ^ 1 , depending on the dark-matter halo model.

sources.summary: The paper treats Carina as the Local Group dwarf whose Milky Way interaction, star-formation history, and modeled evolution motivated and validated the authors' environmental star-formation formalism.

quotes.quote: In Paper I it was shown that it is possible to study the linear response of a gravitationally bound group of stars (e.g., a dwarf galaxy) in this way, and to capture the essence of what is observed in a dwarf galaxy like Carina during its interaction with the MW.

Despite its success in reproducing the star formation history of the Carina dwarf galaxy presented in Paper I, the formalism there developed did not account properly for the deformation of the dwarf galaxy because of tidal interaction, and hence for the star formation instability presented in a real system.
The compatibility of the result was confirmed against a numerical integration of the evolution of an extensively studied LG dwarf galaxy (Carina) 2011A&A...525A..99P .

sources.summary: Carina was a classical dSph ATCA target; its data favor low ellipticity, and candidate FoV sources would require large annihilation rates under a point-source DM interpretation.

quotes.quote: The targets included three classical dSphs (CDS), Carina, Fornax, and Sculptor, and three ultra-faint dSphs (UDS), BootesII, Hercules and Segue2, for a total of 123 hours of observing time.
However, the data available for our sample of targets (which include Carina, Fornax, and Sculptor) do not support this possibility, and point towards low ellipticities (of order of 10-20 ).

Intriguingly, only the sources in Carina and Hercules FoVs would require pretty large annihilation rates, while all the other values are well compatible with constraints from other DM indirect searches.

sources.summary: Carina is modeled as one of the Milky Way dSphs; its dark halo parameters are mapped, and the paper groups it with higher-M/L systems having more flattened halos.

quotes.quote: In this section, we describe the best-fit dark halo model for each of seven MW dSphs (Carina, Fornax, Sculptor, Sextans, Draco, Leo I, and Leo II) and five M31 dSphs (And I, And II, And III, And V, and And VII) and present confidence maps between each parameter.

In Figure  fig:fig2 and  fig:fig3 , we present 68  ( 1 ), 95  , and 99  confidence levels of contours in the two-dimensional plane of Q- _z , _0- _z , _z , b_ halo _z , and i- _z for all sample dSphs: Carina, Fornax, Sculptor, Sextans, Draco, and Leo I are shown in Figure  fig:fig2 , and Leo II, And I, And II, And III, And V, and And VII are shown in Figure  fig:fig3 .

In light of these predictions, it is interesting to remark that the dark halos of Fornax, Sculptor, Leo I, and Leo II with relatively low M/L s tend to be less flattened than Carina, Sextans, and Draco with high M/L s.

sources.summary: The paper measures the Carina dSph distance from infrared RR Lyrae photometry, obtaining a true distance modulus consistent with other Araucaria determinations.

quotes.quote: We obtained single-phase near-infrared (NIR) magnitudes in the J and K band for a sample of 33 RR Lyrae stars in the Carina dSph galaxy. Applying different theoretical and empirical calibrations of the NIR period-luminosity-metallicity relation for RR Lyrae stars, we find consistent results and obtain a true, reddening-corrected distance modulus of 20.118 0.017 (statistical) 0.11 (systematic) mag. This value is in excellent agreement with the results obtained in the context of the Araucaria project from NIR photometry of Red Clump stars ( 20.165 0.015 ) and Tip of Red Giant Branch ( 20.09 0.03 0.12 mag in J band, 20.14 0.04 0.14 mag in K band), as well as with most independent distance determinations to this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper derives Carina I proper motion from Walker probabilities, finds agreement with Helmi, and notes that systematic uncertainty dominates both measurements.

quotes.quote: Carina I We use the catalog of Walker_09_clas with continuous probabilities. Our proper motion agrees within 1 with that by Helmi_18 . For both works the systematic error dominates over the statistical uncertainty.

sources.summary: The paper describes Carina as a bright Milky Way dwarf spheroidal with low inferred central dark-matter density, large half-light radius effects, and a lower-density subhalo orbit avoiding close disk passage.

quotes.quote: The dSphs that have not come close to the Milky Way center (like Fornax, Carina and Sextans) are less dense in DM than those that have come closer (like Draco and Ursa Minor).
From figure fig:corr_rho_pericenter , we see that Fornax, Carina and Sextans have lower _ 150 values in cISO fits than NFW ones, because their half-light radii are considerably larger than 150 pc.

To explain TBTF for the bright MW dSphs in light of the Gaia data using collisionless DM models, one must host Draco and Ursa Minor in the densest subhalos with pericenter distances close to 30 - 40 kpc, while explaining why Carina, Fornax and Sextans are hosted by lower-density subhalos that did not come close to the MW disk.

sources.summary: The paper treats Carina as a massive Milky Way satellite with a long inferred quenching timescale, unusually circular orbit, large pericenter, and reduced environmental-quenching efficiency.

quotes.quote: As evident in Figure fig:tqmstar-punchline , however, there are three dwarfs (Sagittarius, Fornax, and Carina) with inferred quenching timescales significantly longer than the short quenching timescales inferred from previous work.

The other two satellites (Fornax and Carina), for which we measure _ quench > 4 Gyr , suffer far less from the issues associated with modeling Sag I, such that the long quenching timescales inferred for these systems are much more reliable.

Taken together, the orbital eccentricity and pericentric distance for Carina and Fornax are consistent with orbits about the MW that would lead to less efficient quenching via environmental processes (e.g. ram-pressure stripping), which operate with greater efficiency deeper in the host potential.

sources.summary: Carina is described as an elongated, rotating dwarf whose properties can be reproduced by a late-stage tidally stirred disky dwarf model.

quotes.quote: Obvious candidates for tidally stirred dwarfs in the vicinity of the Milky Way include the Sagittarius and Carina dwarfs, although most of dSph galaxies in the Local Group show signs of tidal extensions. In the case of the Sagittarius dwarf it has been demonstrated that its present structural and kinematic properties can be reproduced by a model in which the initially disky progenitor has just passed the second pericenter on its orbit around the Milky Way ( ). In this model the stellar component still preserves its bar-like shape (slightly inclined with respect to the orbit) and shows very little rotation. The recent compilation of data for the Carina dSph galaxy, showing its elongated shape and significant rotation, can also be reproduced by a late-stage tidally stirred disky dwarf ( ).
For example, tidal stirring can account for the non-spherical shape and remnant rotation in Carina and a merger can explain the prolate rotation in Andromeda II.

sources.summary: The paper says XMM observations of Carina give the strongest dwarf-spheroidal X-ray bound on the dark-matter decay width, using its J-factor in the constraint comparison.

quotes.quote: We checked that, among all the dSphs observed by XMM, Carina gives the strongest bounds on . The corresponding J-factor (in the field-of-view of XMM) for this dSph has been extracted from .
The yellow solid line in each panel represents the 95 C.L. upper limit on , coming from the XMM observation of dSph Carina .

sources.summary: The paper gives Carina a high probability of Magellanic association, finding elevated odds in the second-passage case and significant odds even for first passage.

quotes.quote: In particular, four of the classical dwarfs -- Carina, Draco, Fornax and Ursa Minor -- each have a 50 probability of once belonging to the Magellanic system, thus providing a possible explanation for the ``plane of satellites'' conundrum.

In particular, four of the classical dSph -- Carina, Draco, Fornax and Ursa Minor -- have a roughly equal chance to either be accreted with the Magellanic system or originate from elsewhere (in case of Carina, the chances are even higher, 2:1 ).

In this case, all eleven galaxies from the first group are still highly likely to be current LMC satellites, but among the second group, only Carina retains a significant (40--50 ) probability of association, Grus II, Hydra II, Pisces II and Tucana II stay at the level 20--40 , and remaining galaxies are not compatible with the distribution of Magellanic debris.

sources.summary: The paper notes Carina’s mapped velocity-dispersion profile, uniquely episodic star-formation history among extended-history dSphs, and possible large-scale velocity gradient.

quotes.quote: To date, the line-of-sight velocity dispersion profiles in the Fornax, Draco, Ursa Minor, Carina, Leo I, Leo II, Sextans and Sculptor dSphs have been mapped to the (rather poorly defined concept of an) optical edge mateo97, kleyna02, W04, W06a, Munoz2005, Sohn2006, Koch06, Batt06, Walker06 see Figure 2 for a sample of profiles obtained by our group.

Episodic star formation with clearly distinguished periods of star formation has been detected only in Carina; all other dSphs with extended star formation histories show evidence of long-lasting activity without obvious pauses.
Carina may be an exception, as have recently detected a velocity gradient on large scales (beyond the nominal King-model fit `tidal' radius) in this dSph.

sources.summary: Carina is grouped with nearby high-M/L dwarf spheroidals where extra-tidal stars would argue against dominant dark matter and significant dark matter content.

quotes.quote: Carina, Ursa Minor and Draco, on the other hand, cannot contain a dominating dark matter component if the observed 'extra-tidal' stars are located exterior to the tidal radii of these systems.

Even with the large error bars, which are due to the large observational uncertainties in the orbital and structural parameters, figure 1 clearly demonstrates that in this case Carina, Draco and Ursa Minor could not be dark matter dominated (see also Moore 1996).
The existence of 'extra-tidal' stars in Carina, Draco and Ursa Minor therefore would demonstrate that these dSphs cannot contain significant amounts of dark matter.

gmw_00000633.name: NGC 5253
gmw_00000633.overview: NGC 5253 is a nearby blue compact dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1102.0652, arXiv:1411.3844] in the Centaurus A/M 83 group [arXiv:1102.0652] undergoing an intense, centrally concentrated starburst [arXiv:1109.6360]. This active starburst is likely fueled by the accretion of infalling molecular gas [arXiv:1009.1416, arXiv:2402.17004] and is dominated by extremely young, dense super star clusters [arXiv:1102.4854, arXiv:2108.04387].

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 5253 as a starburst dwarf with unusually high star-formation efficiency, many young clusters, and accreted gas likely feeding its current starburst.

quotes.quote: A counterexample to the ``lots of gas, lots of stars" theory is the case of NGC 5253. In this starburst dwarf galaxy, the star formation efficiency appears to be extremely high, SFE 75 on 100 pc scales, with H_2 masses based on both CO 1997ApJ...474L..11T, 2002AJ....124..877M and dust emission (Turner et al. \ 2008, in prep.) This SFE is nearly two orders of magnitude higher than generally seen on GMC spatial scales in the Galaxy. NGC 5253 has several hundred young clusters, including several SSCs 1996AJ....112.1886G,1997AJ....114.1834C .

One difference between this starburst and that of the Antennae is that NGC 5253 is a dwarf galaxy, with an estimated mass of 10^ 9 M _ , which was probably originally a gas-poor dwarf spheroidal galaxy that has accreted some gas 1989ApJ...338..789C . Unlike the Antennae, which are in the midst of a full-blown major merger, NGC 5253 is relatively isolated, although part of the Cen A--M83 group 2007AJ....133..504K . The prominent dust lane entering the minor axis is the probable cause of the starburst. Molecular gas is present in the dust lane, and this gas is observed to be falling into the galaxy near the current starburst 2002AJ....124..877M, 2008AJ....135..527K .

sources.summary: The paper observes NGC 5253's embedded supernebula in [S IV], finding a two-component line profile and interpreting the blue wing as gas flowing out of the molecular cloud.

quotes.quote: The nearby dwarf starburst galaxy NGC 5253 hosts a deeply embedded radio-infrared supernebula excited by thousands of O stars. We have observed this source in the 10.5 m line of S^ +3 at 3.8 spectral and 1.4 spatial resolution, using the high resolution spectrometer TEXES on the IRTF. The line profile cannot be fit well by a single Gaussian. The best simple fit describes the gas with two Gaussians, one near the galactic velocity with FWHM 33.6 and another of similiar strength and FWHM 94 centered 20 to the blue. This suggests a model for the supernebula in which gas flows towards us out of the molecular cloud, as in a ''blister'' or ''champagne flow'' or in the regions modelled by ZH .

The supernebula in NGC 5253 is the most compact embedded super star cluster known, one of the most obscured, and one of the very brightest. It is also probably the youngest, as shown by its small size, high gas density about 3-4 10^4 cm^ 3 , TB04 , and the very high ionization state of the gas ( CB99 arrived at an age of 2-3 Myr from analysis of the radiation field, agreeing with 3 Myr AAH04 found from the near-infrared continuum). It is in a complex region of many star clusters and molecular gas.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5253 soft X-ray emission around its central starburst, a more extended southern outflow in combined Chandra data, and declining star formation marking the end of a wind phase.

quotes.quote: NGC 5253 From Figure fig:ngc5253_xraycontour , the strongest soft X-ray emission is in the center of the galaxy where the starburst is concentrated. There are two other notable outflow features.

For NGC 5253, the detected outflow surrounds much of the central star forming region and is strongest in the south. Marlowe1995 measure velocities of the \ detected shells of 35 km s ^ 1 with physical extents of 1 kpc. Summers2004 find the extent of the X-ray emission to be somewhat smaller ( 0.5 kpc). However, using combined Chandra observations, we find a more extended region in the south of the galaxy that reaches 2.4 kpc in projection which cannot be explained by dynamical arguments and short star formation timescales.

Furthermore, the SFR in NGC 5253 has declined by nearly a factor of 6 over the duration of the burst. The declining SFRs and small-scale outflow in NGC 5253 indicate that we are seeing the end of a wind phase (see also Figure fig:xray_stellar_mass ), rather than just the beginning of an outflow as has been previously suggested Summers2004 .

sources.summary: The paper presents high-resolution JVLA and TEXES observations of ionized gas in NGC 5253, describing its dwarf starburst host, complex supernebula kinematics, arms, and blue-shifted gas.

quotes.quote: The youngest, closest and most compact embedded massive star cluster known excites the supernebula in the nearby dwarf galaxy NGC 5253. It is a crucial target and test case for studying the birth and evolution of the most massive star clusters.

The supernebula in NGC 5253 is the closest and best target for such study and the ionized gas has been extensively studied at radio and infrared wavelengths. NGC 5253 is a peculiar dwarf spheroidal galaxy at 3.8 Mpc distance 2004ApJ...608...42S and the site of an intense starburst that has formed hundreds of massive star clusters in a few hundred Myr 2015ApJ...811...75C, 2016ApJ...823...38S,2013A&A...550A..88W . The radio and infrared emission of the galaxy is dominated by a single remarkable supernebula, which has been extensively observed ( 2003Natur.423..621T , 2004ApJ...602L..85T , 2012ApJ...755...59B , and references therein).

We have presented measurements of the ionized gas in the great nebula in NGC 5253 with high spatial and spectral resolution. The nebula is unexpectedly complex in both spatial structure and gas kinematics. The images show that The ionized gas has has formed 'arms' north-west of the main source.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 5253 was modeled as a low-metallicity dwarf where DUSTY fits favor larger grains, no silicate grains, and carbon-based dust in a local star-forming region.

quotes.quote: The SEDs of SBS0335-052, (1/40 solar metallicity), and NGC 5253 (1/6 solar metallicity) have been modeled using DUSTY plante+02 vanzi+04 . SBS0335-052 was shown by ISOCAM to harbor a significant amount of dust, due to absorption of the 9.7 m silicate feature, accounting for A_ V = 30 to 40 thuan+99 . These mid infrared (MIR) results alone point to a deeply-embedded SSC. Applying the DUSTY model to these two galaxies (Figure fig:sbs , Figure fig:ngc5253 ), the authors note that the observations require dust with a flatter size distribution, favoring larger grain sizes than those in our Galaxy.

While silicate dust is obviously present in SBS0335-052, model results point to the absence of silicate grains in NGC 5253, where only carbon-based grains are used. The non-negligible dust masses that are determined from the observed SEDs, attributed to a single cluster in each case, are of the order of 10 ^ 5 M _ Note that the dust mass in SBS0335-052 was overestimated in plante+02 because the 60 m ISOPHOT point was too high . If stochastic heating does prove to be an important process in these environments, then the mass of dust will necessarily decrease. In both of these cases, the dust that is modeled using DUSTY is perhaps characteristic of local environments around star clusters, rather than dust that appears to be globally distributed throughout the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper reports WC stars in NGC 5253, describes complex spatially separated Wolf-Rayet regions, and notes a distinct bright line region of unclear origin.

quotes.quote: We report the detection of WC stars in five Wolf-Rayet (W-R) galaxies: He 2-10, NGC 3049, NGC 3125, NGC 5253 and Tol 89. The faint broad \ line requires sufficiently high S/N ( 40) to be detected explaining the non-detection of this WC feature in previous observations. From the measurement of W-R emission lines ( + , , and ), we conclude that all W-R regions contain a mixed population of WNL, and early WC stars.

The spatial distribution of W-R stars in this object is complex (see Fig.  N5253_SPAT ). There are two W-R regions, labeled as A and B , located at the two peaks intensity of the continuum and nebular lines distributions. The brightest W-R region ( B ), however, corresponds to the faintest continuum and emission lines region. For this galaxy we made a distinction between the distribution of the \ and \ lines. There is indeed a third bright `` \ region'' (region H in Schaerer , 1997) located 6 \ North-East of the brightest W-R region B .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5253 as a low-metallicity dwarf spheroidal with an embedded supernebula, external gas, an infalling molecular streamer, and dust-rich Cloud D shaped by cluster enrichment.

quotes.quote: The massive cluster in NGC 5253 may be the closest example of a embedded cluster still in the process of formation. Despite having little gas and no disk, this dwarf spheroidal galaxy has formed hundreds of clusters in the recent past ( ). This unlikely environment is the location of the ``supernebula", a bright and compact radio continuum source associated with a luminous HII region ( ).

Most of the gas in NGC 5253 resides outside the galaxy, in a halo of HI filaments and in a molecular streamer of 200 pc extent detected in CO ( ). The streamer is infalling along the minor axis of the galaxy.

The dust mass is high for the metallicity of the galaxy: gas-to-dust radio is 50:1, sub-solar, much lower than the 500-700 value expected based on the low metallicity of NGC 5253. We posit that the dust mass is almost entirely produced by the stars of the cluster; STARBURST99 models predict twice this yield in metals for a cluster of this luminosity and 3.5 Myr age, so this model is entirely plausible.

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA CO(2--1) observations of NGC 5253, resolving molecular clouds, deriving its CO-to-H2 conversion factor, and linking central cloud properties to starburst feedback.

quotes.quote: We present high-spatial-resolution ( 0 2 , or 3 pc) CO(2--1) observations of the nearest young starburst dwarf galaxy, NGC 5253, taken with the Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array. We have identified 118 molecular clouds with average values of 4.3 pc in radius and 2.2 in velocity dispersion, which comprise the molecular cloud complexes observed previously with 100 pc resolution. We derive for the first time in this galaxy the I (CO) - N (H _2 ) conversion factor, X = 4.1^ +5.9 _ 2.4 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 (K ) ^ 1 , based on the virial method. The line-width and mass-to-size relations of the resolved molecular clouds present an offset on average toward higher line-widths and masses with respect to quiescent regions in other nearby spiral galaxies and our Galaxy. The offset in the scaling relation reaches its maximum in regions close to the central starburst, where velocity dispersions are 0.5 dex higher and gas mass surface densities are as high as _ H_2 = 10 ^3 pc ^ 2 .

Furthermore, the star formation efficiencies (SFEs) of some of these clouds exceed those found in star-cluster-forming clouds within our Galaxy. We conclude that once a super star cluster is formed, the parent molecular clouds are rapidly dispersed by the destructive stellar feedback, which results in such a high SFE in the central starburst of NGC 5253.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 5253 as the most active target, with an extremely young clustered starburst, long star-formation activity, Wolf-Rayet population, and perturbed inflowing HI.

quotes.quote: NGC 5253 harbors an extremely young starburst with many massive star clusters e.g., calzetti97,cresci05 , with the majority of them in the central regions having ages of 1-10 Myr e.g., calzetti15 . NGC 5253 is overall more active than NGC 1705. Nevertheless, its SFH shows little evidence of a burst over the last 10-20 Myr, but this could result from the extreme crowding and incompleteness of the central region where most of the current SF is concentrated cignoni19 . NGC 5253's CMD suggests that star-formation activity has been occurring for 450 Myr, similar to the dynamical time of the galaxy mcquinn10 . There is also a substantial population of W-R stars schaerer97,westmoquette13 , consistent with the young age of the central clusters.

The \ in NGC 5253 is highly perturbed e.g., kobulnicky08,lopezsanchez12,lelli14 , with infalling neutral gas that is apparently triggering the powerful current starburst e.g., lopezsanchez12,turner15 . In fact, the \ kinematics is consistent with a disk-like structure dominated by a radial inflow motion of 25 \ lelli14 .

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 5253 is a nearby galaxy with compact but resolved 18 micron emission, dominated by a nuclear super star cluster with high surface brightness.

quotes.quote: For NGC 5253, Spitzer IRS SH high-resolution (R 600) spectrum at 18 m, with spectral mapping mode (total covered area is 22 2 33 4) is available bei06 . The 18 m emission of NGC 5253 is spatially compact but resolvable. For LIRGs whose core emission is clearly extended (VV 114 E, ESO 602 G025, NGC 5253, NGC 1614, and Arp 193), the actual values are adopted for the emission size.

NGC 5253 is a very nearby galaxy (distance 3.9 Mpc), dominated by a nuclear super star cluster gor01,van04,alo04 . We estimated the emission surface brightness of the super star cluster in the very nearby galaxy NGC 5253 to be 10 ^ 13 L _ kpc ^ 2 . For NGC 5253, the classification is from con91 and wal87 , and ``WR'' means a Wolf Rayet galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5253 as a nearby dwarf starburst with a narrow ionization cone, young massive clusters, and ionizing radiation reaching large radii or escaping.

quotes.quote: Here, we report a new detection of such an ionization cone in a starburst galaxy, NGC 5253. Its narrow opening angle suggests that orientation may play an important role in the detectability of escaping Lyman continuum radiation. NGC 5253 is a nearby, dwarf galaxy undergoing intense, centrally concentrated star formation. The most recent episode of star formation produced massive super star clusters with ages of just a few Myr e.g., b:calzetti_aj97 .

Based on our detection of an ionization cone in NGC 5253, we suggest that an orientation bias can explain the difficulty in detecting escaping ionizing radiation in starburst galaxies. We observe evidence that ionizing radiation from NGC 5253 is traveling to large radii, if not escaping the galaxy entirely.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 5253's central ionized gas with FLAMES integral-field spectroscopy, deriving density, temperature, ionization and abundance maps, and finding two localized nitrogen-enhanced regions tied to star clusters.

quotes.quote: The 2D structure of the main physical and chemical properties of the ionized gas in the core of NGC 5253 has been studied. Optical integral field spectroscopy (IFS) data has been obtained with FLAMES Argus and lower resolution gratings of the Giraffe spectrograph. We derived 2D maps for different tracers of electron density ( n_e ), electron temperature ( T_e ) and ionization degree.

There are two locations with enhanced N/O . The first ( (N/O) 0.95 ) occupies an area of about 80 pc 35 pc and is associated to two super star clusters. The second ( (N/O) 1.17 ), reported here for the first time, is associated to two moderately massive ( 2-4 10^4  M _ ) and relatively old ( 10  Myr) clusters.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 5253 as a nearby peculiar BCD starburst and maps optical depth and singly ionized helium abundance across its main ionized region.

quotes.quote: Received: 1 March 2013 ; Accepted: 19 March 2013 NGC 5253 is a nearby peculiar Blue Compact Dwarf (BCD) galaxy that, on account of its proximity, provides an ideal laboratory for detailed spatial study of starburst galaxies.

In contrast to this, the main body of NGC 5253 resembles that of a dwarf elliptical galaxy and recently, three potentially massive ( 10^5 M _ ) and old ( 1-2 Gyr) star clusters have been found in the outskirts of the galaxy har12 . Finally, NGC 5253 is best-known for being one of the few examples (and the closest) of a galaxy presenting a confirmed local excess in nitrogen see e.g. wal89 . mon10 Paper I mon12 Paper II wes13 Paper III We are carrying out a detailed study of this galaxy using Integral Field Spectroscopy (IFS).

We present a map for the optical depth at 3889 in the main \ of NGC 5253 . (3889) is elevated over an extended and circular area of 30 pc in diameter, centered at the Super Star Cluster(s), where it reaches its maximum. The singly ionized helium abundance, y^+ , has been mapped using extinction corrected fluxes of six \ lines, realistic assumptions for T_e , n_e , and the stellar absorption equivalent width as well as the most recent emissivities.

sources.summary: The study finds NGC 5253 has minor-axis radial motions consistent with inflow, weak rotation, and a high-excitation disturbed disk geometry.

quotes.quote: Seven galaxies in our sample have a kinematically disturbed disk: NGC 625, NGC 1569, NGC 4163, NGC 4449, NGC 5253, UGC 6456, and UGC 9128. For these objects, we used channels maps and PV-diagrams to estimate ( _ kin , _ kin ) , V_ sys , PA _ kin , and the rotation velocity in the outer parts. For the inclination, we assumed the optical value.

NGC 5253 has been previously studied by Kobulnicky2008 and LopezSanchez2012 , who discussed the possibility of gas inflows/outflows along the minor axis of the galaxy. The data are, indeed, consistent with a disk with V_ rot < 5 km s ^ 1 and V_ rad 25 km s ^ 1 (see Fig. fig:n5253 ). Shadowing of the X-ray emission indicates that the southern side of the galaxy is the nearest one to the observer Ott2005a , suggesting that the radial motions are an inflow.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 5253 as a nearby blue compact dwarf starburst host of two Type Ia supernovae, enabling decades of archival VLA constraints.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we have compiled over 30 years of radio observations of NGC 5253 for this purpose. NGC 5253 offers an ideal example for such studies because (i) it has hosted two Type Ia SNe in the past 150 years (SN 1972E and SN 1895B), (ii) it is located at very close proximity (d = 3.15 Mpc; Freedman2001 ), and (iii) it has been observed with the historic VLA and upgraded Karl G. \ Jansky VLA multiple times between 1981 and 2016. Such a data set over so many years allows us to probe the density of the CSM out to large radii from the SNe, constrain the presence of CSM shells, and provide insight into various progenitor scenarios for Type Ia SNe.

Two independent Type Ia SNe, SN 1895B and SN 1972E, occurred within a century of each other in the nearby blue compact dwarf galaxy, NGC 5253. NGC 5253 is located within the M83/Centaurus A Group, and throughout this work we adopt the Cepheid distance of 3.15 Mpc from Freedman2001 This distance includes a metallicity correction factor. . NGC 5253 is currently undergoing a starburst phase with a compact, young star forming region at its center Monreal2010 , thought to be triggered by an earlier interaction with M83 vandenbergh1980 .

sources.summary: The review presents NGC 5253 as a nearby blue compact dwarf for studying SSC formation, dense massive-star feedback, CO clumps, ionized gas, and a future galactic wind.

quotes.quote: NGC 5253 : At a distance of about 4 Mpc and with a metallicity of the order of 12 + log(O/H) = 8.25 , the BCD NGC 5253 is one of the best examples to study the birth and evolution of SSCs as well as the interaction between densely packed massive stars and the surrounding gas. Not unexpected in view of the SMC-like metallicity of NGC 5253 (see Section S3.4 ) the ALMA data from , obtained with an angular resolution of 0 2 ( 3 pc), reveal 118 individual CO clumps. The most prominent object associated with a nascent SSC is a cloud termed `D1', which contains within a diameter of only about 6 pc around 1500--2000 O stars .

Therefore it represents a promising candidate for the existence of a top heavy initial mass function (IMF). The correlation between CO line width and cloud size provides enhanced line widths by 0.2--0.3 dex with respect to Galactic disk clouds in the most active regions. The ionized gas comprises (as in Mrk 71, see Section S3.5 two components that are offset in velocity and in this case also in position . These JVLA observations at an angular resolution of 0 15 also show an arm of ionized gas reaching out of the center in northwestern direction, possibly indicating feedback.

sources.summary: The paper portrays NGC5253 as a dwarf starburst with extended 100-200 Myr diffuse star formation, young detected clusters, and rapid central cluster dissolution linked to high velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: The two dwarf galaxies, NGC3077 and NGC5253, show time--extended star formation, spanning the last 100--300 Myr, and a very `chaotic' spatial distribution of the cluster ages. This similarity between the two galaxies is counterbalanced by striking differences. In NGC3077, there are star clusters as old as 300 Myr, while in NGC5253 the oldest clusters unambiguously detected in the HST images do not seem to be significantly older than 20 Myr ( ).

Indeed, the time--extended star formation history for NGC5253 is inferred not from its clusters, but from its diffuse stellar population, whose colors are consistent with constant star formation over the past 100-200 Myr ( ). The dearth of `old' star clusters in NGC5253 could be caused by rapid cluster dissolution timescales in the center of the galaxy; by rescaling the Milky Way model of , and derive dissolution timescales in the range 16--50 Myr for NGC5253. These short timescales are driven by the high central velocity dispersion measured in the galaxy ( ).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5253 as a nearby starburst irregular, focusing on its embedded super-star cluster, mid-infrared spectrum, excitation, and contribution to the galaxy emission.

quotes.quote: Among them is the well studied bright starburst galaxy NGC 5253, a nearby Magellanic type irregular, observed at basically all wavelengths from X-ray to radio. Although this galaxy has a lower metallicity and higher excitation than the typical starbursts observed with ISO cf. thornley00,verma03 , the observations and analysis present here can provide interesting insight on the difficulties related to low spatial resolution spectroscopic observations and on the problems discussed above. The galaxy NGC 5253 is also of prime interest for studies related to super star clusters (hereafter SSCs), as it is one of the first starbursts where a very compact and hidden SSC was found from high spatial resolution radio observations turner98 .

Interestingly, the compact radio SSC in NGC 5253, often also referred to as the radio ``supernebula'', also stands out by its large contribution to the total integrated flux of the galaxy at various wavelength. For instance, estimate that at least 15 of the bolometric luminosity of NGC 5253 is due to this SSC. find that it dominates the total galaxy emission at wavelength longer than 2--3 .

sources.summary: The paper detects diffuse X-ray emission in NGC 5253, says its optical and X-ray emission have similar sizes, and suggests the hot gas may expand relatively freely.

quotes.quote: We present a comprehensive comparison of the X-ray properties of a sample of eight dwarf starburst galaxies observed with Chandra (I Zw 18, VII Zw 403, NGC 1569, NGC 3077, NGC 4214, NGC 4449, NGC 5253, He 2--10). In Paper I we presented in detail the data reduction and analysis of the individual galaxies. For the unresolved X-ray sources we find the following: point sources are in general located close to bright \ regions, rims of superbubbles, or young stellar clusters.

The \ and X-ray emission of NGC 5253 have about the same size, and both are embedded within a larger optical counterpart. Judged from these 2-dimensional images, the hot gas in NGC 3077, NGC 4214, and NGC 4449 is still contained by the cooler, neutral medium. It might be expanding more freely in NGC 1569, NGC 5253, and He 2-10.

sources.summary: The paper studies the central giant H II region of NGC 5253, describing its BCD nature, starburst trigger, nitrogen enrichment, and complex ionized-gas kinematics.

quotes.quote: NGC 5253 is a blue compact dwarf (BCD) galaxy located in the Centaurus A/M 83 group which is suffering a burst of star formation probably triggered by a previous encounter with M 83 . Because of its proximity ( D=3.8 Mpc, ), this galaxy constitutes an optimal target for the study of the starburst phenomenon. Recently, we performed a detailed study of NGC 5253, mapping its central part with 2D optical spectroscopy . Among other results, we delimited very precisely the area enriched with extra nitrogen and explored the possibility of Wolf-Rayet stars as the cause of this enrichment. We also showed that the kinematics of this object is very complex.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5253 as an intermediate starburst dwarf with a northern H I tail, revised mapping from robust-weighted data, and membership in the CenA/M83 group.

quotes.quote: There is not a clear-cut separation between these two types of morphologies, since there are several ``intermediate'' cases that have relatively regular distributions in the inner parts and small tails/filaments in the outer regions (NGC 4163, NGC 625, NGC 6789, and NGC 5253).

NGC 5253 has a 4 kpc tail to the North at V_ los 400 km s ^ 1 . Our total map at 40'' 40'' resolution is slightly different from that of LopezSanchez2012 at 57.8 37.5'' resolution because we used a Gaussian-smoothed, robust-weighted datacube instead of a natural-weighted datacube. The former cube has a much more regular noise structure than the latter one, providing a better estimate of the 3 column density sensitivity. NGC 5253 is in the CenA/M83 group; its projected distance from the spiral galaxy M83 is 150 kpc Karachentsev2005 .

sources.summary: The paper retains NGC 5253 as a very red WISE-color nearby AGN candidate, identifies it as one of the nearest known BCDs, and gives its distance and dwarf-galaxy stellar mass.

quotes.quote: The other two are HIPASS J1247-77 ( W1-W2 2.086 ) and NGC 5253 ( W1-W2 1.757 ), one of the nearest known BCD. For these, we have no optical diagnostic information and we therefore have no reason to remove them from our sample. In total, we therefore are able to identify 313 nearby galaxies with the infrared signature of an active massive black hole.

Our method detected AGN in five galaxies that are closer than NGC 4395: HIPASS J1247-77 (3.2 Mpc), UGC 04459 (3.2 Mpc), NGC 5253 (3.6 Mpc), IC 2574 (3.8 Mpc) and CIRCINUS (4.2 Mpc). One of these, NGC 5253, is a dwarf galaxy with stellar mass of 7.3 10^ 5 M _ (see Table tbl:tbl-1 ), while three others have BH mass estimates indicative of a dwarf galaxy (see Section sec:bhmass ).

sources.summary: The paper presents a Spitzer IRAC mid-infrared point-source catalog for NGC 5253, reporting 722 detected sources and common completeness limits corresponding to sources brighter than M_ 3.6 = -10.

quotes.quote: We present Spitzer IRAC mid-infrared point source catalogs for mosaics covering the fields of the nearby ( 4 Mpc) galaxies NGC 55, NGC 253, NGC 2366, NGC 4214, and NGC 5253. We detect a total of 20159 sources in these five fields. The individual galaxy point source breakdown is the following: NGC 55, 8746 sources; NGC 253, 9001 sources; NGC 2366, 505 sources; NGC 4214, 1185 sources; NGC 5253, 722 sources. The completeness limits of the full catalog vary with bandpass and were found to be m_ 3.6 =18.0 , m_ 4.5 =17.5 , m_ 5.8 =17.0 , and m_ 8.0 =16.5 mag. For all galaxies, this corresponds to detection of point sources brighter than M_ 3.6 =-10 .

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 5253 as a blue compact dwarf starburst with twin nuclear clusters, very young ages, very massive stars, and enough ionizing flux to require VMS.

quotes.quote: The blue compact dwarf galaxy NGC 5253 hosts a very young starburst containing twin nuclear star clusters, separated by a projected distance of 5 pc. One cluster ( 5) coincides with the peak of the H emission and the other ( 11) with a massive ultracompact H \ region. A recent analysis of these clusters shows that they have a photometric age of 1 1 Myr, in apparent contradiction with the age of 3--5 Myr inferred from the presence of Wolf-Rayet features in the cluster 5 spectrum. We examine Hubble Space Telescope ultraviolet and Very Large Telescope optical spectroscopy of 5 and show that the stellar features arise from very massive stars (VMS), with masses greater than 100 , at an age of 1--2 Myr. We further show that the very high ionizing flux from the nuclear clusters can only be explained if VMS are present.

sources.summary: The review highlights NGC 5253 as a dwarf with a massive embedded star-forming region, ALMA-resolved molecular clouds, molecular-gas accretion, and high-pressure starburst cloud conditions.

quotes.quote: NGC 5253 contains a large \ region and massive star cluster with thousands of O-type stars that are detected in 7 mm continuum and near-IR Turner:04 . observed the nearest starburst dwarf NGC 5253 ( Z 0.3Z_ , 3.15 Mpc) in CO(2-1) at 3 pc resolution with ALMA. NGC 5253 is also accreting molecular gas miura15 . They found 118 molecular clouds with an average radius of 4.3 pc and obtained an X_ CO factor about twice the Milky Way value using the virial theorem. Cloud velocity dispersions are a factor of 3 larger for their size than in the Milky Way in the starburst region, and surface densities are 3 higher as well, reflecting 10 higher pressures from cloud gravity.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5253 as an HII galaxy whose infrared-line data fall in the low-velocity, low-density regime where photoionization dominates over shocks.

quotes.quote: The points corresponding to the two galaxies NGC 5253 and II Zw 40, studied by Schaerer Stasinska (1999), are located at the top/right zone. Assuming that all the objects are photoionized by a similar radiation spectrum, our single-cloud results show that the distribution on the diagram can be reproduced if the shock velocity and the pre-shock density increase from the top to the bottom part of the diagonal. When the intensity of ionizing radiation is kept constant, the higher the shock velocity the stronger is the shock effect on the physical conditions in the cloud, relative to photoionization. Since NGC 5253 and II Zw 40 data points are located in the low velocity --- low density zone, photoionization is expected to be the dominant mechanism for these two objects, while shocks are required for explaining the data for the other objects, in agreement with Lutz et al. (1998).
In HII galaxies, like NGC 5253 and II Zw 40, the star forming region is young enough to contain a high fraction of massive O and WR stars which produce a large amount of ionizing photons.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5253 as a nearby young dwarf starburst, analyzing its HST/VLT cluster population and emphasizing inhomogeneous central dust reddening tied to dust-embedded cluster formation.

quotes.quote: Galaxies: individual ( NGC 5253 ) -- galaxies: star clusters -- galaxies: starburst NGC 5253, an irregular dwarf galaxy in the Centaurus Group, is one of the best objects available to study young starbursts. It is in fact one of the closest starburst galaxies, with heliocentric distance of 3.3 Mpc (Gibson et al. ), so that 1 corresponds to 16 parsec. Moreover it is one of the youngest starburst galaxies known (Rieke et al. ), with an age likely 10 Myr, as implied by the detection of spectral features arising from Wolf-Rayet stars (Schaerer et al. ) and from its almost entirely thermal radio spectrum with very little synchrotron emission from supernova remnants (Beck et al. ).

( , hereafter C97) have made it possible to resolve with unprecedented detail the central part of NGC 5253 and to carry out a detailed study of the brightest optical clusters and of the dust content of the galaxy. They found that the dust reddening is markedly inhomogeneous across the galaxy's central 20 region, alternating regions of small and large reddening, so that, even in this relatively low-metallicity galaxy, the early stage of star formation is associated with the formation of dust-embedded clusters.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5253 as a young H II/starburst/AGN composite whose line diagnostics require multiple cloud components, with low-velocity clouds dominating the modeled infrared contribution.

quotes.quote: In a previous analysis by Viegas, Contini, Contini (1999), the modelling of II Zw 40 and NGC 5253 led to gas with low \ and 0, in agreement with their young age, on the basis of high [ OIV ] /( [ NeII ] +0.44 [ NeIII ] ) versus [ NeIII ] / [ NeII ] line ratios. However, in the analysis of [ SIV ] / [ SIII ] , we find that the same two galaxies are explained by HII region clouds with relatively high \ (500 ), by clouds in starbursts with an age of 2.5 Myr and velocities of 200-300 , and by clouds in AGNs with velocities of 300 .

More particularly, the galaxies of the Verma et al. (2003) sample show that II Zw 40, NGC 5253, NGC 4038/4039, NGC 3690A and NGC 3690 B/C present a multiple nature of HII regions, AGN, and starburst galaxies.
Tables 3 shows that relatively low 0 \ clouds ( \ < 200 ) dominate in M82 and NGC 5253.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 5253 as a dwarf starburst with an optically thin ionization cone detected to 800 pc and a narrow opening angle.

quotes.quote: NGC 5253 is a nearby dwarf galaxy currently undergoing an extreme burst of star formation. Using / \ ratio maps, b:Zastrow_apj11 discovered an optically thin ionization cone in this amorphous elliptical galaxy Figure 2 of b:Zastrow_apj11 . The ionization cone is narrow with an opening angle 40 and is detected out to 800 pc from the starburst. The measured opening angle implies a solid angle for the cone that spans 3 of 4 steradians on the sky, assuming axisymmetry about the cone axis b:Zastrow_apj11 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5253 as a nearby blue compact dwarf with a young central starburst, dense super star clusters, a young-skewed cluster age distribution, and complete LEGUS coverage.

quotes.quote: NGC 5253 NGC 5253 is a nearby blue compact dwarf galaxy that hosts a very young central starburst, likely triggered by infalling material along the minor axis of the galaxy Meier_2002,Miura_2015,Turner_2015,Miura_2018 . This results in a dense, clumpy, central region, hosting a rich population of dense super star clusters (SSCs) with very high star formation efficiencies Turner_2004,Calzetti_2015_5253,Turner_2017,Smith_2020 . This galaxy has the lowest number of catalogued clusters in this study, with an age distribution that skews young. The radial extent of the star cluster population is entirely covered in the LEGUS field-of-view, observed with a single pointing.

sources.summary: The paper presents infrared and millimeter observations of starburst galaxy NGC 5253, emphasizing its young stellar clusters and decoupled infrared-submillimeter versus ultraviolet-optical emission regions.

quotes.quote: We present new observations of the famous starburst galaxy NGC 5253 which owes its celebrity to possibly being the youngest and closest starburst galaxy known. Our observations in the infrared and millimeter contribute to shed light on the properties of this interesting object. We have used our new data along with data from the literature to study the properties of the young stellar clusters present in NGC 5253.
We find that NGC 5253 is a striking example of a galaxy where the infrared-submillimeter and ultraviolet-optical emissions originate in totally decoupled regions of vastly different physical sizes.

sources.summary: The paper reports seven point sources embedded in extended NGC 5253 emission, a westward low-surface-brightness diffuse component, and central concentration of all sources except P1.

quotes.quote: Chandra observations revisited The Chandra data of NGC 5253 show a total of seven point sources embedded in an extended region, 1 8 1 3 in size (corresponding to 1.7 1.2 kpc). The area of high surface brightness, however, is less extended in the east-west direction ( 1 0 or 1.0 kpc) as compared to the faint emission. The distribution of the diffuse X-ray emission is similar to the \ morphology, which also exhibits a low surface brightness extension to the west (see Fig. fig:olay5253 b ). Except for the point source P1 (Fig. fig:point5253 a ), which is located toward the south-west, all X-ray point sources lie close to the centre of NGC 5253.

sources.summary: The paper finds disturbed H I kinematics in NGC 5253, with a minor-axis velocity gradient, no regular rotation, and a possible origin in dwarf-companion disruption or interaction.

quotes.quote: The analysis of the \ kinematics in NGC 5253 reveals a velocity gradient along the optical minor axis of the galaxy; it does not show any sign of regular rotation . Some authors suggested that this feature is an outflow. However, we think that its origin may be the disruption/accretion of a dwarf gas-rich companion or the interaction with another galaxy in the M 83 subgroup.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5253 as a dwarf starburst outlier above the infrared-HCN relation, attributing its deviation to extreme starburst conditions and possible heating or ISM effects.

quotes.quote: Note that, NGC 5253 and NGC 4826 lie above the relation of L_ IR and L_ HCN(3-2) . For dwarf starburst galaxy NGC 5253, with an extreme youth of starburst vandenBergh1980,Beck1996,Calzetti1997,Pellerin2007 , a high specific star formation rate Calzetti2015 and a high star formation efficiencies were found in the central starburst of NGC 5253 Miura2018 . The extreme environments and some mechanism, like mechanical heating, non-star-forming ISM component might result in NGC 5253 to deviate from the relation.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5253 as a high-excitation galaxy without clear large-scale outflows, using it as the low density-velocity model where stellar radiation dominates after shocks fade.

quotes.quote: On the contrary, the high-excitation \ galaxies (II Zw 40 and NGC 5253) do not show any clear signature of large scale outflows of gas.

a) figure=h1255_f3a.eps,cm b) figure=h1255_f3b.eps,cm To better understand the role of the input parameters in determining the physical conditions of the emitting gas, the distribution of some important physical quantities across the cloud is given in Figures fig2 a and fig2 b respectively for two significant models listed in Table table1 : NGC 5253, which represents the low density-velocity cloud component, and the high density-velocity component of NGC 253.

For NGC 5253 the shock effect becomes negligible at a distance of about 10^ 15 cm from the shock front, and most of the physical conditions in the cloud are then dominated by the effect of stellar radiation which leads to [ ] / [ ] > 1.

gmw_00000634.overview: 015 is a hyper-luminous, high-redshift quasar located at z ~ 4.4 [arXiv:1804.06399]. It is surrounded by an exceptional overdensity of companion galaxies [arXiv:1804.06399], including a very close companion only 2.2 kpc away that is currently merging with the host [arXiv:1804.06399].

sources.summary: ALMA observations resolve the z=4.4 WISSH QSO 015 host, finding dust and [CII] emission, nearby companions, star formation, and comparable host and SMBH growth timescales.

quotes.quote: We present an ALMA high-resolution ( 0.18'' 0.21'' ) observation of the 840 \ continuum and [ CII ] 157.74 \ line emission in the WISE-SDSS selected hyper-luminous (WISSH) quasi-stellar object (QSO) 015, at z 4.4 . Our analysis reveals an exceptional overdensity of [ CII ] emitting companions with a very small ( <150 ) velocity shift with respect to the QSO redshift. We report the discovery of the closest companion observed so far in submillimetre observations of high-z QSOs. It is only 2.2 kpc distant and merging with 015, while two other [ CII ] emitters are found at 8 and 17 kpc.

In this work we present the ALMA Cycle 4 observation (project 2016.1.00718.S) of the WISSH QSO SDSS 015 (celestial coordinates RA 10:15:49.00, Dec +00:20:20.03). The observation was carried out on 5 March 2016 for a 0.6 hours on-source integration time in C36-5 configuration with a maximum projected baseline of 1396 m. We used the ALMA band 7 receiver and the frequency division mode of the ALMA correlator.

sources.summary: SDSS J101549.00+002020.0 has several close continuum and [CII] sources within 4 arcseconds; velocity offsets imply companions, but low SNR prevented several ADMIT extractions.

quotes.quote: SDSS J101549.00+002020.0 was found to have multiple close sources that were detected in band 7 continuum and [ CII ] line emission less than 4 away bischetti18 . Several of these with detected line emission had small velocity shifts with respect to the line emission of the main quasar, and hence were considered close companions. In the cutout shown in Fig. fig:companions , only one such companion is visible.

This companion was reported by to have a separation from the main quasar of 3.5 and to have a continuum flux of 1252±76 Jy. The other detected companions had continuum fluxes < 400 Jy. The low SNR of these detections is the cause of their non-extraction by ADMIT.

gmw_00000635.name: PG 1351+640
gmw_00000635.overview: PG 1351+640 is a radio-quiet, highly variable quasar [arXiv:2305.03470, arXiv:1810.10245] that exhibits silicate emission features at both 10 and 18 microns [arXiv:astro-ph/0504423]. On parsec scales, it displays a core-jet morphology consisting of a flat-spectrum core and a steep-spectrum jet [arXiv:2305.03470, arXiv:2308.16780] moving at mildly relativistic speeds [arXiv:2307.13599, arXiv:2305.03470].

sources.summary: PG1351+640 is highlighted as a two-component source whose bright extended component resembles a radio jet; multi-epoch separations imply mildly relativistic or possible superluminal proper motion.

quotes.quote: PG1351+640 is an intriguing object exhibiting two components. The core component C1 has a flat slope with _ total = +1.0 , a high T_ B ( > 10^ 8.0 K), and is consistent with the Gaia position ( 1.9 mas), which is in agreement with a compact optically thick synchrotron source. The extended component C2 is optically thin with a steep slope _ total = -0.9 , and is farther away from the Gaia position ( 7.1 mas). Its T_ B is still high ( > 10^ 8.0 K), which is 1--2 orders of magnitude higher than the extended emission in the other objects, which makes this extended component resemble the lobe of a radio jet. Furthermore, the object has a large fraction of extended emission as indicated by the VLBA core/total flux ratio and the VLBA/VLA flux ratio both < 0.5 (Figure fc ).

This implies that the jet moved 3 mas, corresponding to 5.7 pc, in 15 years with an apparent velocity of 1.2c ( is the speed of light). A recent study by Wang2023b who observed this object with the VLBA at 5 GHz in 2015, also finds that this object has a core + jet morphology which is similar to our result. A comparison on the separation of the two components in 2015 and 2022 shows that the jet only moved 0.5 mas, corresponding to 1.0 pc, in 7 years, which suggests that the jet moved at a speed of only 0.5c Wang2023b .

sources.summary: The paper describes PG 1351+640 as a radio-variable quasar with bright and diffuse VLA components, a compact VLBA source, and core-jet morphology resembling a compact symmetric object.

quotes.quote: PG 1351+640 shows a bright component and a faint component located 1.5 \ to the west in the VLA D-array image at 5 GHz 1994AJ....108.1163K . This western component was clearly detected in the 1.4 GHz NVSS image, but not detected in the FIRST and VLASS images, indicating that it has a steep spectrum and diffuse structure. The bright VLA component shows an inverted spectrum (or peaked spectrum) and it is highly variable with a variation factor of 4 within 3 yr at 5 and 15 GHz 1989ApJS...70..257B . A single compact component was detected by VLBA on 2000 February 6 with a flux density of 6.8 mJy 2005ApJ...621..123U .

The morphology of PG 1351+640 in our 5-GHz VLBA image is very similar to a CSO showing double compact components separated by about 10 pc. However, the optical nucleus is close to the southeast component, making it more likely the core. PG 1351+640 has a 'core-jet' like morphology extending along the north-south direction in a higher resolution VLBA image at 8.4 GHz on 1996 June 9 1998MNRAS.299..165B , very similar to our result.

sources.summary: The paper presents PG 1351+640 as a radio-quiet quasar with a compact core-jet structure, variable VLBI components, measured jet proper motion, and a mildly relativistic small-angle jet.

quotes.quote: PG 1351+640 is one of the few RQQs suitable for proper motion studies: it has two compact components on milli-arcsecond scales, a flat-spectrum core and a steep-spectrum jet; both components are 2 mJy at 5 GHz and are well suited for Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) observations. We compare recent VLBA observations with that made seventeen years ago and find no significant change in the core-jet separation between 2005 and 2015 (a proper motion of 0.003 mas yr ^ 1 ). However, the core-jet separation increased significantly between 2015 and 2022, inferring a jet proper motion velocity of 0.063 mas yr ^ 1 , which corresponds to an apparent transverse velocity of 0.37 . The result suggests that the jet of the RQQ PG 1351+640 is mildly relativistic and oriented at a relatively small viewing angle.

PG 1351+640 is highly variable in the radio band, with the total flux density at 5 and 15 GHz varying by more than a factor of 4 from 1977 to 1986 1989ApJS...70..257B . The VLBI components are also highly variable, with the 5-GHz flux density of SE increasing from 0.62 0.03 mJy in August 2005 to 1.98 0.20 mJy in August 2015, and further to 2.43 0.12 mJy in January 2022 (Fig. fig:1351var ). The rapid and large variability is incompatible with the accretion disk wind scenario. In addition, the two VLBI components have different spectral indices: NW has a steep spectrum between 1.6 and 7.6 GHz _ 1.6 ^ 7.6 = -1.45 0.12 on epoch 23 January 2022 and is associated with optically-thin emission, while SE shows a flat spectrum _ 1.6 ^ 7.6 = -0.04 0.12 and is associated with optically thick emission 2023PGpaper2 . The resolved radio structure obtained from the VLBI data and the different spectral indices of the VLBI components support the notion that the parsec-scale radio emission of PG 1351+640 comes from a core-jet structure, that is, SE is the core (jet base) and NW is a jet knot.

sources.summary: PG 1351+640 contains a two-component parsec-scale source interpreted as a core-jet system, with a flat southeastern core, steep northwestern jet, and high brightness temperature.

quotes.quote: PG 1351+640 is not resolved in either the 5 GHz VLA D-array image 1994AJ....108.1163K or the 1.4 GHz VLA B-array image (the VLA FIRST survey, 1995ApJ...450..559B ). The most attractive aspect of this source is that in our 5 GHz VLBA image in Paper 1 2023MNRAS.518...39W , a structure analogous to Compact Symmetric Objects (CSOs, 1982A&A...106...21P,1994ApJ...432L..87W ) is presented with two compact components separated by about 8 parsec. This dual-component structure is confirmed in our new VLBA images: in Fig. fig:1351 , the 4.7, 6.2 and 7.6 GHz images all show two compact components; in the 1.6 GHz image, although the source is not resolved due to the relatively low resolution, the image shows an elongation in the southeast-northwest direction.

From the position of the optical nucleus and the flat or inverted radio spectrum, the southeast component appears to be the radio core and the northwest component is the jet. In all VLBI observations, the brightness temperature of the radio core exceeds 10^7 K and reaches up to 2.5 10^9 K, which is at the same level as the brightness temperature of the RLQs in our sample (Table tab:imagepar ), supporting that its radio emission is associated with the optically thick part of the jet base. No extended jet component is found to the southeast of the radio core (Fig. fig:1351 a). The one-sided jet morphology may be due to the Doppler boosting effect, although the Doppler factor obtained directly from the VLBI data is not very high.

sources.summary: The paper selects PG 1351+640 for prominent 10 and 18 micron silicate emission, defines its continuum from the IRS spectrum, and notes a good synthetic-profile match plus possible crystalline grains.

quotes.quote: Here, we report the first spectroscopic detection of both silicate features at high signal to noise in five PG quasars. Quasars are the high luminosity counterparts of Seyfert 1 galaxies, showing the same broad emission lines that define Type 1 AGNs. The five sources presented in this letter (PG 0804+761, PG 1211+143, PG 1351+640, I Zw 1 [ =PG 0050+124 ] and 3C 273 [ =PG 1226+023 ] ) were selected from a sample of 12 AGNs based upon their prominant silicate emission features at both 10 and 18 .

For PG 1351, I Zw 1 and PG 1211, we define the continuum using a spline interpolation between the 5--8 \ spectrum and a point at the extreme red end of the IRS spectral range, where a change in slope indicates the end of the 18 \ silicate feature.

The quasar profiles generally show good agreement at wavelengths beyond 10 . Below 10 , only PG 1351 and I Zw 1 match the synthetic profile well. In the other three spectra, the blue wing of the 10 \ appears weak, especially in 3C 273 and PG 1211. Apart from the artifacts in the 8--10 \ region of the spectrum of 3C 273 (indicated by the large error bars in Figure fig2 ; see 2) and the presence of atomic lines and PAH emission in some spectra, the 10 \ band has a smooth appearance, showing no sign of a departure from amorphous grain structure. Evidence for some crystalline grains do appear at longer wavelengths, most notably in the spectrum of PG 1351 at 23 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses PG 1351+640 as a radio-quiet PG quasar with steep compact radio emission, an anomalous low-Eddington slope, and intermediate RQQ/RLQ behavior.

quotes.quote: Two belongs to our sample, PG 1216+069 which has _ mas =-0.13 , and PG 1351+640 with _ mas =-0.96 , measured between 2.27 and 5 GHz. One of the two other sources is a bright Seyfert galaxy (Mrk 817), with _ mas =-0.60 between 1.7 and 5 GHz. This AGN is actually listed in the PG catalog (PG 1434+590, Green86 ) but is excluded from the final PG quasar sample based on the lack of a quasi-stellar morphology (being host dominated).

Two of the steep spectra objects, PG 1216+069 and PG 1351+640, were also observed by with the VLBA at 5 GHz, who find that the mas includes 70 and 97 of the VLA flux, in contrast with fractions of 14 and 43 found by at 8.4 GHz. These rather discrepant results reflect the fact that both objects are variable (both have f_c>1 ).

sources.summary: The paper lists PG 1351+640 among outliers to the proposed dust composition, with residuals near the 10 micron feature and requiring CDEOl1, DL01, and Min3 instead of MieOl3.

quotes.quote: We can see that five objects (Mrk 1210, NGC 7213, PG 0804+761, PG 1211+143, and PG 1351+640) are outliers in this distribution with ^2_ >2 . The middle panel of Figure fig:all_residuals shows the residuals of the spectral fits for our 49 objects using this combination, and it highlights these five objects. Comparing these residuals with the ones obtained in the best fits (top panel), we can see that the main difference is that the latter have larger ones ( >10 ) at 9 m < < 12 m for NGC 7213, PG 0804+761, PG 1211+143 and PG 1351+640.

For NGC 7213, PG 0804+761, PG 1211+143, and PG 1351+640, the residuals of the particular combination differ from the ones of the best fit mainly at 8 m < < 13 m , meaning that the former can not reproduce well the 10 \ m silicate feature in the spectra of these objects. To obtain an acceptable fit model, NGC 7213 needs MieOl2, CDEOl1, CDEOl3, and DL2 instead of MieOl01 and MieOl3; PG 0804+761 needs CDEOl1, DL01, and Min3 instead of MieOl01 and MieOl3; PG 1211+143 needs CDEOl1 instead of MieOl01; and PG 1351+640 needs CDEOl1, DL01, and Min3 instead of MieOl3.

sources.summary: The paper notes PG1351+640 is variable with a VLBA core, and detects three aligned arcsecond-scale components with diffuse emission where earlier work securely found only the center.

quotes.quote: PG1351+640 . This source is known to be strongly variable (Barvainis Antonucci ) and has a VLBA core (Blundell Beasley , Ulvestad et al. ). However, on arcsecond scales we detect three aligned components with surrounding diffuse emission (Fig. 10, right). Kellermann et al. ( ) have only securely detected the central component.

gmw_00000636.name: PG 1216+069
gmw_00000636.overview: PG 1216+069 is a radio-quiet quasar [arXiv:1810.10245] hosted by a large spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0002199]. It features a highly compact, unresolved radio core [arXiv:2210.16764, arXiv:2307.13599] that exhibits extreme flux variability [arXiv:2308.16780, arXiv:2307.13599] and a changing, peaked radio spectrum [arXiv:2308.16780].

sources.summary: PG 1216+069 remains compact in VLBI images but shows extreme variability, high brightness temperature, and a peaked spectrum consistent with jet or absorbed core emission.

quotes.quote: PG 1216+069 ( z=0.3313 ) shows compact structure in the VLA images 1993MNRAS.263..425M,1994AJ....108.1163K . It remains unresolved in the VLBA images, but shows significant variability (Fig. fig:1216 ): on 21 January 2000, S_ 5GHz = 6.5 0.3 mJy 2005ApJ...621..123U ; on 7 August 2015, S_ 4.9GHz = 1.20 0.12 mJy with a core brightness temperature >10^8 K 2023MNRAS.518...39W ; in our new 4.7 GHz VLBA observations, S_ 4.7GHz = 8.17 0.41 mJy. The VLBI core has a flux density increase by a factor of 6.8 in the 6.5 years from August 2015 to December 2021, which is the largest variability in our RQQ sample.

From our 2021 observations, PG 1216+069 shows a peaked spectrum (black data points in Fig. fig:1216 e ), possibly due to synchrotron self-absorption (SSA) or free-free absorption (FFA). We fitted the radio spectrum of the core with an SSA model 1971PhT....24i..57P,1999A&A...349...45T and the best-fitted parameters are: the maximum flux density at the turnover frequency F_ m =8.99 mJy, the turnover frequency _ m =6.95 GHz, the spectral indices of the optically thin region _ thin =-3.59 and the spectral indices of the optically thick region _ thick =1.41 . also reported an inverted shape spectrum on parsec-scale with a turnover frequency of about 3 GHz (red data points in Fig. fig:1216 e ), which is different from the shape of our VLBA result. Similarly, the radio spectrum of its total flux density measured by the VLA also showed an inverted shape between 4.89--14.9 GHz, with the turnover frequency around 8.4 GHz 1996AJ....111.1431B . PG 1216+069 has significant variability and a changing spectrum, which warrants further study of the relationship between the variability and the evolution of the radio spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper describes PG 1216+069 as a compact, variable radio quasar with an unresolved VLBA core, possible earlier southward jet extension, flat spectrum, and non-star-formation brightness temperature.

quotes.quote: PG 1216+069 shows a compact structure in the VLA images 1993MNRAS.263..425M,1994AJ....108.1163K . It showed a flat or inverted spectral shape with a spectral index of 0.08 from the VLA D-array observations at 4.89 14.9 GHz 1996AJ....111.1431B . PG 1216+069 (J1219+0638) shows an unresolved core with a peak flux density of 1.04 mJy in our 5-GHz VLBA image observed on 2015 August 7.

A similar structure but with a much higher flux density of 6.4 0.3 mJy was revealed in the VLBA image at 5 GHz observed on 2000 January 21 2005ApJ...621..123U , confirming the variability of this source previously found from VLA observations 1996AJ....111.1431B . The 8.4-GHz VLBA image obtained by 1998MNRAS.299..165B showed a possible jet extension to the south which however is not seen in our 5-GHz image. The radio spectrum of the VLBI component is flat ( ^ 5GHz _ 1.4GHz = 0.07 0.16 or with an inverted spectral shape 2005ApJ...621..123U . The core brightness temperature measured in all these VLBA observations is larger than 10 ^ 8 K, ruling out the star formation origin.

sources.summary: PG1216+069 has a strongly inverted VLBA spectrum, the highest brightness temperature in the sample, and strong variability, suggesting a compact synchrotron self-absorbed source smaller than parsec scales.

quotes.quote: PG1216+069 has a strongly inverted slope with _ total = +2.2 and _ core = +2.3 , which indicates synchrotron self-absorption, and again a compact optically thick source smaller than 0.01 pc. The VLBA coordinate is consistent with the Gaia position ( 5.8 mas) and the T_ B is the highest one ( > 10^ 8.5 K) in our sample. The object is highly compact as indicated by the VLBA core/total flux ratio 1 , and is variable, since the VLBA/VLA flux ratio > 1 . Large variability is also seen by comparing the flux density at 5 GHz in our observation ( S_ total = 6.9 mJy) which is a factor of five higher than that in Wang2023a ( S_ total = 1.3 mJy). The two observations were made a few years apart, so the light crossing times imply a size smaller than pc scales, which is consistent with the small size derived from synchrotron self-absorption.

sources.summary: The paper notes PG 1216+069 as a radio-quiet PG quasar with high compactness, a measured mas-scale slope, and steep radio emission that may be compact.

quotes.quote: Note that despite the lack of an _R versus f_c correlation in the RQ PGs, the four objects with f_c>1.1 , PG 1011 040, PG 1216+069, PG 1351+640, and PG 2304+042, i.e. the objects which are inevitably variable, and therefore necessarily compact, do tend to be on the flat _R side. There is an equal number of RQ PGs with steep and flat core emission of the eight objects at f_c<0.5 . This may well be a true, rather than variability effect on f_c , as the core can still be extended only to a fraction of kpc, and be dominated by optically thin synchrotron source. The main difference between RLQ and RQQ is that in RLQ the core emission is generally flat excluding the small sub-class of compact steep spectrum objects, see . , while in the RQ PGs, the core can be either steep or flat.

sources.summary: The paper says its tip-tilt imaging and nearby PSF star aided analysis of 1216+069, and it finds a large spheroidal host with M_K=-25.1.

quotes.quote: Our analysis of this quasar benefited because the images were obtained using the tip-tilt system and there is a nearby bright star which was placed on the same frame as the quasar and used to obtain a psf measurement. Previously, `nebulosity' has been observed around this quasar , and a more detailed HST study found a best-fit spheroidal ( =4 ) galaxy with M_ V =-22.3 . We also find that the most likely host is a large spheroidal galaxy and obtain M_K=-25.1 , giving V-K=2.8 .

gmw_00000637.name: PG 1700+518
gmw_00000637.overview: PG 1700+518 is a radio-quiet, low-ionization broad-absorption-line quasar [arXiv:1306.3500] that exhibits a compact radio core [arXiv:1110.6603, arXiv:2210.16764] and a bent jet structure [arXiv:2308.16780]. The host galaxy is rich in molecular gas [arXiv:1110.6603] and is undergoing a merger with a nearby ring-shaped companion galaxy [arXiv:1110.6603, arXiv:astro-ph/0203362].

sources.summary: The paper describes PG 1700+518 as an optically bright, radio-quiet low-ionization BAL quasar with dust reddening and flat, absorbed, unexpectedly weak X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: The other three are also X-ray weak but the nature of their X-ray weakness is not as well understood; among these three, we chose the two lower redshift objects as our targets here (PG 1004+130 at z=0.241 and PG 1700+518 at z=0.292 ; the other object is PG 0043+039 at z=0.384 ). PG 1004+130 and PG 1700+518 are among the optically brightest BAL quasars known; see Figure fig-lz . PG 1004+130 and PG 1700+518 show clearly detected BALs e.g., Pettini1985,Wills1999,Brandt2000,Young2007 . Aside from dust reddening in PG 1700+518, their optical and UV spectra (continua and emission lines, excluding the BAL regions) appear normal compared to typical quasars. PG 1700+518 is also sub-classified as a low-ionization BAL (LoBAL) quasar.

PG 1700+518 is radio quiet ( R 10 ). PG 1700+518 has been observed by XMM-Newton with a 60 ks exposure. The spectrum is flat ( 0.2 ) compared to the typical photon index of 1.8 for radio-quiet quasars e.g., Reeves1997,Page2005,Just2007,Shemmer2008,Scott2011 , and the absorption column density was constrained to be 2 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 with an absorbed power-law model Ballo2011 .

sources.summary: PG1700+518 is treated as a BAL quasar with a compact, flat-spectrum VLBA core, high brightness temperature, partly resolved extended emission, and prior radio structures interpreted as an outflow base.

quotes.quote: PG1700+518 is a BAL quasar. It has a flat slope with both _ total and _ core = 0.3 . The VLBA coordinate is consistent with the Gaia position ( 1.4 mas), and the T_ B is high ( > 10^ 7.8 K). The object is very compact as indicated by the VLBA core/total flux ratio 0.8 . The VLBA/VLA flux ratio 0.5 suggests that part of the extended emission is resolved out on the VLBA scales but unresolved on the VLA scales.

An EVN observation at 1.6 GHz of one of the two components reveals a core plus two-sided extended structures Yang2012 . These structures may result from an outflow, as the object is a BAL quasar, which has a high velocity outflow Young2007,Runnoe2018 . However, the extended emission detected in Yang2012 is resolved out on the VLBA scales, and we thus only see a compact core, which is probably the base of the outflow.

sources.summary: The paper maps PG 1700 + 518 as a BAL quasar host with AGN-dominated radio emission, a compact radio core, two-sided jets, a molecular-gas-rich host, and a nearby companion indicating merging.

quotes.quote: The quasar PG 1700 + 518 was first discovered by sch83 in an optical survey and later identified by wam85 as a rare, nearby BAL quasar. It is an ultra-bright ( m_ R 15.5 ) infrared source, while its host galaxy is one of the most molecular gas-rich Palomar-Green QSO hosts. The star-forming molecular gas mass was estimated to be 6 10^ 10 M_ from CO(1 0) emission observations eva09 . A ring-shaped companion galaxy was found in deep infrared observations sto98 at 1.6 arcsec North of the quasar with a similar redshift ( z 0.29 ), indicating a merging process between the two galaxies.

With the new EVN observations and public VLA archive data, we present the jet structure of PG 1700 + 518 on parsec to kilo-parsec scales. We find a compact component with a brightness temperature of 10 ^ 8 K and identify it as the radio core. The jet components on two sides of the core have similar angular separation from the core, similar size and total flux density, indicating a large viewing angle.

sources.summary: The paper describes PG 1700+518 as a radio-quiet BAL quasar with disk-wind outflow signatures and a compact VLBA component whose spectrum and brightness temperature match an AGN radio core.

quotes.quote: PG 1700+518 is a broad absorption line (BAL) quasar at a redshift of 0.292 1985ApJ...296..416W and the first radio-quiet BAL QSO to discover radio jets. The broad Balmer line system has not only radial but also rotational motion, suggesting that the outflow originates from the rotating accretion disk wind 2007Natur.450...74Y . Radio images can give independent constraints on the wind geometry.

Our 5-GHz VLBA, with a higher resolution than the 1.6-GHz EVN image, reveals a compact radio component, and a faint extension towards the southeast to be confirmed in future observations. The two hotspots observed in the EVN image are not detected in our VLBA image, due to their reduced flux density (assuming a steep spectrum) at 5 GHz and the lack of a sufficiently compact structure. Based on our VLBA data and EVN data from the literature, we estimate the spectral index of the VLBA component to be ^ 5GHz _ 1.6GHz = -0.36 and the brightness temperature to be 1.8 10^8 K. The flat spectrum and high brightness temperature of the compact VLBA component are consistent with it being the radio core of the AGN.

sources.summary: The paper uses PG 1700+518 to verify the Nobeyama setup, detecting CO(1-0) at 89.370 GHz and measuring a lower CO luminosity than the IRAM 30m value.

quotes.quote: We observed PG 1700+518 to confirm that our observation setups were correct. In PG 1700+518, CO (1--0) was detected by IRAM 30m telescope at 89.370 GHz (z=0.290) with the CO (1--0) luminosity of L'_ CO =(1.4 0.2) 10^ 10 K km s ^ 1 pc ^ 2 Evans_2009 . Figure fig:spec_pg is the spectrum obtained by us during this project, showing that CO (1--0) emission is detected at the same frequency of 89.370 GHz. The spectrum shows that peak antenna temperature is 2 mK, the rms noise level is T_ rms =0.6 mK, and velocity width is FWHM=270 km s ^ 1 (This FWHM is consistent with the measurements by IRAM 30m). The integrated intensity is I_ CO =0.47 0.07 K km s ^ 1 in the unit of antennae temperature.

Assuming _ mb =0.58 , the corresponding CO (1--0) luminosity is L'_ CO =(0.74 0.11) 10^ 10 K km s ^ 1 pc ^ 2 . This Nobeyama 45-m measurement is 50 of the value measured by IRAM 30m, which possibly suggests that the main beam efficiency is less than _ mb =0.58 .

sources.summary: The paper describes 1700+518 as a low-redshift BAL quasar with a disturbed host, a likely ring-galaxy companion, and estimated K-band magnitudes for both.

quotes.quote: Quasar 1700+518 is a bright BAL quasar of low redshift ( z=0.29 ). Such low redshift BAL objects are rare and hard to discover since the broad absorption lines are in the UV and consequently quasar 1700+518 has received much interest: specific studies of this quasar have been undertaken in many different wavebands . Because of the low redshift and the brightness of the quasar, 1700+518 has also been included in many samples of quasars imaged to obtain details of their host galaxies , although these have only provided upper limits for the host magnitude.
