More recent imaging studies have shown that the morphology of the underlying structure consists of a disturbed host predominantly to the SW of the core and a close interacting companion to the NE which is most likely a ring galaxy . Deconvolution of the light from this quasar, as shown in Fig. fig:disturbed , confirms this picture of the structure. With the disturbed morphology it is difficult to know how to split the light in the central pixels into nuclear and host components. As for quasar 1636+384 the host luminosity was estimated by summing the counts in the pixels surrounding the central one (ignoring those from the NE companion).

sources.summary: The paper highlights PG 1700+514 as an infrared-luminous radio-quiet BAL quasar whose host and companion show disturbed ring-like structure, a bright host extension, and a southeastern knot in adaptive-optics imaging.

quotes.quote: PG 1700+514 is one of the most infrared-luminous, radio-quiet BAL quasar . Ground-based imaging revealed an extension about 2 arcsec north-east of the quasar which was shown by adaptive-optics imaging and follow-up spectroscopy to be a companion with a redshift 140 km s ^ 1 blueward of the quasar . NICMOS observations lead Hines et al. (1999) to argue that the companion is a collisionally induced ring galaxy. The fit to the SED and the Keck spectrum of the companion imply that the light is emitted by an old population of stars plus a 85 Myr old star-burst .

The companion is seen as a highly disturbed system with a bright nucleus and a ring-like structure; the nucleus beeing decentered with respect to the ring. The host-galaxy is clearly seen around the quasar with a bright extension to the south-west, first noted by stickel and clearly visible in the optical images by stockton98 . In addition, we detect a bright knot to the south-east which is not seen in the NICMOS data probably because of the presence of residuals in the PSF subtraction.

sources.summary: The paper newly detects a compact ENLR in PG 1700 + 518 and links its high-velocity EELR to either a radio hot spot, companion, or merger starburst region.

quotes.quote: Our improved deblending technique allows us also to detect a small ENLR close to the nucleus of PG 1700 + 518 and PG 2130 + 099. Similar to Mrk 1014, which we noted as an outlier in Hu08, these objects are known to host a triple radio source. Radio jets may therefore be an additional ingredient altering the general trends, which we will explore more closely in the next sections.
Still, it is worthwhile to note that we measured a redshifted radial velocity of 800 km s ^ 1 for the EELR structure of PG 1700 + 518 (cf. Table tab_pmas:line_ratios ).

The EELR of PG 1700 + 518 is almost coincident with the south-east radio hot spot, considering the low spatial resolution of the optical compared to the radio observation. It is tempting to speculate that the radio-jet is accelerating the gas to the observed velocities ( 800 km s ^ 1 ).

sources.summary: PG 1700+518 is a broad-absorption-line quasar whose compact VLBI component, spectral behavior, and bent extended jet connect radio emission with jet and wind geometry.

quotes.quote: PG 1700+518 ( z=0.292 ) is a well-known broad absorption line quasar with high-speed broad Balmer lines associated with outflows from rotating accretion disk winds 2007Natur.450...74Y . In AGN, winds and jets can co-exist e.g. III Zw 2, 2023ApJ...944..187W . The driving mechanism of the fast outflow, whether wind or jet or both, is still uncertain. Exploring the starting point of the wind requires sub-parsec resolution.

The outer part of the 1.6-GHz extended jet extends eastward, which means that there is a jet bending from the east to the southeast (the SE component in 2012MNRAS.419L..74Y ) between 10 mas (43 parsec) and 270 mas (1.2 kpc). The bending of the jet could be caused by the jet colliding with clouds in the torus or BLR, or by the pressure gradient of the rotating accretion disk wind.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes 7.5 years of spectroscopic monitoring of PG 1700+518, deriving continuum and line light curves, emission-line time lags, and spectral-slope variability behavior.

quotes.quote: Using 7.5 yr spectroscopic monitoring data of one Palomer-Green (PG) quasi-stellar object (QSO), PG 1700+518, with strong optical emission, we obtain the light curves of the continuum , , the broad component of , and the narrow component of by the spectral decomposition. Through the interpolation cross-correlation method, we calculate the time lags for light curves of , the total , the broad component of , and the narrow component of with respect to the continuum light curve. We find that the time lag in PG1700+518 is 209^ +100 _ 147 days, and the time lag cannot be determined. Assuming that and emission regions follow the virial relation between the time lag and the FWHM for the and emission lines, we can derive that the time lag is 148^ +72 _ 104 days. The time lag calculated from the empirical luminosity--size relation is 222 days, which is consistent with our measured time lag.

sources.summary: The paper describes PG 1700+518 as a radio-loud-by-RX, absorbed BAL quasar in the RQ PG context, with VLBI observations and compact steep radio emission.

quotes.quote: Three of our RQ PGs, PG 1351+640, PG 1700+518, and PG 2112+059, are RL based on the R_X>-3.5 criterion. However, all three also show significant C IV absorption (PG 1700+518 is a BALQ - broad absorption line quasar), and have a steeper _ ox than found in unabsorbed quasars (see Fig.4 in LB02 ). It is therefore possible that both the X-rays and the UV are affected by absorption, which will bias their R_X values to be higher than intrinsic.
We conclude that the steep component may be as compact as the pc scale (PG 1216+069, PG 1351+640, PG 1700+517), but potentially a significant fraction originates on larger scales.

sources.summary: The quasar had no INOV or longer-timescale fluctuations in one monitoring set, but Jang later reported a confirmed intranight optical variability detection.

quotes.quote: RIQ J1701+5149: This RIQ was monitored on four nights by Carini et al. (2007), but no INOV was seen (Table 2). Also, significant fluctuations were found to be absent both on inter-night or longer timescales. This RIQ was also monitored by Jang (2005) who detected a confirmed INOV ( C_ eff = 3.1 , Table 2).

sources.summary: The review describes PG 1700+512 as an extreme Pop. A BAL QSO with high accretion and very large terminal velocity inferred from its absorption trough.

quotes.quote: BAL QSOs like PG 1700+512 are extreme Pop. A sources radiating at high . PG 1700+512 shows a very large v_ t \ inferred from the maximum \ in the \ absorption trough. This extreme phenomenology is probably restricted to highly accreting sources.

gmw_00000638.name: MCG+12-02-001
gmw_00000638.overview: MCG+12-02-001 is an interacting luminous infrared galaxy system [arXiv:1810.02371, arXiv:2012.10054] undergoing a starburst with a star formation rate of 30 to 55 solar masses per year [arXiv:2012.10054]. The system contains a hard X-ray active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2107.10855] and is considered a triple or quadruple galaxy system [arXiv:1810.02371, arXiv:2107.10855].

sources.summary: MCG+12 02 001 is treated as a triple system; the western source has a central X-ray peak and luminosities comparable to the eastern galaxy.

quotes.quote: [ 83 ] MCG+12 02 001 : We consider this system to be composed of three individual sources: a northern component and a main pair, separated by 0.3 '. The western source in the pair is considered an individual galaxy in close interaction with the eastern source, though there is also the possibility of it being an extended star-forming region. Given the fact that we see an X-ray peak at its centre, and its X-ray luminosities of L_ SX =1.3 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 and L_ HX =2.4 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 are comparable to those of the eastern galaxy, we are inclined to treat this whole system as a triple. The northern source does not contribute to the IRAS flux DiaCha2010 , as specified in Table table:IRfrac , and therefore it is not analyzed. It is detected with Chandra , with 9 cts in the full 0.5-7 keV range.

sources.summary: The paper treats MCG+12-02-001 as the eastern interacting LIRG pair, identifying it as the hard X-ray AGN and suggesting a Compton-thick low-luminosity nucleus.

quotes.quote: IRAS F00506+7248 (MCG+12-02-001): This system is a stage-C merger composed of four galaxies; the northern source (MCG+12-02-001a), the western source (MCG+12-02-001 NED01), and the eastern interacting pair (MCG+12-02-001) with a separation of 0 9 (0.3 kpc; Ricci2017bMNRAS ). The eastern pair is assigned as a LIRG, which is the brightest source of this system in the IRAC 1--4 (3.6, 4.5, 5.8, and 8.0 m) images. We identify this pair (MCG+12-02-001) as the hard X-ray detected AGN. Ricci2017bMNRAS report that the detection significance with NuSTAR in the 3--10 keV and 10--24 keV bands are < 3 in this system. In our analysis, it is 2.98 and 3.37 in the 8--24 keV and 10--40 keV bands, respectively.

sources.summary: MCG+12-02-001 is discussed as an interacting LIRG with high stellar mass, substantial star formation, a 40-200 Myr starburst age, GOALS membership, and sparse optical data.

quotes.quote: MCG+12-02-001 is a LIRG and an interacting system. The stellar mass is about 8 10^ 10 M_ and SFR is 30 to 55 \ M_ /yr 2014A&A...568A..62D,2010ApJ...715..572H . The starburst age is found to be 40–200 Myr from SED fitting 2015A&A...577A..78P . It is in the GOALS sample, but this source has very little optical observational data available.

gmw_00000639.name: IC 1623
gmw_00000639.overview: IC 1623 is a closely interacting, gas-rich spiral galaxy pair in the mid-stages of a major merger [arXiv:1204.5520, arXiv:2210.04647]. The system is a luminous infrared merger featuring a highly dust-obscured eastern nucleus with starburst and potential active galactic nucleus activity, alongside a UV-bright western component [arXiv:0709.3304, arXiv:2208.14507].

sources.summary: VV 114 is a LIRG merger with extended CO/CN structure, the most extreme line-ratio variations, and high ratios in its eastern nucleus and overlap region.

quotes.quote: This paper presents the results of an archival project using ALMA Cycle 0 data to survey the CO/CN ratio in 17 nearby galaxies. We identified a sample of 8 galaxies with distances < 200 Mpc for which both the CN N=1-0 and CO J=1-0 lines were detected. These galaxies range from nearby starbursts (M83, NGC 253) to ultra/luminous infrared galaxies (AM 1300-233, AM 2246-490, AM 2055-425, NGC 3256, VV 114) to an S0 galaxy with an unusual far-infrared excess (NGC 1377). We measure S/N-matched CO/CN line ratios as well as ratios calculated from the total flux detected in each line with ALMA.

VV 114 VV114 shows the most extreme variations in the CO/CN line ratio and also has a complicated velocity structure. For the global value for the S/N-matched CO/CN line ratio, the processing parametres are as described in Table table-sample . However, because of the large variations, it was impossible to match the CO moment 0 multiplier closely to the global ratio value. Separate ratio maps were made for the eastern nucleus and overlap (western) region to try to capture this spatial variation.

sources.summary: JWST/MIRI images describe VV 114 as a redshift 0.020 LIRG merger with an obscured VV 114E nucleus, NE/SW cores, star-forming knots, and diffuse PAH emission.

quotes.quote: James Webb Space Telescope ( JWST ) Mid-InfraRed Instrument (MIRI) images of the luminous infrared (IR) galaxy VV 114 are presented. This redshift 0.020 merger has a western component (VV 114W) rich in optical star clusters and an eastern component (VV 114E) hosting a luminous mid-IR nucleus hidden at UV and optical wavelengths by dust lanes. With MIRI, the VV 114E nucleus resolves primarily into bright NE and SW cores separated by 630 pc.

The NE core, previously speculated to have an Active Galactic Nucleus (AGN), has starburst-like mid-IR colors. In contrast, the VV 114E SW has AGN-like colors. Approximately 40 star-forming knots with L_ IR 0.02-5 10^ 10 L _ are identified, 25 of which have no optical counterpart.

Finally, diffuse emission accounts for 40--60 of the mid-IR emission. Mostly notably, filamentary Poly-cyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbon (PAH) emission stochastically excited by UV and optical photons accounts for half of the 7.7 m light of VV 114.

sources.summary: The paper presents JWST/MIRI spectroscopy of the interacting LIRG VV 114, finding a highly obscured eastern nucleus, CON-like nuclear continuum, star-forming PAH ratios, and merger-stage disturbed morphology.

quotes.quote: We present a spectroscopic analysis of the heavily obscured nucleus and the surrounding environment of the eastern region of the nearby ( z = 0.02007 ) interacting galaxy VV 114 with the JWST Mid-InfraRed Instrument (MIRI). We model the spectrum from 4.9 - 28 m to extract Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbon (PAH) emission and the underlying obscured continuum. We find that the NE nucleus (A) is highly obscured where the low PAH equivalent width (EW) ratio, EW(12.7)/EW(11.3), reveals a dust enshrouded continuum source. This is confirmed by decomposing the continuum into nuclear and star-forming where the nuclear component is found to be typical of CONs. The 11.3/6.2 PAH flux ratio is consistent with originating in star-forming regions rather than typical AGN.

In this work we apply the technique to JWST data for the first time with MIRI observations of the highly obscured interacting system VV 114 (IC 1623, Arp 236). This is a local z = 0.02007 , D= 84 Mpc, Strauss1992 galaxy with a highly disturbed morphology. This object is a LIRG with L_ IR = 10^ 11.71 L _ Armus2009 and is in the mid-stages of a major merger Stierwalt2013 .

sources.summary: The paper models VV 114 as an interacting luminous infrared pair whose continuum fits require high-velocity dusty clouds and lower-velocity shock components in its emission regions.

quotes.quote: VV114 VV114 is a pair of interacting galaxy, particularly interesting for its high infrared luminosity (10 ^ 11.3 L_ ) and high FIR-to-visible flux ratio. VV 114 is composed of two stellar components separated by 15 , designated by VV 114 E and VV 114 W (Knop et al 1994).

Although model M2 explains satisfactorily S01 data both in the bremsstrahlung and in the MIR ranges, the contribution of high velocity ( \ = 1000 ) high density ( 0 \ = 1000 3) dusty clouds with d/g =0.02 (short-dashed lines) is invoked to explain MIR data at higher frequencies. The maximum temperature of the grains corresponding to the high velocity shock is 330 K. This model is radiation-bound and the temperature of the gas in the downstream region behind the shock never drops below 1.2 10^7 K. Therefore, these clouds do not contribute to the optical lines.
The IRAS FIR data indicate that lower shock velocities are also present in the emission regions. IRAS data at about 100 \ can be explained by models with \ = 100 \ and 0 \ = 100 3 \ (Fig. 3 bottom).

sources.summary: The paper describes VV 114 as a local galaxy merger whose western component dominates UV and X-ray emission, while VV 114W drives hot-gas outflows typical of powerful starburst winds.

quotes.quote: The spectra of VV 114 included all events within a radius of 30 of the peak in the soft X-ray emission seen in the X-ray images, which closely coincides with the NED position for VV 114. AGN and Starburst Activity in E While the western component of dominates the UV and X-ray emission, E is the more powerful near-IR emitter (right panel in Figure chandrafalse ). NIR spectral observations by doy95 show evidence of a gas photo-ionized by 4 10^5 OB stars in E. The extended emission observed in the radio con91 and NIR doy95 bolster the argument that a compact starburst dominates E. floc02 however observed a continuum bump at 5-6 which is typical of AGN spectra. They suggest that a heavily absorbed AGN could contribute up to 40 of the mid-IR flux in E.

The total rate at which supernovae and stellar winds provide mechanical energy in VV 114W is 6 10^ 42 erg/sec, and the estimated energy outflow rate is therefore only 5 of the total. The implied outflow rates in the hot gas are then M 50 f^ 1/2 M _ /year and E 5 10^ 42 f^ 1/2 erg/sec. These outflow rates significantly exceed the rates for the cooler gas derived from the FUSE data above, but are are typical of winds in powerful local starburst galaxies e.g. heck03 .

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 1623 as a dusty close LIRG merger with two galaxies, widespread shock excitation, high line ratios near dust lanes, and no evidence for an embedded AGN.

quotes.quote: IC 1623: IC 1623 (also commonly referred to as VV 114 and with the IRAS designation IRASF 01053-1746) consists of two galaxies: IC 1623 A in the west and the more heavily obscured IC 1623 B in the east. The dust lane seen in figure fig1 covers the eastern nucleus, which is actually brighter in the near-IR than the western disk knop94,Doyon95,Scoville00 . The galaxy nuclei are separated by approximately 5 kpc, firmly classifying the pair as a close merger in the Veilleux02 classification scheme. Interestingly, the heavily obscured eastern galaxy has two very bright sources of similar magnitude in the near-IR, raising the possibility that IC 1623 B itself is a merger.

Neither of the galaxies exhibits evidence of an AGN, obscured or otherwise Doyon95,Veilleux95 . Extended tidal features can be seen in optical and near-IR imaging, though the low surface brightness of these features makes observations of them prohibitively time-consuming. The galaxy pair is at a redshift of z 0.0201 , corresponding to a 1 projected distance of 385 pc.

sources.summary: The DESHIMA first-light paper observes interacting galaxy pair VV 114 and detects its redshifted CO(3-2) line at 339 GHz, estimating the line intensity and total CO flux density.

quotes.quote: VV 114 The interacting galaxy pair VV 114 has been observed on 16 and 21 November 2017. The typical PWV measured by ALMA were 0.7 mm on the 16th and 0.9 mm on the 21st. The scan pattern and data reduction method were the same as for IRC+10216; the continuum emission was removed by subtracting a median baseline of the spectrum, estimated in the frequency range of < 335 GHz and > 345 GHz. The broadband spectrum of VV 114 is shown in Fig. fig:concept b.

A significant emission line is detected at 339 GHz, corresponding to the redshifted CO(3--2) spectrum . The peak intensity of the CO line is 8.9 mK in T_ a ^ and the integrated intensity is estimated by integrating over 337.9--341.5 GHz to be 12.4 K km s ^ 1 . Adopting the beam size of DESHIMA on ASTE measured on Mars and the main beam efficiency of _ MB =0.34 , the total flux density of the CO(3--2) emission is estimated to be 2.44 10^3 Jy km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: VV 114 is a mid-stage major merger with a dusty eastern component and complex nuclei; the paper finds highly obscured NE dust and a less-obscured hot SW component.

quotes.quote: VV114 is a mid-stage major merger e.g. Stierwalt2013 where the eastern component appears extremely dusty outputting a large infrared luminosity Armus2009 . The region is complex, with multiple nuclei and a large scale shock resulting from the interaction Saito2017, Donnan2023b, Rich2011, Rich2023, Gonzalez-Alfonso2023, Buiten2023 .

VV 114 VV 114 NE shows a significant highly obscured dust continuum appearing more obscured than the PAHs, molecular gas or HII regions. VV 114 SW also shows a strong continuum but it is significantly less obscured than the NE nucleus. There is a significant isolated hot dust component that dominates the continuum between 2-10 m. This is consistent with the claim that this is an AGN by Rich2023 , due to its low 3.3 m and 6.2 m PAH equivalent widths, where the hot dust continuum dominates over the emission features.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Arp 236 as an early-stage gas-rich merger whose eastern knot has MIR signatures near the AGN locus, implying about 40 percent obscured AGN contribution.

quotes.quote: Such a candidate is Arp 236 (IC 1623, VV 114), an infrared luminous system (L _ IR = 10 ^ 11.62 L _ ), classified as an early-stage gas-rich merger (M(H _2 ) = 5.1 10 ^ 10 M _ , ), and composed of several nuclei and/or compact starburst regions. Previous near-infrared and radio studies showed no evidence for an AGN ( , ). However, the MIR signatures of the eastern region (knot A, ) place it near the AGN locus in our CVF diagram (Fig. fig6 ) and, therefore, may contain a non-negligible fraction of AGN contribution in its MIR spectrum. Nevertheless, one should note that the possible AGN contribution in Arp 236 is not revealed with the LW diagnostic (Fig. fig8 ) which is less accurate compared to the CVF diagnostic.

Knot A is the brightest source in the near-infrared. It is also redder and more compact than all other sources in the system (not resolved at 3.7 m with a FWHM of 0.3 arcsec, ). We estimate that 40 of the MIR emission in the region can originate from an optically obscured AGN.

sources.summary: IRAS F01053-1746 is treated as a complex, closely interacting spiral pair whose merger stage prevents assigning gas metallicities to each galaxy and whose data show widespread shocks.

quotes.quote: Although IRAS F01053-1746 and IRAS F10257-4339 are both composed of two individual galaxies, they have progressed along the merger sequence to the point where we are unable to effectively assign gas metallicities to either of the galaxies. We thus treat both IRAS F01053-1746 and IRAS F10257-4339 as individual systems with the brightest optical nucleus defined as the zero point for a metallicity gradient. IRAS F01053-1746 (IC 1623) This system contains two very closely interacting spiral galaxies. It is kinematically very complex and exhibits evidence of widespread radiative shocks, which are analyzed in detail in Rich11 .

The deprojected radii are plotted using the hyperleda values for the western, less obscured galaxy IC 1623 A. The eastern system is very intensely star-forming as seen in the infrared (e.g. Howell10 ), but is so enshrouded in dust that our optical spectra do not trace star-forming gas.

sources.summary: The paper treats VV 114 as a close, separated major merger with tidal structures; in the IceCube search it is the most correlated of seven interesting mergers but not significant.

quotes.quote: Additionally, we also look into a few interesting galaxy merger candidates: UGC 12914/5, UGC 813/6, VV 114 (discussed in Ref. ), NGC 660, and NGC 3256 (mentioned in Ref. ). Furthermore, we explored ESO 303-IG 011, a pair of star-forming galaxies captured by the Hubble Space Telescope and SDSS J084905.51+111447.2, a system of three colliding galaxies revealed by Chandra data. These candidates have some interesting common characteristics: (i) all the candidates are close mergers (UGC 12914/5, UGC 813/6, ESO 303-11, VV 114) or appear to have merged already so that their nuclei lie within a massive bulge in the center of the galaxy (NGC 660, NGC 3256). (ii) The few galaxies that are separated (UGC 12914/5, UGC 813/6, ES0 303-11, VV 114) appear to be major mergers, i.e., their galaxy or bulge masses are similar. (iii) All the seven galaxies have extended tidal tails, bridges or loops of hot, magnetized gas that can be clearly distinguished in the blue (or g) band images.

The source VV 114 was found to be the most significantly correlated among these sources with a pre-trial p-value and significance given by 2.12 10^ 2 , 2.03 , respectively. However, it's important to note that while we didn't find a significant correlation, the best-fit n_s values allow us to set upper limits on the neutrino flux originating from these sources.

sources.summary: The paper treats VV 114 as a double-nucleus LIRG/merger where the infrared-bright eastern nucleus and its southwestern peak show evidence for a buried AGN.

quotes.quote: VV 114 (z = 0.020) is a double-nuclei LIRG (L _ IR 10 ^ 11.6 L _ ) with a separation of 15 '' kno94 . Both nuclei are classified optically as HII regions vei95 . In the short optical wavelength, the western nucleus (VV 114W) is brighter than the eastern nucleus (VV 114E), but VV 114E becomes more important with increasing wavelength and is prominent in the infrared band kno94,lef02 , probably dominating the infrared luminosity of the VV 114 merging system. The VV 114E nucleus has a secondary peak 1 5 southwest of the VV 114E peak (VV 114E _ SW ; Soifer et al. 2001). The presence of a luminous buried AGN is suggested in VV 114E from the strong featureless mid-infrared 15 m continuum emission lef02 .

sources.summary: The paper treats VV 114 as a local LBG-like comparison merger, describing its UV-bright western component, IR-bright eastern component, and multiple nuclei plus diffuse emission when simulated at z=0.15.

quotes.quote: The two local comparison objects are Haro 11 and VV 114, both of which lie at z=0.02 for details see and references therein knop94,scoville00,goldader02,bergvall02,kunth03,grimes06,grimes07 . VV 114 is a merging system consisting of a UV-luminous Western component (VV 114W), and an IR-luminous Eastern component (VV 114E) with very little associated UV emission. For VV 114(W), we use a Space Telescope Imaging Spectrograph (STIS) image in the NUV ( _c 2365 ) from Program 8201 (PI: Gerhardt Meurer). Both Haro 11 and VV 114 possess multiple bright nuclei ( 1 \ apart) when observed in \ at z=0.15 . VV 114 also shows a significant amount of diffuse emission in between the knots.

sources.summary: The paper treats VV 114 as a nearby Lyman-break analog with extended X-ray emission, little nuclear contribution to hard X-rays, and excess luminous ULX candidates.

quotes.quote: Benefitting from the high angular resolution capability of , three of the six LBAs in our sample, VV 114, Haro 11, and J082355.0 + 280621.8 appear to have extended X-ray emission (see Figure fig:LBA3 ), and we can distinguish multiple X-ray point sources, all of which are luminous enough to qualify as ULXs ( >10^ 40 ). The nuclear regions, potentially associated with AGN, contribute only < 15 to the 2--10 keV emission in each case, immediately indicating that the elevated /SFR for those LBAs cannot be attributed to AGN activity. While VV114 may harbor an obscured AGN (in the Eastern component, VV114E), Grimes07 conclude that star formation dominates the 2--10 keV power in this galaxy.

In Figure fig:LBA3 , the images of extended X-ray emission in three LBAs (VV 114, Haro 11 and J082355.0 + 280621.8) display multiple off-nuclear point sources with 10^ 40 , presumed to be ULXs, associated with each galaxy. Based on their SFRs and the analysis of HMXBs in star-forming galaxies by , we would have expected fewer ULXs with >10^ 40 in these sources than what we observe (five, three and three off-nuclear sources with >10^ 40 in VV 114, Haro 11and J082355.0 + 280621.8, respectively).

sources.summary: The paper treats IC 1623A/B as a large radio source with a real offset between radio-frequency peaks, shocked/outflow evidence, and a radio-AGN classification based on the shift and steep spectral index.

quotes.quote: IC 1623A/B does not give a measurement for the flux density at 8.44 GHz of this source, but we provide a flux density above 3 in Table data measured from the map. The source is large (24 arcsec long) at 1.49 GHz, but due to the small angular size of the 8.44 GHz map, only a part of the object is observed, hence the map is small. The radio spectral index has a wide range of values. The maximum flux pixel in the 1.49-GHz map is not at the same R.A. and Dec as the maximum flux pixel in the 8.44-GHz map. We classify this object as a radio-AGN because of the shift, and because the radio spectral index in the map exceeds out theoretical value of 1.1.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 1623 as a stage-C LIRG merger with two nuclei, contrasting the IR-bright eastern source with the X-ray-bright western source.

quotes.quote: IRAS F01053--1746 W/E (IC 1623A and IC 1623B): The LIRG IC 1623 (= VV 114) is a stage-C merger consisting of two nuclei with a separation of 15 7 (6.4 kpc). The eastern object (IC 1623B) is much brighter than the western source (IC 1623A) in the Spitzer/IRAC bands. Whereas, the Chandra results by Grimes2006 show that IC 1623A is much brighter than IC 1623B in X-rays. IC 1623A is also very luminous in the UV and optical, with many star clusters detected in HST images Cortijo-Ferrero2017a and in soft X-ray images (Chandra and XMM-Newton). Grimes2006 imply the possible presence of a low-luminosity AGN in IC 1623B ( N_ H 1.5 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 ), although its contribution to the total soft X-ray flux of the system is small.

sources.summary: The paper uses JWST NIRCam to reveal young massive star clusters in VV 114, including hidden dusty clusters and a steep cluster age gradient over 0.5 Gyr.

quotes.quote: We present the results of a James Webb Space Telescope NIRCam investigation into the young massive star cluster (YMC) population in the luminous infrared galaxy VV 114. Based on yggdrasil stellar population models, we identify 16 YMCs in our JWST catalog with F150W-F200W and F200W-F356W colors suggesting they are all very young ( 1-4 Myr), dusty ( A_ V 5 ), and massive ( 10^ 5.8 < M_ < 10^ 6.1 ). The discovery of these `hidden' sources, many of which are found in the `overlap' region between the two nuclei, triples the number of t < 4 Myr clusters detected in VV 114. Extending the cluster age distribution ( dN/d ^ ) to the youngest ages, we find a slope of = -1.27 0.32 for 10^ 6 < ( yr ) < 10^ 7 , which is consistent with the previously determined value from 10^ 7 < ( yr ) < 10^ 8.5 , and confirms that VV 114 has a steep age gradient for all massive star clusters across the entire range of cluster ages observed. Finally, the consistency between our JWST and HST derived age distribution slopes indicates that the balance between cluster formation and destruction has not been significantly altered in VV 114 over the last 0.5 Gyr.

sources.summary: The survey describes VV 114 as a two-component HII LIRG with no compact VLA source, possible eastern AGN signatures, and extended infrared emission.

quotes.quote: VV 114 (Arp 236, IC 1623) v95 classify the optical spectrum as HII for both components. 8.4 GHz images from the VLA show no sign of a compact source c91 . However, l02 find that the mid-infrared spectrum shows signs of an AGN in the eastern component. Near and mid-infrared images show extended emission in both components with a bright compact source in the eastern component sco00,s01 .

sources.summary: The paper describes a close interacting spiral pair with complex kinematics, widespread radiative shocks, and heavily dust-obscured infrared star formation in the eastern system.

quotes.quote: IRAS F01053-1746 (IC 1623) This system contains two very closely interacting spiral galaxies. It is kinematically very complex and exhibits evidence of widespread radiative shocks Rich11 . The eastern system is very intensely star-forming as seen in the infrared (e.g. Howell10 ), but is so enshrouded in dust that our optical spectra do not trace any of the buried star-formation.

sources.summary: The paper derives the 0.3--30 keV SED of low-metallicity starburst galaxy VV 114, finding XRB and ULX dominance and enhanced X-ray luminosity per star-formation rate.

quotes.quote: Using a combination of , , and \ observations, we present new constraints on the 0.3--30 keV SED of the low-metallicity starburst galaxy VV 114, which is known to host several ultra-luminous X-ray sources (ULXs) with luminosities above 10 ^ 40 erg s ^ 1 . We use an archival \ observation of VV 114 to constrain the contributions to the X-ray SED from the major X-ray emitting components of the galaxy, and newly acquired, nearly simultaneous \ and \ observations to extend the spectral model derived from \ to cover the 0.3--30 keV range. Using our best-fit galaxy-wide spectral model, we derive the 0.3--30 keV SED of VV 114, which we find is dominated by emission from the XRB population, and in particular ULXs, at energies > 1.5 keV, and which we find to have an elevated galaxy-integrated X-ray luminosity per unit star formation rate relative to higher-metallicity star-forming galaxies. We discuss our results in terms of the effect of metallicity on XRB populations and the hot ISM, and the importance of X-ray emission from star-forming galaxies in the high redshift Universe.

sources.summary: The paper treats VV 114 as a nearby gas-rich merging galaxy-pair candidate whose interaction may have triggered starburst and AGN activity relevant to cosmic-ray scenarios.

quotes.quote: Another interesting candidate is VV 114 VV_2001 , which is a gas-rich merging galaxy pair with a core separation of 6 \ kpc , located at d_L 77 \ Mpc from the Earth Soifer_et_al_1987 . The galaxy interaction may have already triggered the starburst and active galactic nucleus (AGN) activity in this system Iono_et_al_2004 .
A fraction of starburst and AGN activities are triggered by galaxy mergers, as observed in VV 114.

gmw_00000640.name: NGC 833
gmw_00000640.overview: NGC 833 is an Sa-type galaxy [arXiv:1910.03420] that hosts an obscured active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1910.03420, arXiv:2107.10855] classified as a LINER [arXiv:1910.03420]. It is closely interacting with its neighbor NGC 835 [arXiv:2301.07138], displaying a highly disturbed velocity field [arXiv:1512.02633] and off-center residual gas [arXiv:1910.03420].

sources.summary: The paper identifies HCG 16b as an Sa, LINER/AGN galaxy that is the group most H I deficient, with off-center residual gas and a disrupted velocity gradient.

quotes.quote: HCG 16b HCG 16b is an Sa galaxy and the most \ deficient galaxy in the group, probably having lost about 4.6 10^ 9 \ of . It is correct that this galaxy has lost less \ than HCG 16a, but is more \ deficient. This is because \ deficiency is defined as the logarithmic decrement, not the additive decrement. The galaxy has a central source identified as an AGN with X-rays and line ratios Turner+2001,Martinez+2010,Oda+2018 , it has also been classified as a LINER Veron-Cetty+Veron2010 . This is the only galaxy in the group that was classified as quiescent in terms of its WISE IR colours Zucker+2016 .

Figure fig:HCG16b_moms shows that the little remaining \ in HCG 16b is very off centre compared to its optical disc. The velocity field shows a small, but clear velocity gradient aligned with the major axis of the optical disc, suggesting that at least some of the remaining \ is likely still rotating with the optical disc. However, this gradient is disrupted on the eastern side of the galaxy where there is a gas extension to the North-East that connects to emission around the NW tail and that around HCG 16a.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 833 in its close interaction with NGC 835, carefully avoiding contamination, and finds all models fit it without requiring line-of-sight N_H variability.

quotes.quote: NGC 833 Data reduction/fitting: NGC 833 is part of a closely interacting system with NGC 835 (separation 1 ). The second Chandra observation (Obs. ID: 10394) considered for this merging system does not include NGC 833, but rather only NGC 835. We opted to add this observation to the table (with blank data) to avoid confusion with the epochs shown for NGC 835.

Analysis of results: This source is well-fit by all models. The torus parameters are highly unconstrained, likely due to a very subdominant reflection component see e.g. Torres-Alba2021 . The ^2_ red comparison shows, for all models, that N_ H,los variability is unnecessary to explain the data. Likewise, the p-value of all N_ H,los being the same is large enough that one cannot rule out the hypothesis. Thus, we classify this source as `Non-variable in N_ H,los '.

sources.summary: The paper identifies HCG 16B as a strong AGN with compact-group luminosity excess, and notes its interaction with HCG 16A, diffuse bridge, disturbed velocity field, warping, and misalignment.

quotes.quote: The two known strong \ AGNs HCG 16B osullivan2014a,osullivan2014b and HCG 92C (NGC 7319). The former is a single nuclear \ point source. As shown in T14 the CG environment is very rarely host to unambiguously strong nuclear AGN emission; for this reason we have not attempted to exclude nuclear point-source emission in this work.

These are the only late types in our sample that are strong AGNs, clearly the primary cause of the observed excess. HCG 16B is also strongly interacting with its neighbor 16A (classification LINER/weak AGN in T14), to which it is linked by a bridge of diffuse ( \ and optical) emission jeltema2008 . The Fabry-Perot analysis of M98 detects a highly disturbed velocity field in HCG 16B, kinematic warping and G/SMM.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 833 in HCG 16 as one of two hard X-ray AGNs detected in the system, with obscuration measured from NuSTAR and other X-ray data.

quotes.quote: IRAS F02071--1023 W2/W/E/S (NGC 833, NGC 835, NGC 838 and NGC 839): The system IRAS F02071--1023 (= HCG 16 or Arp 318) is a stage-A merger with seven member galaxies, including the central four spiral galaxies Hickson1982 . With the Chandra observations, OSullivan2014 detect obscured AGNs in the pair of NGC 833 and NGC 835 separated by 56 4 (15.3 kpc), whereas they conclude that NGC 838 and NGC 839, separated by 148 8 (39.1 kpc), are starburst-dominant sources. Bitsakis2014 estimate their total IR luminosities by the UV-to-submillimeter SED decomposition, and find that only NGC 838 is a LIRG. We identify both NGC 833 and NGC 835 as the hard X-ray detected AGNs. Oda2018 carry out a detailed X-ray study of this system using NuSTAR, Chandra, and XMM-Newton, detecting the obscured AGNs with N_ H 3 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 in both NGC 833 and NGC 835.

gmw_00000641.name: NGC 835
gmw_00000641.overview: NGC 835 is an Sab-type [arXiv:1910.03420] Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:1910.03420, arXiv:1501.02022] that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2301.07138, arXiv:1511.01926] and a star-forming ring [arXiv:1910.03420, arXiv:1501.02022]. It is tidally interacting with NGC 833 [arXiv:1501.02022] and exhibits line-of-sight X-ray obscuration variability [arXiv:2301.07138, arXiv:1511.01926].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 835 as a merging-system LIRG with extended soft emission, obtains reasonable torus-model agreement, and finds all models require line-of-sight N_H variability.

quotes.quote: NGC 835 Data reduction/fitting: The extraction region was limited to 40 to avoid contamination from NGC 833. For the same reason, the background was extracted from a circular region (instead of the usual annulus) of radius 60 . Nearby source NGC 838, a starburst galaxy at 3.5 from NGC 835, shows no emission, and therefore is not contaminating the spectrum. The data was taken using a larger-than-usual 8 circular region to include all the soft emission (this source is a known Luminous Infrared Galaxy, or LIRG), for easier comparison to the data.

Analysis of results: The data is well-fitted by all models, which are in reasonable agreement. However, the best-fit values for cos ( _ Obs ) derived with borus02 and UXCLUMPY are incompatible. The former favors an edge-on configuration, while the latter favors an almost face-on one. Our results are compatible with those of Zhao2020 , whose analysis also favors an edge-on scenario. All models agree that this source shows significant N_ H,los variability. We classify this source as `variable in N_ H,los '.

sources.summary: The paper includes ARP 318 among six O-type ring galaxies, finding standard simulations fail to match its extended central region and testing a larger-disc model for improvement.

quotes.quote: For the sixth one, ARP 318, the major difference was that the central part was very big and extended to a much larger radius. The simulation profile dropped and could not fit the observed flat feature. In next section, a simulation is introduced, in which the disc's length scale is doubled. The purpose was to see if the larger disc could produce a bigger central part with a flat density profile, as well as to study the effect of different initial conditions for the major galaxy.

In order to obtain a better model for the galaxy ARP 318, a primary galaxy with a larger length scale is used in the simulation presented here. The disc galaxy was set up using the same method as described in Section 3, except that the radial scale length h was 7 kpc, which was two times the radial scale length of the main disc galaxy in S1-S4. After an initial relaxation of 15 dynamical time, this disc galaxy approached a new equilibrium and was used as the target galaxy in the merging simulation.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 835 as a nearby obscured LLAGN, finding extended mid-infrared emission dominated by circumnuclear star formation and X-ray variability most likely caused by absorber changes.

quotes.quote: We aim to study the nuclear obscuration of LLAGN NGC 835 and its extended emission using mid-infrared observations. We present sub-arcsecond-resolution mid-infrared 11.5 m imaging of the LLAGN galaxy NGC 835 obtained with the instrument CanariCam in the Gran Telescopio CANARIAS (GTC), archival Spitzer /IRS spectroscopy, and archival Chandra data observed in 2000, 2008, and 2013. The GTC/CanariCam 11.5 m image reveals faint extended emission out to 6 arcsec. We obtained a nuclear flux of F(11.5 m) 18 mJy, whereas the extended emission accounts for 90 of the total flux within the 6 arcsec. This means that the low angular resolution ( 4 arcsec) IRS spectrum is dominated by this extended emission and not by the AGN.

The nucleus of NGC 835 is clearly dominated by the AGN emission at both X-rays and mid-infrared frequencies. Furthermore, the observed emission of this nucleus has undergone variations between the first two epochs of X-ray observations (in 2000 and 2008) and the latest three (in 2013). We found that these variations are most likely due to changes in the absorber according to the X-ray to mid-infrared luminosity relation expected for AGN.

sources.summary: The paper describes HCG 16a as an Sab, Seyfert 2, active star-forming galaxy with a visible star-forming ring, strong H I deficiency, and disturbed outer gas linked to interactions.

quotes.quote: HCG 16a HCG 16a is an Sab galaxy that has been classified as an active star forming galaxy in IR Zucker+2016 and hosts an AGN (Seyfert 2) that has been confirmed with both optical line ratios Veron-Cetty+Veron2010,Martinez+2010 and X-rays Turner+2001,Oda+2018 . The galaxy also hosts a ring of SF (star formation) that is clearly visible in the IR and UV Tzanavaris+2010,Bitsakis+2014 . In terms of its , HCG 16a is the second most deficient galaxy in the group, apparently missing approximately 5.5 10^ 9 \ of .

However, in velocity the gas that is co-spatial with HCG 16a forms a clear, continuous, and steep gradient, as would be expected from an inclined disc galaxy. This is most readily seen in the http://amiga.iaa.es/FCKeditor/UserFiles/X3D/HCG16/HCG16.html X3D interactive figure which accompanies this paper. The emission associated with HCG 16b forms a small, offset clump at approximately the same velocity as the lower velocity "horn" of the HCG 16a profile (Figure fig:spectra ). The two galaxies are connected in \ by a faint bridge of emission, visible in the channel maps (available in the electronic version) between velocities 3858 and 4006 . Given the size of the beam and the fact that the optical discs of HCG 16a and b overlap, it is not possible to reliably assign the emission to either galaxy. Therefore we simply split it approximately half-way between the two sources. The uncertainty in where this bridge should be split introduces a negligible error to the \ properties of HCG 16a as the entirety of the emission that we attribute to HCG 16b would only increase the \ mass of HCG 16a by 0.13 dex. When viewed projected in the plane of the sky there is an \ tail which connects HCG 16a to HCG 16c (the NW tail, see Figure fig:mom0 , left panel). There is also an accompanying stellar tail identified by Rubin+1991 that extends in the same direction away from HCG 16a (clearly visible in the Figures fig:optim fig:pv_plot ).

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 835 in HCG 16 as one of two hard X-ray AGNs detected in the system, with obscuration measured from NuSTAR and other X-ray data.

quotes.quote: IRAS F02071--1023 W2/W/E/S (NGC 833, NGC 835, NGC 838 and NGC 839): The system IRAS F02071--1023 (= HCG 16 or Arp 318) is a stage-A merger with seven member galaxies, including the central four spiral galaxies Hickson1982 . With the Chandra observations, OSullivan2014 detect obscured AGNs in the pair of NGC 833 and NGC 835 separated by 56 4 (15.3 kpc), whereas they conclude that NGC 838 and NGC 839, separated by 148 8 (39.1 kpc), are starburst-dominant sources. Bitsakis2014 estimate their total IR luminosities by the UV-to-submillimeter SED decomposition, and find that only NGC 838 is a LIRG. We identify both NGC 833 and NGC 835 as the hard X-ray detected AGNs. Oda2018 carry out a detailed X-ray study of this system using NuSTAR, Chandra, and XMM-Newton, detecting the obscured AGNs with N_ H 3 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 in both NGC 833 and NGC 835.

sources.summary: NGC 835 is described as a Seyfert 2 galaxy interacting with NGC 833, with a long tidal tail, AGN X-rays, and an ENLR inside a starburst ring.

quotes.quote: NGC 835: This Seyfert 2 galaxy is tidally interacting with NGC 833, and dispelays a long tidal tail. The nuclear spectrum is dominated by a strong post-starburst continuum with deep NaD absorption. In X-rays, Turner01 confirmed the presence of an AGN in both NGC 835 and NGC 833. The ENLR, 8 5 arcsec ( 1.9 1.3 kpc), is contained within an elliptical starburst ring 14 9 arcsec ( 3.4 2.3 kpc) in diameter.

gmw_00000642.name: UGC 2608
gmw_00000642.overview: UGC 2608 is a luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:2107.10855] and a Seyfert 2 galaxy located in the Perseus cluster [arXiv:2006.07631, arXiv:astro-ph/0012035]. It hosts a heavily obscured active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2107.10855] and forms a noninteracting pair with the nearby galaxy UGC 2612 [arXiv:2107.10855, arXiv:astro-ph/0012035].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 2608 as the northern LIRG in a noninteracting pair and cites broadband XCLUMPY modeling that identifies a heavily obscured AGN.

quotes.quote: IRAS F03117+4151 N/S (UGC 2608 and UGC 2612): Each galaxy in IRAS F03117+4151 is a stage-N source with no sign of interaction. The northern object (UGC 2608) is a LIRG, while the southern object (UGC 2612) is faint in the IR e.g., Inami2013 . Guainazzi2005 report the results of the XMM-Newton observation of UGC 2608. Adding the NuSTAR and Suzaku data to the XMM-Newton ones, Yamada2020 perform a broadband spectral analysis of UGC 2608 with XCLUMPY and identify a heavily obscured AGN ( N_ H = 5.4^ +7.0 _ 3.1 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 ) with an absorption-corrected luminosity of log L_ 2-10 = 43.59 ^ +0.19 _ 0.25 . For UGC 2612, no X-rays are detected with NuSTAR, Chandra, or XMM-Newton.

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 2608 as a Sy 2 cluster AGN with broad H emission, radio and hard X-ray counterparts, warm WISE colours, and possible q-based radio-AGN ambiguity.

quotes.quote: The 12th edition of the `Catalogue of Quasars and Active Nuclei' Veron-Cetty_2006 lists eight objects in our survey field, among them are three with z < 0.03 : the Sy 1.5 NGC 1275 ( 691), the Sy 2 UGC 2608 ( 278), and the BL Lac V Zw 331. The latter one is a background system ( z=0.0283, R = 65 5 ). In this Section, we discuss the search for AGN in additional cluster galaxies via the emission lines in optical spectra, non-thermal radio emission and IR colours indicating a dusty torus. (a) Optical AGN Broad hydrogen Balmer line components signify type 1 AGNs. We identified a broad H component not only in the two known cluster AGN NGC 1275 and UGC 2608 (Fig. fig:line_deblending ), but also in the edge-on disc galaxy UGC 2715 ( 1096), which was not previously listed as an AGN. Furthermore, a broad component was invoked to fit the H line profile in the spectrum of NGC 1294 where the S/N is low however. Broad H was also found in the two background systems UGC 2724 ( 1152) and J032006.3+402159 ( 735). All six broad-line galaxies are classified as AGN in the WHAN diagram (Fig. fig:WHAN_Xray_radio ): NGC 1275, UGC 2608, UGC 2715, and the two background sources as sAGN, whereas NGC 1294 is a wAGN.

The two galaxies with very warm, accretion-dominated colours W1 - W2 > 0.8 mag are NGC 1275 ( 691) and UGC 2608 ( 278), which display both broad H emission, radio emission with low q values and hard X-ray counterparts.

sources.summary: The paper presents Mrk 1073 as a Perseus-cluster galaxy paired with UGC 2612, with resolved soft X-rays best interpreted as thermal emission from an extended source.

quotes.quote: The optical and X-ray alignment of Mrk 1073 is uncertain.
The soft X-rays are resolved in Mrk 1073 and IC 3639, so the most likely spectral model for both is thermal emission.
Mrk 1073 Mrk 1073 is part of the Perseus cluster and paired with UGC 2612.

gmw_00000643.name: CGCG 468-002E
gmw_00000643.overview: CGCG 468-002E is a luminous infrared galaxy that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2107.10855]. It forms a stage-B merging pair with the nearby galaxy CGCG 468-002W, separated by a distance of 10.3 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2107.10855].

sources.summary: The paper identifies CGCG 468-002E as the LIRG and hard X-ray AGN in its stage-B merging pair, with NuSTAR/Swift results constraining absorption and luminosity.

quotes.quote: IRAS F05054+1718 W/E (CGCG 468-002W and CGCG 468-002E): These two stage-B merging galaxies have a separation of 29 5 (10.3 kpc). The eastern source (CGCG 468-002E) is a LIRG, while the western source (CGCG 468-002W) is not luminous in the IR band (log L_ IR / L_ = 10.74; Ricci2017bMNRAS ). We identify CGCG 468-002E as the hard X-ray detected AGN. The recent study with NuSTAR and Swift/XRT observations by Ricci2017bMNRAS reveals the presence of an AGN with N_ H 1.5 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 in CGCG 468-002E. This is consistent with our results using NuSTAR, Swift/BAT and XRT data ( N_ H = 1.5 0.1 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 and log L_ 2-10 = 42.84 ^ +0.04 _ 0.03 ).

gmw_00000644.name: ESO 060-IG016 East
gmw_00000644.overview: ESO 060-IG016 East is a galaxy that forms part of a stage-B luminous infrared galaxy merger system [arXiv:2107.10855]. It hosts an obscured, hard X-ray-detected active galactic nucleus, while its western companion galaxy is not detected in NuSTAR and Chandra observations [arXiv:2107.10855].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO 060-IG016 East as the hard X-ray AGN in a stage-B LIRG merger, while the western component lacks significant X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: IRAS F08520--6850 W/E (ESO 060-IG016 West and ESO 060-IG016 East): This LIRG system is a stage-B merger, composed of two galaxies separated by 15 4 (13.6 kpc). We identify ESO 060-IG016 East as the hard X-ray detected AGN. The Chandra spectrum of ESO 060-IG016 East reveals the presence of an obscured AGN with N_ H 1 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 Iwasawa2011 , whose 8--24 keV emission is detected with NuSTAR. Combining these observations, we constrain the column density and absorption-corrected AGN luminosity to be N_ H = (8.4 ^ +4.0 _ 2.9 ) 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 and log L_ 2-10 = 41.94 ^ +0.07 _ 0.08 , respectively. NuSTAR and Chandra detect no significant X-ray emission from ESO 060-IG016 West.

gmw_00000645.name: NGC 4922N
gmw_00000645.overview: NGC 4922N is a luminous infrared galaxy that contains an obscured, hard X-ray active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2107.10855]. It is part of a stage-C merger system, separated from its southern companion galaxy by 10.6 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2107.10855].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4922N as the LIRG and hard X-ray AGN in a stage-C merger, supporting an obscured nucleus with XCLUMPY analysis.

quotes.quote: IRAS F12590+2934 S/N (NGC 4922S and NGC 4922N): The system is a stage-C merger with the two galaxies separated by 22 1 (10.6 kpc). The northern source (NGC 4922N) is a LIRG, whereas the southern (NGC 4922S) is less luminous (log L_ IR / L_ = 8.87; Ricci2017bMNRAS ). We identify NGC 4922N as the hard X-ray detected AGN. The X-ray study with NuSTAR and Chandra by Ricci2017bMNRAS suggests a CT AGN in NGC 4922N ( N_ H 4.27 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 ). Our analysis with XCLUMPY supports the presence of the obscured AGN, with an absorption-corrected X-ray luminosity of log L_ 2-10 = 42.00 ^ +0.20 _ 0.18 , although the absorption is somewhat smaller ( N_ H = 7.6^ +28.5 _ 2.2 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 ) than that in Ricci2017bMNRAS .

gmw_00000646.name: Arp 220W
gmw_00000646.overview: Arp 220W is the western nucleus of the merging system Arp 220, located 0.4 kiloparsecs away from its eastern counterpart [arXiv:2107.10855]. It is identified as a hard X-ray active galactic nucleus that is heavily Compton-thick and coexists with starburst emission [arXiv:2107.10855].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Arp 220W in the nearest stage-D ULIRG as the hard X-ray AGN, favoring heavy Compton-thick obscuration with coexisting starburst emission.

quotes.quote: IRAS F15327+2340 W/E (Arp 220W and Arp 220E): Arp 220 (= UGC 9913) is the nearest ULIRG among the stage-D mergers, containing two nuclei (west and east components) with a separation of 1 0 (0.4 kpc). The far-IR and millimeter/submillimeter observations e.g., Downes2007,Gonzalez-Alfonso2012,Scoville2015,Falstad2021 suggest that Arp 220W contains a heavily CT AGN ( N_ H 5 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 ), whereas no clear hints for an AGN in Arp 220E have been obtained in previous multiwavelength studies. We identify Arp 220W as the hard X-ray detected AGN. The Chandra image finds that the > 2 keV emission is peaked at the western nucleus Clements2002,Iwasawa2005 , which shows an X-ray color consistent with those of obscured AGNs Iwasawa2011 . Paggi2017 constrain the absorption-corrected AGN 2--10 keV luminosities to be < 1 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 (west) and < 0.4 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 (east), which are derived from the 3 upper limits of the neutral Fe K flux of the nuclear regions.

gmw_00000647.name: NGC 6240N
gmw_00000647.overview: NGC 6240N is the northern nucleus of the luminous infrared galaxy merger IRAS F16504+0228 [arXiv:2107.10855]. It hosts a Compton-thick active galactic nucleus separated by 0.8 kiloparsecs from its southern counterpart [arXiv:2107.10855].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6240N as the northern nucleus of NGC 6240, part of a dual Compton-thick AGN system with measured column density and luminosity.

quotes.quote: IRAS F16504+0228 S/N (NGC 6240S and NGC 6240N): This LIRG is a stage-D merger composed of two nuclei, NGC 6240S (south) and NGC 6240N (north), with a separation of 1 7 (0.8 kpc). This target has been well studied with Chandra Ptak2003,Grimes2005,Iwasawa2011 , and it is revealed that both nuclei contain CT AGNs showing reflected-dominated spectra Komossa2003 . Puccetti2016 carry out a detailed spectral analysis of the NuSTAR, Chandra, XMM-Newton, and BeppoSAX data, and confirm the presence of the dual CT AGNs (see also Nardini2017 ). They estimate the column densities ( N_ H = 1.47^ +0.21 _ 0.17 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 and N_ H = 1.55^ +0.72 _ 0.23 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 ) and the absorption-corrected X-ray luminosities (log L_ 2-10 = 43.72 ^ +0.20 _ 0.19 and log L_ 2-10 = 43.30 ^ +0.24 _ 0.20 ) for NGC 6240S and NGC 6240N, respectively.

gmw_00000648.name: ESO 148-2
gmw_00000648.overview: ESO 148-2 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy in the final stages of a merger [arXiv:2306.14843, arXiv:2107.10855]. The system features a star-forming northern nucleus and an active galactic nucleus in the southern nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0510282, arXiv:1509.08468], which drives a galactic outflow [arXiv:1806.03311].

sources.summary: The paper describes the southern nucleus of IRAS 23128-1808 as having low 3.3 micron equivalent width, strong ice absorption, and compact-source evidence, with hard X-rays revealing an AGN.

quotes.quote: IRAS 23128-1808 . The southern nucleus has a flat continuum, with a moderate equivalent width 3.3 m emission (EW _ 3.3 =43 5 , well below the typical value for starbursts). A strong ice absorption ( _ 3.1 =0.45 ) is clearly detected. The relatively low EW of the 3.3 m suggests the presence of an AGN. The 3.1 m absorption feature also suggests the presence of a compact source.

In this case, the good continuum determination leaves no doubts on the interpretation of the 2.9-3.3 m spectrum, since the alternative scenario of an inverted continuum and a 2.9-3 m excess is ruled out by the data. The northern nucleus has a typical starburst spectrum with a steep continuum and a strong 3.3 m emission line (EW 130 nm). No AGN indication is found in mid-IR spectra, while the hard X-ray spectrum clearly reveals the presence of an AGN. This source is therefore a case where the L-band analysis proves to be more effective than ISO spectroscopy in revealing the presence of an active nucleus as the origin of the infrared emission.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS23128-5919 as a deep-diving (U)LIRG with depressed O/H relative to its mass, high SFR, late merger stage, and solar-like N/O.

quotes.quote: Interestingly, four galaxies namely Haro11, IRAS12112+0305, IRAS20551-4250 and IRAS23128-5919 strongly deviate from the MZR showing metallicities lower by a factor of 2 than expected for these deep-diving (U)LIRGs stellar masses according to the MZR. While Haro11 is not a ULIRG galaxy like the others, this star-forming galaxy is reported to be a well known merger dominated galaxy that shared many of properties observed in ULIRGs. Notably, IRAS12112+0305 was previously reported to have a super-solar abundance ( 2Z _ ) , while IRAS20551-4250 and IRAS23128-5919 where reported to have abundances close to the values expected from the MZR ar their masses . These high estimations of O/H are driven by the nearly solar N/O values obtained here. However, our O/H estimation -- independent of N/O-- is significantly lower, thus suggesting a more complex mixing process of the gas affecting their chemical composition.

We show in Figure fig:MZR that these deep-diving (U)LIRGs have the highest SFR values in our sample ( >100 M _ yr ^ 1 ) and they are in the final stages of their merges. Additionally, we find that the N/O ratios for these four galaxies are similar to the solar ratio, which suggests that they have reached a relatively mature stage of their chemical enrichment history .

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 23128-5919 as a marginally resolved merging pair, derives nuclear sizes and extinction, and classifies it as a starburst.

quotes.quote: IRAS 23128-5919 - The two galaxies in this merging pair are separated by a projected distance of less than 5 , and are marginally resolved by ISOCAM. We derive HPBWs of the order of 2.5 \ for both nuclei. For lack of adequate high-resolution data, and in analogy with NGC 6240, we adopt a total size of 3 \ for each nucleus, thus an equivalent size of 4.2 . To derive the extinction, we combined measurements of pure H and Pa fluxes in the southern nucleus, which is much brighter than the northern nucleus.

In the MIR, we performed the brightness profile fitting and aperture photometry of the two blended nuclei together. Since the CVF spectrum has poor signal to noise ratio, and the 12-18 m flux simulated from the CVF is not in agreement with the observed broadband flux, we used only broadband images. From both optical and MIR diagnostics, this system is classified as a starburst in which the large velocities observed in emission lines of the southern nucleus are caused by starburst superwinds Johansson88, Lut99b .

sources.summary: The paper describes this close ULIRG as a two-nucleus system with star formation in the north and AGN-linked Seyfert/composite broad components in the south.

quotes.quote: IRAS F23128-5919 (ESO 148-IG002) The nuclei in this closely interacting ULIRG are separated by a projected distance of about 4 kpc and show distinctly different properties in our spectra. The northern nucleus has line ratio and velocity dispersion values consistent with star formation. The southern nucleus is about three times brighter in at 24 and hosts an X-ray and IR detected AGN Franceschini03,Petric11,Iwasawa11 , which are mirrored in the enhanced composite/Seyfert line ratios and broadened velocity dispersions seen in our IFS diagnostic diagrams and emission line maps. The emission line components associated with the composite/Seyfert emission show broad velocity dispersions of 200-400 , though there is still a significant contribution below 100 , likely due to the ongoing star formation in both nuclei. The broad component shows signs of rotation while the underlying narrow component seems to show none, indicating most of the narrow emission may be coming from the non-AGN galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO 148-2 as a stage-C ULIRG merger with a hard X-ray AGN, variable spectral features, and a column density reaching Compton-thick levels.

quotes.quote: IRAS F23128--5919 (ESO 148-2): This ULIRG is a stage-C merger with two galaxies separated by 4 5 (3.9 kpc; Zenner1993 ). We identify this pair (ESO 148-2) as the hard X-ray detected AGN. Previous studies using Chandra Ptak2003,Grimes2005,Iwasawa2011 and XMM-Newton Franceschini2003 demonstrate that the southern nucleus hosts an obscured AGN. NuSTAR detects the 8--24 keV emission (3.1 ) and variable spectral features between the NuSTAR and other soft X-ray observations, indicating the presence of an AGN. Adopting the XCLUMPY model, we find that the column density increases dramatically from a Compton-thin level with N_ H = (2.7^ +0.9 _ 0.6 ) 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 (Chandra and XMM-Newton) to a CT level with N_ H = (1.6 0.5) 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 (NuSTAR), as reported in Section subsub6-1-5_NHvariability .

sources.summary: The survey reports double-peaked CO and [C I] emission in ESO 148-IG002, uncertain north-south continuum structure, and uses a large aperture to avoid calibration uncertainty.

quotes.quote: ESO 148-IG002 A double peak was observed in both CO (4--3) and [ C 1 ] (1--0) emission. The uncertain north-south structure is observed in the continuum emission. This could be due to the calibration issues, but the clear reason is unknown. Further sensitive observations are required. To avoid the uncertainty, we use 20 20 aperture to measure continuum flux density.

sources.summary: The paper treats ESO 148-IG002 as a highly ionized LIRG near the AGN-classification boundary; its southern nucleus is AGN dominated and it has a relatively high [O IV]/[Ne II] ratio.

quotes.quote: The 6.2 m PAH EQWs of these sources are large ( 0.5 m ), with the exception of ESO 148-IG002 , which is on the boundary of our AGN classification with a 6.2 m PAH EQW of 0.31 m . Its X-ray Iwas11 and optical (Rich et al. 2013) spectra indicate that the southern nucleus is dominated by AGN. The corresponding [ OIV ] / [ NeII ] ratios of IRAS 03582+6012 W, IRAS 05129+5128 , NGC 6052 , and ESO 148-IG002 are 0.06 0.02 , 0.04 0.01 , 0.08 0.02 , and 0.47 0.08 ( CGCG 448-020 W does not have a [ OIV ] detection).

sources.summary: The paper uses IRAS F23128-5919 as a benchmark galaxy where outflowing gas diagnostics indicated in-situ star formation, then includes its measurements when fitting MaNGA outflow relations.

quotes.quote: They analysed optical/near-IR spectroscopic data of the prominent galactic outflow previously detected in the galaxy IRAS F23128-5919 Leslie2014,Bellocchi2013,Arribas2014,Cazzoli2016,Piqueras-Lopez2012 and found that multiple optical and near-IR diagnostics of the outflowing gas are consistent with in-situ star formation, i.e. inside the outflow.
Blue symbols are star forming outflows discovered in this paper, while the red symbol is the star formation previously discovered inside the outflow of the galaxy IRAS F23128-5919 Maiolino2017 .
Again, two fits were performed: firstly to the MaNGA outflows alone, and secondly to the MaNGA outflows including IRAS F23128-5919 in the fit.

gmw_00000649.name: 3C454.3
gmw_00000649.overview: 3C 454.3 is a highly variable flat-spectrum radio quasar and blazar located at a redshift of z = 0.859 [arXiv:1007.0483, arXiv:1807.02926]. The active galactic nucleus features a complex, curved relativistic jet [arXiv:0908.2722, arXiv:1702.02354] that exhibits superluminal motion [arXiv:1509.07529, arXiv:astro-ph/0612149] and drives frequent multiwavelength outbursts [arXiv:1107.1093, arXiv:1712.08274].

sources.summary: The paper presents Fermi LAT observations of a record 2010 gamma-ray outburst from the flat-spectrum radio quasar 3C454.3, describing its historical activity, rapid plateau-to-flare evolution, spectral changes, and extreme apparent luminosity.

quotes.quote: The radio source 3C454.3, a well-known flat-spectrum radio quasar (FSRQ) at redshift z=0.859 , has shown remarkably high activity since 2000. It has been particularly bright in the ray band covered by AGILE and the Fermi LAT, reaching a daily record flux level F [ E>100 MeV ] ( F_ 100 in units of 10^ 6 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 ) of 22 1 in December 2009 Str10,2010ApJ...721.1383A . This high flux allowed detailed analysis to be performed, making it the best-studied blazar in the GeV band. Gamma-ray variability on timescales as short as a few hours Tav10 and a flux-doubling time scale of less than 3 hours for a short subflare on 2009 Dec 5 MJD55170, 2010ApJ...721.1383A have been reported.

During its five-day outburst from 2010 November 17 to 21 (flare interval in Figure fig:light_curve_1 ), 3C454.3 was the brightest GeV ray source in the sky, with a flux F_ 100 =66 2 on 2010 November 18--19. Prior to the flaring phase, the Fermi LAT light curve displays a 13-day long flux plateau preceding the major outburst. The onset of the plateau is marked by a rapid ( <1 ) day flux increase by a factor of 2 . This feature appears to be a characteristic behavior indicating that 3C454.3 is about to flare, as noted in .

sources.summary: The paper presents 2008-2010 multiwavelength monitoring of 3C 454.3, finding radio-to-gamma variability, optical-UV dilution by steadier components, prominent gamma flares, and jet-driven behavior.

quotes.quote: The blazar 3C 454.3 is one of the most active sources from the radio to the ray frequencies observed in the past few years. We present multiwavelength observations of this source from April 2008 to March 2010. The radio to optical data are mostly from the GASP-WEBT, UV and X-ray data from Swift, and ray data from the AGILE and Fermi satellites. The aim is to understand the connection among emissions at different frequencies and to derive information on the emitting jet. Light curves in 18 bands were carefully assembled to study flux variability correlations.

The observations reveal prominent variability above 8 GHz. In the optical--UV band, the variability amplitude decreases with increasing frequency due to a steadier radiation from both a broad line region and an accretion disc. The optical flux reaches nearly the same levels in the 2008--2009 and 2009--2010 observing seasons; the mm one shows similar behaviour, whereas the and X-ray flux levels rise in the second period. Two prominent ray flares in mid 2008 and late 2009 show a double-peaked structure, with a variable /optical flux ratio.

sources.summary: The paper highlights 3C 454.3 as the sample's most spectacular FSRQ, with post-2005 major outbursts, high millimeter brightness, broad flux tails, and changing spectral index.

quotes.quote: 3C 454.3 This flat spectrum radio quasar shows the most spectacular lightcurves among the sources of our sample. For the first nine years (1996-2005) of monitoring the activity of this source was restricted to variability on levels of 2--8 Jy. This changed dramatically in 2005 when the first of at least three major outbursts occured, peaking at flux densities of 35--40 Jy.

All outbursts show short exponential rise and decay times in the range 0.1--0.5 years. The 3-mm (1.3-mm) flux distribution is dominated by a Gaussian peak centered at 6 Jy ( 4 Jy) with an extended tail ranging out to 40 Jy ( 35 Jy). The spectral index has been fluctuating around 0 , with the most extreme values being 0.4 and 0.5 , respectively. A somewhat steep value of 0.5 can be observed for the time 1996--2000. Notably, the most extreme change (from 0.4 to 0.4 ) occurs at the time of the peak of the first bright outburst in 2005.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C 454.3 as the dominant source of the brightest Fermi blazar flares, with longer, more complex flares attributed to its unusually small jet viewing angle.

quotes.quote: My sample of the brightest gamma-ray flares of blazars reported in Table tab_top40 is produced by only 5 blazars: 3C 454.3 (16 entries), PKS 1510 089 (14 entries), PKS 1222+216 (6 entries), 3C 273 (3 entries), and PKS 0402 362 (1 entry). Remarkably, 3C 454.3 produced 7 of the 10 brightest flares. About 20 flares exceed the peak flux level of 10^ 5 ph s^ 1 cm^ 2 .

There appears to be no general trend between the flare duration and the peak flux. However, there is a significant difference between 3C 454.3 on one hand, and PKS 1510 089, PKS 1222+216 and 3C 273 on the other. Flares produced by 3C 454.3 are on average longer than those produced by other blazars. 3C 454.3 did not produce a flare shorter than 1.6 d , while other blazars generally produced flares shorter than 3 d , with the sole exception of flare 28 (PKS 1510 089 at MJD 55991).

The distinct structure of flares produced systematically by 3C 454.3 could be explained by its special jet orientation. provide estimates of the jet Lorentz factor _ j =(1- _ j ^2)^ 1/2 , where _ j =v_ j / , and viewing angle _ obs for a large sample of blazars monitored in the MOJAVE program. They estimate the viewing angle of 3C 454.3 at _ obs 1.3^ 0.45 _ j ^ 1 , which is significantly smaller than the values for PKS 1510-089 ( 3.4^ ), PKS 1222+216 ( 5.1^ ) and 3C 273 ( 3.3^ ). The corresponding Doppler factor for 3C 454.3, D = [ _ j (1- _ j _ obs ) ] ^ 1 33 1.7 _ j , is the highest among the blazars producing the brightest gamma-ray flares.

sources.summary: The paper models the blazar 3C 454.3, explaining its GeV spectral break through near-equipartition external inverse-Compton scattering by BLR and torus photons.

quotes.quote: The ray emission of \ (2251+158) at redshift z = 0.859 , was first observed with EGRET Hartman93 . Major pre- \ outbursts occurred in 2005 Villata06, Pian06, Fuhrmann06 , and a large flare in 2007 was observed in rays with the AGILE experiment Ghisellini07, Vercellone08, Raiteri08, Vercellone09, Donnarumma09 . Since the launch of the \ mission, \ has been monitored in the GeV energy band, providing the first continuous long-term light-curve in rays. The source exhibited several major flares in December 2009 and April 2010 Ackermann10 , and in November 2010 Abdo11, Vercellone11 . The \ observations have shown that the spectrum of this source does not follow a power-law behavior, but instead shows a clear break at an energy of a few GeV Ackermann10,Abdo11 .

The modeling of the SED of \ has been performed using a one-zone SSC model as described in Katarzynski01 , adding the computation of the EIC emission component for low-energy target photons from the BLR, the dust torus, and the accretion disk. As can be seen in Fig. fig1 , this scenario reproduces the \ observations for both the lower and the higher flux states. We have applied the equipartition scenario described in a companion paper equipartitionpaper , but improved by the inclusion of multiple emission lines in the BLR and use of blackbody rather than monochromatic dust spectrum, to model the SED of the well-studied FSRQ .

sources.summary: The paper discusses 3C 454.3 as the known blazar with a reported gamma-ray PSD break, but finds only a lower limit on its OU characteristic time scale.

quotes.quote: However, the featureless power-law like Fermi/LAT blazar PSD have been so far preventing this kind of inference, with 3C 454.3 being the only blazar with a ray PSD break reported in the literature. Subsequently, NM13 found a break frequency corresponding to t 7.9 days in the first 4-year Fermi/LAT light curve of 3C 454.3. This assumption allowed them to estimate the black hole mass in 3C 454.3 to be in the 10^8 - 10^ 10 M _ range. The PSD slopes below and above the breaks estimated by Ackermann et al. (2010) and NM13 as well as the location of the PSD breaks were inconsistent with each other which may suggest either a non-stationarity of the variability process in this source, or indeed distinct variability properties of the ray flares.

In the case of 3C 454.3 the characteristic time scale is not constrained, and we derive the lower limit on its value, _ 0 / day > 2.0 . In 3C 454.3 the OU modeling resulted only in a lower limit, _ 0 / day > 2.0 , which was inconsistent with the NM13 result. NM13 found that the PSD of 3C 454.3 can be characterized with 1 and 3 below and above the PSD break, respectively. In 3C 454.3, PKS 1510-089, 3C 273, BL Lac and Mkn 421 our slopes were shallower than those derived by NM13.

sources.summary: The paper models two major 3C 454.3 gamma-ray flares, deriving luminosity, variability, Lorentz-factor, and distance constraints that place dissipation near the BLR.

quotes.quote: 3C 454.3 at MJD 55520 3C 454.3 ( z = 0.859 , d_ L 5.49 Gpc ) provided us with the most spectacular gamma-ray flares in the Fermi era 2013MNRAS.430.1324N . On MJD 55520 (2010 Nov 20) it produced a flare of apparent peak bolometric ( E > 100 MeV ) luminosity of L_ ,bol 2.1 10^ 50 erg s^ 1 2011ApJ...733L..26A . We convert the bolometric peak luminosity L_ ,bol into the peak L_ luminosity L_ , using a bolometric correction factor g_ ,bol = L_ ,bol /L_ 4.5 calculated from the best-fit spectral model (power-law with exponential cut-off), resulting in L_ 4.7 10^ 49 erg s^ 1 . The flare temporal template fitted by has a flux doubling time scale of t_ var,obs 8.7 h 3.13 10^4 s .

The intersection of the first two of these constraints gives the marginal solution -- the minimum Lorentz factor _ min 30 and the minimum distance scale r_ min 0.16 pc . For (r_ min , _ min ) , other constraints yield the following predictions: _ SSA,obs 125 m , E_ max,obs 10 TeV , u_ '/u_ B ' 3.3 , and L_ j,min 1.7 10^ 46 erg s^ 1 0.25 L_ d . On the other hand, in the IR region ( r_ IR ), the SSC constraint is much stronger and hence there are no solutions with < 50 . Therefore, in this case the dissipation region is clearly constrained to be located not far from r_ BLR .

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 454.3 as a flat-spectrum radio quasar whose 2014 Fermi outburst had plateau-flare-post-flare structure, hard multi-day gamma-ray spectrum, high peak flux, and >20 GeV photons.

quotes.quote: A prominent outburst of the flat spectrum radio quasar 3C 454.3 was observed in 2014 June with the Fermi Large Area Telescope. This outburst was characterized by a three-stage light-curve pattern---plateau, flare and post-flare---that occurred from 2014 May to July, in a similar pattern as observed during the exceptional outburst in 2010 November. The highest flux of the outburst reported in this paper occurred during 2014 June 7--29, showing a multiple-peak structure in the light-curves. The average flux in these 22 days was found to be F [ E > 100 MeV ] = (7.2 0.2) 10^ 6 , with a spectral index, for a simple power law, of = 2.04 0.01 . That made this outburst the first ray high state of 3C 454.3 ever to be detected by Fermi with such a hard spectrum over several days.

The highest flux was recorded on 2014 June 15, in a 3 hr bin, at MJD 56823.5625, at a level of F [ E > 100 MeV ] = (17.6 1.9) 10^ 6 . The rise time of one of the short subflares was found to be T_r= 1200 700 s at MJD = 56827, when the flux increased from 4 to 12 10^ 6 . Several photons above 20 GeV were collected during this outburst, including one at 45 GeV on MJD 56827, constraining the ray emission region to be located close to the outer boundary of the broad-line region, leading to fast flux variability.

sources.summary: The paper models the November 2010 flare of the flat-spectrum radio quasar 3C 454.3, finding lepto-hadronic fits reproduce its broadband SED and multi-band light curves.

quotes.quote: In this study, we use a one-zone leptonic and a lepto-hadronic model to investigate the multi-wavelength emission and the prominent flare of the flat spectrum radio quasar 3C 454.3 in Nov 2010. We perform a parameter study with both models to obtain broadband fits to the spectral energy distribution of 3C 454.3. Starting with the baseline parameters obtained from the fits, we then investigate different flaring scenarios for both models to explain an extreme outburst and spectral hardening of 3C 454.3 that occurred in Nov 2010. We find that the one zone lepto-hadronic model can successfully explain both the broadband multi-wavelength spectral energy distribution and light curves in the optical R, Swift XRT and Fermi ray bandpasses for 3C 454.3 during quiescence and the peak of the Nov. 2010 flare.

The flat spectrum radio quasar 3C 454.3 (z = 0.859) is one of the brightest and most variable ray sources in the night sky. 3C 454.3 exhibited many outbursts in the pre Fermi era. In 2005, 3C 454.3 displayed a prominent optical outburst, reaching its historical maximum with R = 12.0 , Villata06 . The event triggered follow-up X-ray observations with INTEGRAL Pian06 and Swift Giommi06 , ray observations with AGILE Vercellone07 as well in the radio band, where an outburst with about a one year delay Villata07 was seen.

sources.summary: The paper finds that 3C 454.3 has a northwest-bending jet, transverse Faraday-rotation gradients 10-35 mas from the core, enhanced core RM, and an overall longitudinal magnetic field.

quotes.quote: 3C 454.3 : The 1358 MHz jet of 3C 454.3 extends towards the north-west in a PA of 55^ . On the smaller scales probed by the 2 cm MOJAVE observations, the jet of 3C 454.3 extends towards the west before bending towards the north-west to lie in roughly the same PA shown in our map. The EVPAs corrected for the integrated RM are predominantly transverse to the local jet direction (Fig. Fig2_3C345_BLLac_3C454.3 f). The degree of polarization ranges from 2 per cent to 8 per cent in the core region (Fig. Fig2_3C345_BLLac_3C454.3 g) and reaches tens of per cent in the jet (Fig. Fig2_3C345_BLLac_3C454.3 h). Our RM map shows monotonic, transverse Faraday rotation gradients at projected distances of 10-35 mas from the core, as is shown in Fig. 3C454.3_RM .

The direction of both transverse RM gradients is the same, with higher RM values towards the southern side of the jet. The RM values display no sign change and the RM magnitude is appreciably enhanced in the core. Both fractional polarization slices show appreciable asymmetry, with higher degrees of polarization observed on the northern side of the jet, where the RM values are lower.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes the December 2009 flare of blazar 3C 454.3, emphasizing simultaneous high-energy and optical/NIR behavior, unusual polarization swings, and SED models for jet emission.

quotes.quote: The blazar 3C454.3 exhibited a strong flare seen in rays, X-rays, and optical/NIR bands during 3--12 December 2009.
