sources.summary: The paper models 3C 454.3 as a bright gamma-ray blazar; its inverse-Compton peak and SED peak separation rise with gamma-ray activity, while its synchrotron peak lacks a significant flux correlation.

quotes.quote: We selected 3C 279 and 3C 454.3 because these are among the brightest ray blazars with hundreds of existing multi-wavelength observations supplemented with more-than-a-decade of continuous monitoring with the Large Area Telescope ( LAT). The dense sampling has allowed us to study their multi-epoch SED evolution using contemporaneous data covering all accessible electromagnetic bands. Moreover, near-IR (NIR) to optical-ultraviolet (UV) spectra of both objects are strongly dominated by non-thermal synchrotron emission from the jet, with signatures of direct accretion disk emission only emerging in very low activity states e.g., Pian1999 and at frequencies far beyond the synchrotron peak.

Both _ pk ^ syn and _ pk ^ IC have been found to correlate positively with the ray activity for 3C 279. For 3C 454.3, _ pk ^ IC follows the same trend though _ pk ^ syn does not show any significant correlation with the ray flux. Interestingly, the separation of both SED peaks strongly correlates with the ray flux for both sources. This can be understood by comparing the _ pk ^ syn and _ pk ^ IC panels in Figure fig:peak which reveals that the extent of variation is larger for the latter.

sources.summary: The paper follows 3C 454.3 after its exceptional 2005 bright state, using WEBT multifrequency monitoring to connect optical-radio delays, radio outburst evolution, variability mechanisms, and jet structure.

quotes.quote: In spring 2005 the blazar 3C 454.3 was observed in an unprecedented bright state from the near-IR to the hard X-ray frequencies. A mm outburst peaked in June--July 2005, and it was followed by a flux increase at high radio frequencies. In this paper we report on multifrequency monitoring by the WEBT aimed at following the further evolution of the outburst in detail. In particular, we investigate the expected correlation and time delays between the optical and radio emissions in order to derive information on the variability mechanisms and jet structure.

In May 2005 the quasar-type blazar 3C 454.3 (2251+158) was observed in an unprecedented luminous state from near-IR to hard X-ray frequencies. A big observing effort was spent to follow the outburst, involving both ground-based and space instruments fuh06,gio06,pia06,vil06 .

sources.summary: The paper monitors quasar-type blazar 3C 454.3 during 2007 activity, presenting radio-to-UV and optical light curves, rapid variability, SED changes, and evidence for a faint-state thermal accretion-disc contribution.

quotes.quote: The quasar-type blazar 3C 454.3 underwent a phase of high activity in summer and autumn 2007, which was intensively monitored in the radio-to-optical bands by the Whole Earth Blazar Telescope (WEBT). The ray satellite Astro-rivelatore Gamma a Immagini LEggero (AGILE) detected this source first in late July, and then in November--December 2007. In this letter we present the multifrequency data collected by the WEBT and collaborators during the second AGILE observing period, complemented by a few contemporaneous data from the UltraViolet and Optical Telescope (UVOT) onboard the Swift satellite. The aim is to trace in detail the behaviour of the synchrotron emission from the blazar jet, and to investigate the contribution from the thermal emission component. Optical data from about twenty telescopes have been homogeneously calibrated and carefully assembled to construct an R band light curve containing about 1340 data points in 42 days.

sources.summary: During a May 2005 outburst, 3C454.3 was monitored with 45 XPOL measurements; its brightness enabled precise polarization measurements, but intrinsic variability limited elevation-dependent calibration tests.

quotes.quote: A new opportunity for a more precise measurement of this effect occured in May 2005 when the quasar 3C454.3 had an outburst. We made 45 dedicated XPOL measurements over the elevation range from 10 \ to 69 . Due to the brightness of the source ( S_ 15 Jy) the precision of an individual polarization measurement was 0.1 , and the linear polarization of the quasar ( \ =4 , =60 ) was easily detected. The suspected elevation dependence of the instrumental polarization would show up as an elevation modulation of \ around its mean value. Indeed such modulations were found at levels of 1.5 ( Q ) and 0.5 ( U ). Unfortunately, these modulations are indistinguishable from short--timescale intrinsic polarization variations of the quasar. Indeed, our measurements show that the total power (Stokes I ) of the quasar varied over the 12 hours monitoring period by more than 20 .

sources.summary: The paper presents 3C 454.3 as a highly active FSRQ with a 2005 outburst observed by ground facilities, Swift, Chandra, and INTEGRAL, interpreted using a jet economic model.

quotes.quote: 3C 454.3 (z=0.859) This FSRQ has been extremely active since 2005 and several multiwavelength campaigns were performed. In May 2005 , 3C 454.3 underwent a long and intense outburst observed by many ground based telescopes (WEBT ) and satellites ( Swift , Chandra ). Also INTEGRAL was activated and pointed to the blazar on May 15-18 , 2005 (PI Pian) . This observation can be better understood in the framework of the ``jet economic model'' and by comparing the source evolution over several years . According to this model, the ratio between Synchrotron Self-Compton (SSC) and External Compton (EC) emission is regulated by the bulk Lorentz factor , which in turn is linked to the compactness of the source.

sources.summary: Suzaku XIS/PIN spectra of 3C 454.3 are modeled with absorption above the Galactic value, yielding a hard photon index and high 2-10 keV flux.

quotes.quote: 3C 454.3 We first fitted the XISs and PIN spectra with a single power-law model with a Galactic absorption of N_ H = 7.24 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 (Murphy et al. 1996). We obtained the photon index of = 1.41 0.01 ( ^2_ = 1.00 for 1090 dof), but the residuals show some scatter around 1 keV. To investigate this scatter in more detail, we fitted the spectra with a single power-law and a free absorption model.

In the case of 3C 454.3 we obtained the best fit to the XISs and HXD/PIN spectra assuming an absorbed power-law model with a photon index of = 1.61 0.01 and a column density of N_ H = (9.07 0.35) 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 , which is larger than the Galactic value at the 99.9 confidence level. The unabsorbed 2-10 keV flux is F_ 2-10 keV = (1.66 0.01) 10^ 11 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 (Figure fig:QHBspec ).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Fermi/LAT observations of the blazar 3C 454.3 during its early April 2010 outburst, finding variability on 2-5 hour time scales and no significant evidence for sub-hour variability.

quotes.quote: We presented the variability analysis of the ray observation with Fermi /LAT of the blazar 3C 454.3 pointed during the outburst of early April 2010. Contrary to the usual scanning mode, this time Fermi performed a 200 ks pointed observation (Wallace et al. 2010), which guarantees a higher sensitivity and a longer source ontime. These particular settings, together with the exceptionally high flux emitted by the blazar, enabled us to probe the shortest time scales that we found to be in the range of 2-5 hours, confirming the indications found by Bonnoli et al. (2010), Tavecchio et al. (2010) and in agreement with the current theories. Although the significance of the events is not very strong ( 3 ), the fact that it is present in the light curves with different time bins is an indication that the findings are well grounded. On the other side, we have not found significant evidence of sub-hour time scales, but the present data set is not yet conclusive.

sources.summary: The paper reports AGILE observations of the blazar 3C 454.3 during its December 2009 gamma-ray flare, including the light curve, spectra, flux rise, and energetics.

quotes.quote: We present the gamma-ray data of the extraordinary flaring activity above 100 MeV from the flat spectrum radio quasar 3C 454.3 detected by AGILE during the month of December 2009.

Because of the AGILE new pointing strategy, the FSRQ 3C 454.3 has been constantly monitored with no gaps since early November 2009, with a typical 2-day exposure value of 10^7 cm^2 s at 100 MeV. All the gamma-ray fluxes reported in this paper were obtained with a maximum likelihood analysis, using our FM3.119 calibrated filter. We used a standard event selection procedure that takes into account the SAA passage, and a standard Earth albedo photon filtering procedure. Fig. LC shows in the upper panel the 2 and 1/2 year gamma-ray lightcurve of obtained by integrating all available AGILE gamma-ray data since July, 2007. The lower panel of Fig. LC shows the detailed AGILE-GRID gamma-ray lightcurve of 3C 454.3 obtained for the period from November 7, 2009 until January 9, 2010. Integrating from 2009-11-10 (MJD = 55145.7) until 2009-12-02 (MJD = 55167.7), the source shows an increasing flux above 100 MeV from a value of F_ ,1 100 10^ 8 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 up to a Vela-PSR like flux, F_ ,2 1000 10^ 8 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 .
We carried out a time-resolved spectral analysis of 3C 454.3 dividing the period of exceptional gamma-ray activity in three time intervals.

sources.summary: The paper compares 3C 454.3's low-frequency ATA polarization with 8 and 43 GHz behavior, noting a single RM component and unusually high 1-2 GHz polarization fraction.

quotes.quote: 3C 454.3 Source J225357+160853 is 3C 454.3 (a.k.a. \ B2251+158), which has a low polarization fraction of 0.7 at 8 GHz, as compared to 9 in our observations z03 . The RM distribution is irregular with a value at peak brightness of 263 rad m ^ 2 at 8 GHz; we measure a single component of 54 rad m ^ 2 from 1--2 GHz. This source is a blazar and is highly variable at radio wavelengths, so it is difficult to make strong conclusions in this comparison. However, monitoring at 43 GHz shows that its polarization fraction rarely reaches 9 j10 . If so, this source may have a higher polarization fraction from 1--2 GHz than at higher frequencies, which differs from the trend observed toward 3C 273, 3C 446, and 3C 279.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 454.3 as a highly variable FSRQ monitored by AGILE and many observatories during optical, X-ray, and gamma-ray flaring states.

quotes.quote: 3C 454.3 Among the FSRQ class of blazars, 3C 454.3 is one of the brightest objects and also the source that exhibited the most variable activity in the last years. In particular, during May 2005 the source was reported to undergo a very strong optical flare (Villata et al. 2006). This exceptionally high state triggered observations by high-energy satellites (RXTE: Remillard 2005; Chandra : Villata et al. 2006; INTEGRAL: Pian et al. 2006; Swift : Giommi et al. 2006), which confirmed an exceptionally high flux also in X-ray band. Unfortunately, no ray satellite was operative at that time. In mid-July 2007, 3C 454.3 underwent a new optical brightening that triggered observations at all frequencies, including a Target of Opportunity (ToO) by the AGILE ray satellite (Vercellone et al. 2008). That was the beginning of an extraordinary long-term ray activity of the source until the huge ray flares observed in early December 2009 (Striani et al. 2010a) and April 2010 (Ackermann et al. 2010).

sources.summary: The paper finds a physically meaningful NPSD turnover for 3C 454.3, interprets it as a gamma-ray variability timescale, and uses it to infer a central black-hole mass scale.

quotes.quote: One source, 3C 454.3, shows a specific time scale of 6.8 10^5  s, and this value suggests, assuming the internal shock model, a mass for the central black hole of (10^8 - 10^ 10 )M_ which is consistent with other estimates. It also indicates the typical time interval of ejected blobs is (7 - 70) times the light crossing time of the Schwarzschild radius.

On the other hand, the NPSD plot for 3C 454.3 show a turnover at 10^ 5.83 Hz and the slope above it, 3.08 0.83 , is well determined. The reduced ^2 value decreases from 1.29 for single power-law fit, which is not at acceptable level, to 0.23 for broken power-law fit, which is acceptable. Thus, only the plot for 3C 454.3, which exhibited an extraordinary large flare in 2010 November Abdo2011 , seems to show a physically meaningful turnover, at 10^ 5.83 Hz, which we discuss further in the next section.

sources.summary: The paper monitors 3C 454.3 through its 2010 outburst, finding rapid redder brightenings, high frequency-dependent polarization, and evidence for two polarization-vector components.

quotes.quote: The gamma-ray-detected blazar 3C 454.3 exhibits dramatic flux and polarization variations in the optical and near-infrared bands. In December 2010, the object emitted a very bright outburst. We monitored it for approximately four years (including the 2010 outburst) by optical and near-infrared photopolarimetry. During the 2010 outburst, the object emitted two rapid, redder brightenings, at which the polarization degrees (PDs) in both bands increased significantly and the bands exhibited a frequency-dependent polarization. The observed frequency-dependent polarization leads us to propose that the polarization vector is composed of two vectors.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 2251 + 158, also 3C 454.3, as an extremely active high-energy blazar with major multi-band flares, rapid optical variability, and strong internight/microvariability.

quotes.quote: PKS 2251 + 158: (also known as 3C 454.3) with z=0.860 , is one of the most active blazars at high energies Hartman92 ; during 2005 it underwent a major flaring in almost all energy bands Giommi06 . During 2007, a multiwavelength study was carried out by several observatories from radio to rays. In December 12, the flux increased 1.1 mag in 1.5  h, followed by a decrease of 1.2 mag in 1 h at optical wavelengths Raiteri08 , being one of the fastest variations ever detected in blazars. A new SED has been determined using AGILE /Swift data Vercellone08 . Another outburst was detected in 2009, at optical, X-ray and gamma frequencies.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes 3C 454.3 during 2014 high activity, finding correlated multi-band variability and changing lags consistent with a localized leptonic emission region.

quotes.quote: We present a multi-wavelength temporal analysis of the blazar 3C 454.3 during the high ray active period from May-December, 2014. Except for X-rays, the period is well sampled at near-infrared (NIR)-optical by the SMARTS facility and the source is detected continuously on daily timescale in the Fermi LAT ray band. The source exhibits diverse levels of variability with many flaring/active states in the continuously sampled ray light curve which are also reflected in the NIR-optical light curves and the sparsely sampled X-ray light curve by the Swift XRT. Multi-band correlation analysis of this continuous segment during different activity periods shows a change of state from no lags between IR and ray, optical and ray, and IR and optical to a state where ray lags the IR/optical by 3 days. The results are consistent with the previous studies of the same during various ray flaring and active episodes of the source.

sources.summary: The paper finds weak suborbital variability evidence for 3C 454.3, connects its gamma-ray and millimeter-core emission, and discusses upper limits and radio-core distances.

quotes.quote: Consecutively zooming in on the brightest flares, which we identify in an objective way through Bayesian blocks and a hill-climbing algorithm, we find variability on subhour time scales and as short as minutes for two sources in our sample (3C 279, CTA 102) and weak evidence for variability at time scales less than the Fermi satellite's orbit of 95 minutes for PKS 1510-089 and 3C 454.3. This suggests extremely compact emission regions in the jet. We do not find any signs for ray absorption in the broad-line region (BLR), which indicates that rays are produced at distances greater than hundreds of gravitational radii from the central black hole. This is further supported by a cross-correlation analysis between ray and radio/millimeter light curves, which is consistent with ray production at the same location as the millimeter core for 3C 273, CTA 102, and 3C 454.3. The inferred locations of the ray production zones are still consistent with the observed decay times of the brightest flares if the decay is caused by external Compton scattering with BLR photons. However, the minute-scale variability is challenging to explain in such scenarios.

As noted in the Introduction, two of the sources (3C 279 and CTA 102) have already been shown to be variable on extremely short timescales TheFermi-LAT:2016dss,2018ApJ...854L..26S . Here we will introduce a novel objective method to identify a large set of flares in order to conduct a comprehensive search for the short variability in our source sample. Furthermore, PKS B1222+216 2011ApJ...730L...8A , 3C 279 2008Sci...320.1752M , and PKS 1510-089 2011ApJ...730L...8A,2013A&A...554A.107H,2014A&A...569A..46A are among the seven FSRQs also detected above 100 GeV with imaging air Cerenkov Telescopes. For 3C 454.3, the MAGIC and VERITAS telescopes only obtained upper limits on the flux during flaring states in 2007 2009A&A...498...83A and 2010 2016AJ....151..142A .

The following distances d_ core, 15GHz were determined under the assumption that k_r = 1 : for PKS B1222+216 d_ core, 15GHz = 23.41 pc; for 3C 279 d_ core, 15GHz <7.88 pc; for PKS 1510-089 d_ core, 15GHz = 17.71 pc; for CTA 102 d_ core, 15GHz =46.7 pc; and for 3C 454.3 d_ core, 15GHz = 20.36 pc. Dedicated analyses have also been carried out and found for 3C 454.3 k_r = 0.6 0.8 and d_ core, 15GHz 38 pc, and, since d_ core , ^ 1/k_r , d_ core, 43GHz 9 pc 2014MNRAS.437.3396K . For 3C 273 2013ARep...57...34V found k_r = 1.4 and d_ core , = 134 ^ 1/1.4 using radio observations at frequencies between 4.8 and 362 GHz. Lastly, 2015A&A...576A..43F conducted VLBA observations of CTA 102 ranging from 5 to 86 GHz and found k_r = 1.0 as a best-fit value and d_ core, 86GHz 7 pc.

sources.summary: The paper highlights 3C 454.3 as a blazar with an optical polarization flare, near-simultaneous multiwavelength high-energy peak, no radio counterpart, and polarization anti-correlation.

quotes.quote: An intriguing optical polarization flare was recorded during the monitoring of the blazar 3C 454.3 on Dec., 3-12 (2009) (Fig. \ 7 below, Gupta et al. \ 2017). The reported light curves span 7 months and cover radio, optical, near-infrared, X-ray and ray bands. The strong flare peaked almost simultaneously in all the wavebands, however a radio counterpart was conspicuous by its absence.

Another intriguing aspect is that the onset of the dramatic rise in the optical polarization (and its position angle) from 3 to 20 coincides rather precisely with the beginning of the flare's decline in the high energy bands, as well as in the optical/near-infrared (Fig. 7). While this striking anti-correlation might be understood in terms of the `swinging jet' model (Gopal-Krishna Wiita 1992; also, Bachev 2015), the lack of a radio counterpart points towards a more complex situation.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 454.3 as having similar gamma-ray and radio variability, no measured lag, red-noise PSDs, large luminosity variation, and a strong harder-when-brighter trend.

quotes.quote: 3C 454.3 The correlations of variability between ray and radio bands for some individual flares have been widely reported for this source. We find that the long-term variability behaviors in the two energy bands are also very similar, however more spike flares are presented in the ray light curve than in 43 GHz. The DCF results indicate that the flux variations in the two energy bands are correlated without time-lag at a 2 confidence level. The PSD slopes in the two energy bands are also roughly consistent and correspond to the red-noise process. The largest luminosity variation among the eight blazars is observed in this source, almost changing three orders of magnitude, from 6.1 10^ 46 to 4.1 10^ 49 erg s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 454.3 as a distant, gamma-ray-bright FSRQ with strong multiwavelength flaring, a heavily studied 2010 outburst, SED modeling, and a short optical QPO claim.

quotes.quote: 3C 454.3 3C 454.3 ( =22^ h 53^ m 57 75, = 16 08 53 56, J2000 epoch) is the brightest in rays Vercellone11 and relatively distant FSRQ with z=0.859 Lynds67 , a BH mass of 8.3 10^ 8 M_ Woo02 , and known for its high variability in multiwavelength as well as prominent flaring activity e.g. Kushwaha17,Gupta17 . The 2010 outburst, which made the blazar the brightest in HE in the sky Abdo11 , was particularly well studied. Sasada14 analyzed mid-IR and optical data, spanning over 4 years, finding large-amplitude outburst lasting months, and rapid flares appearing and disappearing within a few days. Diltz16 modeled the broad-band SED generated with data gathered during the 2010 flare, concluding that the one zone lepto-hadronic model provides merely poor fits. Interestingly, Weaver19 discovered a QPO with a characteristic period of 36 minutes, analyzing R-band optical data of the 2016 flare.

sources.summary: The paper finds compact flare-emission regions in 3C 454.3, with log-parabolic spectra and November 2010 timing favoring BLR emission but December timing favoring MT emission.

quotes.quote: 3C 454.3 The variability timescales for 3C 454.3, the brightest of the sources, predict a compact emission region of size (3.87 0.84) 10^ 13 m for the December 2009 flare and (7.38 1.03) 10^ 13 m for the November 2010 flare. Both of these are compatible with emission from the inner regions of the BLR in a simple one-zone model. This conclusion is reinforced by evidence that the spectra of both flares favour a log parabolic model over a power law, particularly in the case of the November 2010 flare. This bright flare also provides sufficient statistics for a LCCF to be obtained; this shows no evidence for a time-lag between the high- and low-energy emission, again supporting a BLR origin of the emission. However, the LCCF for the December 2009 flare indicates the low energy flux is delayed with respect to the high energy flux with evidence of a time-lag of 11.5 3.3 hours.

sources.summary: The paper models 3C 454.3 as an FSRQ in quiescent and flaring phases, exploring synchrotron self-Compton fits, variability sizes, and required electron densities.

quotes.quote: For FSRQ, we have selected a quiescent and a flaring phase of 3C 454.3 from . In our conical jet model for a given ray flare time scale ( t_ var ) the emission region size L can be estimated as, L t_ var 1+z . We defined, L^o = t_ var /(1+z) as a maximal observable emission size. For flaring phase of 3C 454.3, Shah-etal2017 have reported a 10 hours ray variability, so L 5 10 ^ 14 cm.

Only SSC for FSRQ SEDs In figure fig:ssc3C454 , the left and right panel show a quiescent and flaring state SED of FSRQ 3C 454.3 from Shah-etal2017 respectively. For the quiescent state, the solid and dashed curves in panel (a) represent the model flux for R/L = 5 and 0.5 respectively while the bottom panel (b) represents model flux for R/L = 0.01. For the flaring state, the model SSC flux is computed for R/L = 0.01 (upper panel) and 0.0001 (bottom panel) and the corresponding size L = 8.3 10^ 15 cm and = 3.3 10^ 15 cm respectively at n_e = 10 ^ 16 cm ^ 3 (as listed in table tab:ssc3C454 ). In single scattering limit, R/L < 0.01 is preferred for both flaring and quiescent phase. To explain 10 hours ray variability (L 5 10 ^ 14 cm), the electron number density should be order of 10^ 17 cm ^ 3 , which is very large density even two order greater than estimated BLLs electron density and 4--5 order greater than EC dominated case.

sources.summary: The paper models 3C454.3 as a powerful gamma-ray-bright FSRQ with an accelerating, magnetically dominated jet and transition-region gamma-ray and synchrotron emission.

quotes.quote: We choose 3C454.3, a powerful FSRQ at redshift z=0.859 that is bright in gamma rays and has been observed in both flaring and quiescent states. Jet We model the jet within the Potter Cotter framework (PC, see ) as discussed in the context of ALP-photon mixing in Ref. And available within the gammaALPs PYTHON package, hosted on GitHub ( https://github.com/me-manu/gammaALPs ) and archived on Zenodo . See Ref. for an overview. . The structure of this jet is an accelerating, parabolic, magnetically dominated jet base which transitions to a decelerating conical ballistic jet in rough equipartion at around 10^5 r_g .

Within the PC model, the steady-state gamma-ray emission is strongly dominated by the transition region ( r_ vhe =59.8 pc for 3C454.3). A large portion of the synchrotron emission is also produced here, though synchrotron emission from the rest of the jet is also important.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 454.3 as a highly variable gamma-ray blazar whose apparent broadband QPO candidates are flare-driven and not significant after red-noise and trial-factor treatment.

quotes.quote: 3C 454.3 3C 454.3 is one of the most variable ray blazars, with its emission and variability dominated by bright flares in all wavelengths, as observed from Figure 3c454lc . These flares clearly appear between MJD 52000-55500 and MJD 56500-58500, with a quiescent period in between both epochs. Moreover, the observations performed before MJD 52000 do not present any major flare or variability, as shown by the optical R- and V-band LCs. Due to the presence of these flares, some potential QPOs appear in the first analysis step. However, the ZDCF, LS periodogram and WWZ do not show any significant period in the broadband emission of this blazar after considering both the red noise and the trial factors (see Figure 3c454_3_results ).

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes the 3C 454.3 repeating gamma-ray pattern, improves time-scale agreement for later events, and associates two radio knots with repetitions.

quotes.quote: We found 22 repeated intervals of the light curves of 8 blazars from our sample, including the patterns in J1256.1 0547 (3C 279) and J2253.9+1609 (3C 454.3) reported earlier (see section sec:intro ).

For J2253.9+1609, the pattern was identified by , where they were presented without any time scale modification. According to our analysis, adjusting the time scale by factors 0.44 and 1.478 in the second and the third events gives a better agreement between the curves.

For J2253.9+1609, two radio knots, K09 and K10 in Fig. fig:2253full , were presented in the same paper where the pattern in this source was discovered Jorstad2013 . Following their work, we associate K09 with the pattern repetition 1 and K10 with repetition 3.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2251+158 as 3C454.3 and reports optical polarization, day-scale variability, smaller K-band than optical variation, and correlated infrared colors.

quotes.quote: 2251+158, 3C454.3 , z=0.859 A polarization of P_ opt = 0 16.0 and m = 2.3 are reported in the paper of Angel Stockman (1980). A variability of 0.5 mag over a time scale of one day has also been observed in this object (Lloyd, 1984). Smith et al (1988) observed this object on 1986 Dec. 23, 1987 June 23 and 24 and found the optical polarization is less than 6 and the polarization is wavelength-dependent with dP/d > 0 . The light curve indicates a variation of 1.57 mag in K which is smaller than in the optical one. (J-K) is found to be correlated with (J-H) and (H-K) (see Fig. 15): (J-K) = 0.48 + 1.52 (J-H) with = 0.94 and p = 1 10^ 4 , (J-K) = -0.44 + 2.36 (H-K) with = 0.86 and p = 2.3 10^ 3 .

sources.summary: The paper cites 3C 454.3 as a luminous blazar whose multiwavelength variability and 2006 outburst support expectations for parsec-scale EC(HDR) emission zones.

quotes.quote: Hence, the characteristic variability time scale predicted by the EC(HDR) model is more consistent with the one implied by the observed variability patters in `optically violent variable' quasars, which is of the order of days/weeks (Pica et al. 1988) A nice example where such time scales are seen both in the ray and IR/optical frequency ranges is provided by multiwavelength observations of blazar 3C 454.3 (see Fig. 1 in Bonning et al. 2009). We note in this context that in some cases rapid, day/intraday flares are imposed on the observed light-curves of blazar sources. One example is the extremely rapid ( 2 hours) optical flare, as recorded also in 3C 454.3 (Raiteri et al. 2008), or intraday ray flare recorded by Fermi /LAT in the radio quasar PKS 1454-354 (Abdo et al. 2009a).

In contrast, in the EC(HDR) model the blazar zone extends up to the millimeter photosphere, and thus mm-band variations accompanying ray flares are expected, as in fact observed, e.g., during the 2006 outburst event in 3C 454.3 (Krichbaum et al. 2008).

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 454.3 as a blazar with large EVPA rotations, complex VLBI jet structure, and observed properties explainable by circular jet motion.

quotes.quote: Blazar 3C 454.3 also experiences large EVPA rotational events (see Table tb1 and Table tb2 ). Its jet shows complex VLBI structure e.g. 2005AJ....130.1418J with the indication of a very small angle of jet direction to the line of sight. This may explain complex overall behavior of its properties during both active and quiescent states. Considering the 3C 454.3 jet executing a circular motion, which in a sky projection turns into planar motion in some cases, our model may explain its observed overall properties: fast EVPA swings with the amplitude less than 180 ^ 2010PASJ...62..645S,2015MNRAS.453.1669B,2016MNRAS.457.2252B as well as large and continuous EVPA rotations 500 ^ and + 400 ^ , 2010PASJ...62..645S,2012PASJ...64...58S .

sources.summary: The paper selects 3C 454.3 as an extreme FSRQ, models disk external-Compton emission, finds low synchrotron flux in comparison with 3C 273, and places the emission region inside the BLR.

quotes.quote: We selected the six FSRQs (CTA 102, 3C 273, 3C 279, 3C 454.3, PKS 1510-089 PKS B1222+216) from the Fermi LAT monitored source list, also studied in 2019ApJ...877...39M , which are known for exhibiting brightest ray flares with average daily fluxes over 10^ 5 cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 within 1 statistical uncertainties above 100 MeV 2019ApJ...877...39M .

The contributions from EC emissions of Doppler de-boosted disk radiation ( EC_ disk ) were required in 3C 273 and 3C 454.3 SED, and SSC contribution was required in CTA 102 and PKS 1510-089 SED. Synchrotron emission flux from 3C 273 is the highest and that of 3C 454.3 is the lowest.
Emission regions for 3C 273 and 3C 454.3 were found to be inside BLR.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C 454.3 as a blazar with significant hardness-ratio variability, but its spectrum cannot be conclusively described by the simple monthly changing power-law model.

quotes.quote: We find that when using the HR plane method, we cannot conclusively describe the spectra of either 3C 454.3 or 1ES 0033+595 in the same way. We should note, however, that these sources are also different in that they show statistically significant variability for only two of the hardness ratios that we calculate, namely M L and H L . The fact that these are the two ratios that vary suggests that these spectra may require at least a changing broken power law with a pivot at 25-30 keV in order to adequately describe their variability. However, the BAT data do not have a high enough S/N to be able to be described by such a complex model, as compared to a simple power law.

sources.summary: The paper maps 3C 454.3 at 15 GHz, finding circular polarization in both the core and first jet component and comparing it with prior positive detections.

quotes.quote: 2251 + 158 (3C 454.3): Figure f:2251cp shows the first 4 milli-arcseconds of the jet of 3C 454.3 at 15 GHz in March of 2003. Positions where circular polarization measurements or limits were taken are marked and numbered on the Stokes I map. The first jet component (position 1) appears slightly more strongly circularly polarized, m_c = +0.38 0.10 , than the core, m_c = +0.23 0.10 ; however, an alternative calibration assuming no circular polarization for calibrating the phases (see s:phase ) spreads the circular polarization between the core and component 1 more evenly with both having m_c 0.3 . HAW01 measured m_c = +0.08 0.04 in the core of 3C 454.3 at 5 GHz in December of 1996, and the source was repeatedly detected with positive circular polarization at 8 GHz and below in the integrated historical measurements compiled by WdP83 and K84.

sources.summary: The core-dominated quasar 3C 454.3 is highly variable between PSPC observations and shows neither intraday variability nor extended X-ray emission in the selected data.

quotes.quote: 3C 454.3 This is a well-known highly variable core-dominated quasar. We report results from PSPC observations since the HRI exposures are all short. The count rates in a 2-arcmin source circle are significantly different in three separate PSPC observations, and so we have used the longest (from 1992 Dec 16 -- 1992 Dec 18), which gives a count rate consistent with that of Sambruna (1997). We find no evidence for intraday variability or extended X-ray emission.

sources.summary: The paper reports that 3C 454.3 was detected in circular polarization, had one polarized jet region in prior VLBI work, and shows core polarization consistent with the survey despite an extreme millimeter flare.

quotes.quote: Among our 8 sources with detected circular polarization, only 3C 84 (0316+413) and 3C 454.3 (2251+158) were also detected at 3 by Homan:2006p238 . They found significant circular polarization in the core and in 3 downstream jet emission regions in 3C 84, and in one jet region in 3C 454.3.

In contrast, both m_ C and its sign in the core of 3C 454.3 as measured by Homan Lister's, and our measurements are consistent, despite the extreme mm flare displayed by this source during our observations e.g., Raiteri:2008p123,Jorstad:subm .

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C454.3 as an exceptional distant blazar/AGN flare whose gamma output and possible UHECR association could motivate a Z-shower interpretation.

quotes.quote: In particular in last figure it is shown the last (second December 2009) dramatic bright gamma shining of the AGN 3C454.3 whose last flare at cosmic edge ( redshift z=0.859 well above half size Universe) and its eventual correlation might force us to a Z-Shower solution .

However the very exceptional blazar 3C454.3 flare on 2nd December 2009 , a month ago, and the few AGN connection of UHECR far from a GZK volume may force us, surprisingly, to reconsider an exceptional model: Z-Shower one.

Even for the minimal UHE Z-UHECR conversion as low as 10^ 4 (see table 1 ,last reference in ), even for a not clustered relic neutrino halo as diluted as cosmic ones the present gamma 3C454.3 output (above 3 10^ 48 erg s^ 1 ) is comparable with UHECR (two events in 4 years in AUGER), assuming a flat Fermi spectra (for neutrinos) extended up to UHECR ZeVs edges.

sources.summary: The paper cites 3C 454.3 multiwavelength flaring as an FSRQ case whose observed band correlations are consistent with injection-luminosity changes and delayed X-ray variability.

quotes.quote: Multi-wavelength observations of the FSRQ 3C 454.3 from August-December 2008 have shown pronounced flaring activity in the IR, UV, x-ray and ray bands with correlations for all bands except the x-rays Bonning09 . These correlations are consistent with a model in which a change in the injection luminosity of higher energy electrons takes place and interacts with external photons, causing the flaring observed in the rays Bonning09 . The much longer cooling time of the low-energy electrons responsible for the X-ray emission leads to much delayed variability, on much longer time scales compared to the optical and ray bands, which might be washed out by super-imposed longer-term variability.

sources.summary: The paper monitored 3C 454.3 for optical flux and colour variability across multiple timescales and reports intraday flux variations with amplitudes up to 31 percent.

quotes.quote: To search for optical variability on a wide range of timescales, we have carried out photometric monitoring of 3C 454.3, 3C 279 and S5 0716+714.

The key motivation of this work was to search for optical flux and colour variability of 3C 454.3, S5 0716+714 and 3C 279 on diverse time scales, including analyses of colour-magnitude variations, inter-band cross correlations, and optical/NIR SEDs.

The percentage variation on a given night is calculated by using the variability amplitude parameter A , introduced by Heidt Wagner (1996), During our 37 nights of monitoring for IDV we found flux variations, on intraday time-scales for 21 nights in B, V, R or I with amplitudes of variability up to 31 for 3C 454.3, 9.2 for 3C 279 and 13.5 for S5 0716+714.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 454.3 as an INTEGRAL-targeted flat-spectrum radio quasar whose 2005 multiwavelength state made it a testbed for economic jet models.

quotes.quote: 2015), the flat-spectrum radio quasars 3C 279, 3C 454.3, PKS 1502+106, and PKS 1510--089 were INTEGRAL targets in various occasions. Our collaboration observed 3C 454.3 ( z = 0.859 ) in May 2005 at many frequencies other than hard X-rays, and found a rather different multi-wavelength spectral shape with respect to an earlier epoch, although the total energy had not varied significantly (Pian et al. 2006). This made this source (which is also a strong AGILE source and TeV candidate, Vercellone et al. 2008) an excellent testbed for the "economic" jet model, whereby variations in individual bands can be conspicuous (larger than a factor of 10), with the total energy injected in the emitting region remaining however approximately constant (Katarzy nski Ghisellini 2007; Ghisellini et al.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 454.3 as a source whose four XMM-Newton observations are power-law dominated, with spectral indices of 1.5--1.7.

quotes.quote: 3C 454.3: This source is studied by Donato et al. (2005) and found that it is well described by simple power law with =1.34. XMM Newton observations are analyzed by Raiteri et al. (2007); 2008 in its post outburst and outburst states, respectively. We analysed four observations of XMM Newton and found that all are well described by the power law model. The spectral indices varies between 1.5--1.7 which is in accordsnce with the previous findings.

sources.summary: The paper studies 3C 454.3 as a variable flat-spectrum blazar/quasar, applying WTMM multifractality analysis to its 22 and 37 GHz radio observations.

quotes.quote: The quasar 3C 454.3 is an HPQ 2011A&A...532A.146L at z = 0.859 1996MNRAS.279..429N with a flat spectrum that belongs to the blazar class of active galactic nuclei 2006A&A...453..817V , which has been studied across the electromagnetic spectrum 2010ApJ...715..362J and known as variable source at all wavelengths. This quasar has been monitored by Mets a hovi Radio Observatory at 22 and 37 GHz for 24 and 38 years, respectively.

In this work, we apply a wavelet transform-based multifractality analysis approach called wavelet transform modulus maxima (hereinafter WTMM) and study the multiscaling or multifractal behavior of the quasars 3C 273, 3C 279, 3C 345, and 3C 454.3 radio observations at 22 and 37 GHz.

sources.summary: The paper singles out 3C 454.3 as the brightest high-latitude extragalactic Fermi-LAT source and the only selected blazar whose 4FGL spectrum is fitted with the cutoff form.

quotes.quote: The ``Cutoff'' form (a power--law with a super--exponential cutoff) is used in the 4FGL to fit the spectra of 220 sources (218 Pulsars, the Small Magellanic Cloud, and the blazar 3C 454.3).
Only one of these sources (the blazar 3C 454.3, the brightest object in this sky region) is fitted with the ``Cutoff' form.

As an illustration, ranking in total flux the extragalactic sources, after 3C 454.3 the next 86 objects are all fitted with the log--parabola form, before finding the first source fitted with a simple power--law form (therefore in Fig. fig:gamma_resolved all the best fits lines are curved).

sources.summary: The paper checks a reported 47-day QPO in 3C 454.3, but its CWT maps show no evidence for that feature even with 7-day binning.

quotes.quote: 3C 454.3: claimed a >4 QPO at period 47 d analysing the 1-day binned light curve of 3C 454.3, lasting from MJD 56800 to MJD 57250. We can observe in our CWT maps of this source that there is a strong vertical signal before MJD 56000 and no further significant feature appears afterwards. Thus, our CWT maps of 3C 454.3 show no evidence of such a QPO even for the 7-days binned light curve.

sources.summary: The paper calls 2251+158 its best periodicity case, with strong consistent signals at all monitored frequencies and residuals suggesting an additional impulsive luminosity-variation component.

quotes.quote: As shown by Fig. A2251_1 , 2251+158 is probably our best case. Strong and consistent periodic signals are detected at all frequencies, most clearly at 22 , 4.8 and 8.0 GHz. At all frequencies, however, an inspection of the the residuals obtained by subtracting the first harmonic from the observed data, evidences an additional, impulsive, source of luminosity variations, over-imposed on the underlying periodic component.

gmw_00000650.name: B2 0258+35
gmw_00000650.overview: The massive lenticular galaxy NGC 1167 hosts the young, low-luminosity compact steep-spectrum radio source B2 0258+35 [arXiv:1908.00374, arXiv:2405.13471]. This Seyfert 2 galaxy features a giant, regularly rotating neutral hydrogen disk [arXiv:1503.01836, arXiv:2202.05222] with minimal, peripheral star formation [arXiv:2405.13471] and diffuse radio lobes from recurrent active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:1807.07280, arXiv:2202.05222].

sources.summary: The paper studies B2 0258+35 as a CSS radio source in NGC 1167, surrounded by diffuse lobes whose unexpectedly non-steep spectrum suggests remnant or ongoing fueled activity.

quotes.quote: In the context of restarting radio galaxies the CSS source B2 0258+35 has revealed a very intriguing story. The radio source has a power equal to 10^ 24.37 \ W \ Hz^ 1 at 408 MHz ( ) and it is associated to the field early-type galaxy NGC 1167 (z=0.0165) which is optically classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy ( ). Its radio morphology was first studied by using the VLA and MERLIN+EVN at 5 and 1.6 GHz respectively. They classified it as a CSS source even though the high frequency spectrum is not very steep = 0.54 .

The morphology of the extended emission as seen by LOFAR at 145 MHz reflects what was previously observed at 1.4 GHz by (see Fig. fig:map ). The linear extension of the lobes at low frequency is in agreement with the high frequencies and the surface brightness has an average value of 4.7 mJy arcmin ^ 2 . The two broad enhancements in surface brightness observed in the Northern and Southern direction are clearly visible at both high and low frequencies but we cannot interpret univocally their origin. The extended emission that we see has likely been originated by large-scale jets that are currently switched off or smothered.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1167 as a face-on fast rotator with extended UV, neutral, molecular, and warm ionized gas, central AGN-like line ratios, and residual star formation.

quotes.quote: The MASSIVE fast rotator NGC 1167 represents an interesting case of both gas rotation near the center of the galaxy and a patchy distribution of gas at larger galactocentric radius. The galaxy itself is likely a face-on disk, and exhibits extended UV emission in images taken with the Galaxy Evolution Explorer GALEX ; martin05 . Interestingly, struve10 detected a 160 kpc disk of neutral hydrogen in NGC 1167 and suggested satellite accretion as its origin. In total, the warm ionized gas in NGC 1167 reaches roughly 18 kpc, but rotation is evident in the gas only within about 10 kpc. Since gas is detected in the outermost bins for NGC 1167 and there might be more detectable emission outside the field of view of the Mitchell Spectrograph, our R_ gas can be considered a lower limit for this galaxy.

We see evidence of an AGN based on the [ O 3 ] /H line ratio, and our detection of [ O 2 ] in binned fibers at large galactocentric radius hints at excitation by H 2 regions. As part of the CALIFA survey, gomes16 found clear evidence of localized H clumps at large galactocentric radii (see their Figure 1), which they attribute to low-level ongoing star formation. They also comment on the patchiness of the gas by classifying NGC 1167 as having a ``perturbed" rotational pattern (see their Table 1).

sources.summary: The paper examines B2 0258+35 as a restarted radio galaxy in NGC 1167, finding faint outer lobes whose spectra argue against very old remnants and imply continued fueling or recent jet switch-off.

quotes.quote: In this work we investigate the duty cycle of the radio galaxy B2 0258+35, which was previously suggested to be a restarted radio galaxy based on its morphology. The radio source consists of a pair of kpc-scale jets embedded in two large-scale lobes ( 240 kpc) with relaxed shape and very low surface brightness, which resemble remnants of a past AGN activity. We have combined new LOFAR data at 145 MHz and new Sardinia Radio Telescope data at 6600 MHz with available WSRT data at 1400 MHz to investigate the spectral properties of the outer lobes and derive their age.

Interestingly, the spectrum of both the outer northern and southern lobes is not ultra-steep as expected for an old ageing plasma with spectral index values equal to _ 1400 ^ 145 =0.48 0.11 and _ 6600 ^ 1400 =0.69 0.20 in the outer northern lobe, and _ 1400 ^ 145 =0.73 0.07 in the outer southern lobe. Moreover, despite the wide frequency coverage available for the outer northern lobe (145-6600 MHz), we do not identify a significant spectral curvature (SPC 0.2 0.2 ). While mechanisms such as in-situ particle reacceleration, mixing or compression can temporarily play a role in preventing the spectrum from steepening, in no case seem the outer lobes to be compatible with being very old remnants of past activity as previously suggested (with age 80 Myr). We conclude that either the large-scale lobes are still fuelled by the nuclear engine or the jets have switched off no more than a few tens of Myr ago, allowing us to observe both the inner and outer structure simultaneously.
The radio source B2 0258+35 is hosted by the giant early-type galaxy NGC 1167 ( M_B=-21.7 \ mag, \ D_ 25 = 56 \ kpc ), which is optically classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy ho1997 .

sources.summary: The paper studies B2 0258+35 as a young, low-luminosity compact steep-spectrum radio source in NGC 1167 whose jets disturb turbulent cold gas and shock ionized gas in the host ISM.

quotes.quote: We report high-resolution Very Large Array (VLA) and European VLBI Network (EVN) observations of absorption from a young, compact steep-spectrum radio source, B2 0258+35, nested in the early-type galaxy NGC 1167, which contains a 160 kpc disc. Our VLA and EVN \ absorption observations, modelling, and comparison with molecular gas data suggest that the cold gas in the centre of NGC 1167 is very turbulent (with a velocity dispersion of 90 ) and that this turbulence is induced by the interaction of the jets with the interstellar medium (ISM). Furthermore, the ionised gas in the galaxy shows evidence of shock heating at a few kpc from the radio source. These findings support the results from numerical simulations of radio jets expanding into a clumpy gas disc, which predict that the radio jets in this case percolate through the gas disc and drive shocks into the ISM at distances much larger than their physical extent.

We present here one such study of a nearby z = 0.0165; Wegner93 , low-luminosity L _ 1.4 GHz = 2.1 10 ^ 23 W Hz ^ 1 ; Shulevski12 radio source: B2 0258+35. This source is nested in a gas-rich galaxy, NGC 1167, and appears to be interacting strongly with the ISM. The galactic is well studied Struve10c and so are the properties of the radio source Sanghera95, Giroletti05, Brienza18 . This radio galaxy is thus a good candidate to understand the nature of jet--ISM interactions and the possible impact the radio source has on the ambient medium. is classified as a compact steep-spectrum (CSS) source Sanghera95 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1167 as a gas-rich, massive lenticular disk galaxy with low HI surface density, little star formation, an extended rotation curve, and unusually thin smooth spiral arcs.

quotes.quote: NGC 1167. This galaxy contains a substantial amount of HI . According to the observations of Noordermeer et al.  [ 14 ] , the total HI mass is 1.7 10^ 10   M_ , but the gas is distributed over a very large area, so that the average surface density HI within the optical radius R_ 25 is less than 2   M_ / pc ^2 . The gas density remains below the critical value required for gravitational instability of the gaseous layer at all galactocentric distances , explaining the lack of observed star-forming regions in the galaxy. HI observations show an extended rotation curve, which slowly decreases after reaching its maximum and extends over 10 radial disk scales [ 5, 6 ] . The maximum rotational velocity of the disk is almost 400 km/s, making this one of the most rapidly rotating and massive disk galaxies known.

The images taken with the 6-m telescope of the Special Astrophysical Observatory reveal a system of ring-like arcs or spirals, which were also noted by Noordermeer  [ 5 ] . The spirals are remarkably thin and smooth, without characteristic irregularities and bright knots (usually due to local star-forming regions in the galaxy's spiral arms), suggesting a lack of O stars in these structures.

sources.summary: The paper describes B2 0258+35 as a young radio source in NGC 1167, with old stellar populations, a large H I disk, accreting halo gas, and circumnuclear absorption.

quotes.quote: B2 0258+35 is a young radio source 10^6 yr, giroletti05 with a two-lobes structure extended about 1 kpc. The stellar population contains old plus intermediate age ( >4 Gyr) stars, but does not have a young component emonts06 , suggesting that the host galaxy NGC 1167 is not a recent merger. Therefore, the radio source is unlikely to be triggered by a merging event.

The large, regular rotating disk (which is not warped) further confirms that NGC 1167 is an old merger but the presence of halo gas suggests that ``cold accretion'' from IGM may be occurring, and could perhaps be related to the triggering of the AGN. Against the radio source (unresolved in the WSRT observations) fairly symmetric blue- and redshifted absorption is found, v_ absorp > 400 (Fig. 0258 , Struve et al. in prep.). Unlike for Cen A, the continuum structure of B2 0258+35 is extended around the nucleus on the arcsecond scale in all directions and not only along the jet axis giroletti05 . Hence, the data suggests that the absorption is caused by a cold, circumnuclear disk.

sources.summary: The appendix describes B2 0258+35 as a CSS radio source with a 160 kpc rotating H I disc, asymmetric outer gas, absorption from the disc, and a bulge plus faint spiral disc host.

quotes.quote: B2 0258+35: The \ results of this source will be published in detail in a forthcoming paper by Struve et al. (in prep.). The \ emission-line gas around B2 0258+35 is distributed in a regularly rotating disc with a diameter of 160 kpc see for a position-velocity plot str08_0258 . A slight asymmetry appears in the \ gas towards the outer western part of the otherwise settled disc. It is likely that the bulk of the absorbing \ gas is located in the large-scale disc, although part of it could also come from a circum-nuclear disc see str08_0258 .

The optical host galaxy has a large bulge component and what appears to be a very faint and tightly wound spiral disc. Our \ and optical data are in good agreement with earlier data presented by noo05 . The radio source in B2 0258+35 has been classified as Compact Steep Spectrum CSS; san95 .

sources.summary: The paper describes B2 0258+35 as a young CSS radio galaxy in NGC 1167 with faint relic emission, recurrent activity, and gas-fueling likely tied to hot-halo cooling.

quotes.quote: Outlined is the discovery of a very faint, diffuse, low surface-brightness (0.5 , 1.4 on average) structure around the radio source B2 0258+35 hosted by an HI-rich early-type galaxy (NGC 1167). Since B2 0258+35 is a young Compact Steep Spectrum (CSS) source, the newly discovered structure could represent a remnant from an earlier stage of AGN activity. We go on by explaining in detail all the possibilities for triggering the radio activity in B2 0258+35 regarding gas accretion in a recurrent AGN activity framework.

NGC 1167 hosts a very regular, extended and massive \ disc that has been studied in great detail. It has regular kinematics on large scales, which, together with stellar population studies of NGC 1167, exclude the possibility of a recent merger as the trigger for the current AGN activity responsible for the CSS source. Previous studies of the \ closer to the core seem to go against the assumption of a circum-nuclear disc of \ as the source of the accreting gas. We consider the cooling of gas from the hot, X-ray halo as a possible alternative option for the fueling of the AGN, as suggested in the case of other sources of similar radio power as B2 0258+35.

sources.summary: The paper studies B2 0258+35 as an active radio galaxy whose lobes show unique S-shaped polarized structures, depolarization, low magnetic fields, and possible magnetic draping or compression.

quotes.quote: Using the source B2 0258+35 we want to investigate the interaction of its radio lobes with the surrounding environment and examine the underlying physical effects. Published H i and radio continuum data at 21 cm were combined with newly reduced archival Westerbork Radio Synthesis Telescope polarisation data at the same wavelength to investigate the polarised emission in the radio lobes of B2 0258+35. We assumed energy equipartition between the cosmic rays and the magnetic field to calculate their pressure and investigate the physical processes leading to the detected emission. We detected a unique S-shaped diffuse polarised structure.

We observe depolarisation in the northern lobe, which might originate from the H i disc in the foreground. In addition we see an anti-correlation between the pressure and the fractional polarisation along the S-shaped structure. Therefore we consider magnetic draping and magnetic field compression as possible effects that might have created the observed S-shape.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1167 as a gas-rich early-type host of a low-luminosity radio AGN, with disturbed inner gas, regular large-scale rotation, and recurrent radio activity.

quotes.quote: The central radio source is nested in NGC 1167 ( z= 0.0165 We assume a flat Universe with H_0 = 67.3 \ Mpc 1 , _ = 0.685, and _ M = 0.315 for all our calculations. At z=0.0165 , 1 '' corresponds to 0.349 kpc. ), a gas-rich, massive early-type galaxy and the optical AGN in NGC 1167 is found to be radiatively inefficient . It has been suggested that the cold gas in the inner few kiloparsecs of the galaxy shows signatures of disturbed kinematics . Furthermore, the diffuse large-scale lobes are caused by a previous phase of activity and indicate that \ is a radio source which has undergone multiple episodes of activity .

The host galaxy, NGC 1167, is a gas-rich ( M_ = 1.5 10^ 10 ) early-type galaxy, with a 160 kpc diameter regularly-rotating \ disc. The disc has very regular kinematics within a radius of 65 kpc, and shows signs of interactions, perhaps with a satellite gaalxy, only in the very outer parts. This suggests that the galaxy has not undergone a major merger in the last few billion years .

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC1167 has nearly flat stellar metallicity, possible Seyfert-like bulge ionisation, no recent disc star formation, and a disc possibly formed from merger remnant gas.

quotes.quote: Both the bulge and disc of NGC1167 show a nearly constant stellar metallicity radial profile over the whole extension of the galaxy, i.e., even beyond the effective radius of the disc (blue lines in Figure fig:metallicity ). This trend is also independent of the metallicity being light or mass weighted. We note that NGC1167 was photometrically classified as unknown, i.e., the presence of a disc cannot be assured statistically, and therefore this result might be interpreted as a lack of such a disc.
We found that the bulge spaxels in NGC1167 also show high values of the EW H , therefore confirming the Seyfert nature of the ionisation.

The analysis of the ionised gas in the BPT diagram (Fig. fig:BPT ) shows that NGC1167 has no recent star formation in the disc, reinforcing the idea that it was formed from the remnant gas just after the merger leading to the bulge formation.

sources.summary: The paper flags NGC1167 as a Seyfert-2 source with low X-ray-to-[OIII] flux, low fitted absorption, and possible hard-energy flattening suggesting a buried or reflected component.

quotes.quote: There are also 5 Seyfert-2 galaxies (NGC676, NGC1167, NGC3254, NGC6951 and NGC7743) occupying the low F_ 2-10 keV /F_ [ OIII ] regime. We do not consider NGC4169 because of the large error in the estimation of the [ OIII ] flux (see Ho et al. 1997). These, according to the expected N_H F_ 2-10 keV /F_ [ OIII ] relation, should present high values of N_H . According to Fig. oiii , the minimum N_H value is 2 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 for NGC676, 6 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 for NGC6951 and 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 for NGC1167 and NGC7743. However, the X-ray spectral fittings show low absorption ( <10^ 22 cm ^ 2 ) while in addition there is no indication for a flat photon index or strong FeK _ line.

Note however, that in the spectrum of at least two sources (NGC1167 and NGC7743) there is some indication for a flattening at hard energies which could suggest a heavily buried or reflected component.

sources.summary: The paper presents B2 0258+35 as a low-power jet case with turbulent circumnuclear molecular gas and a fast outflow comprising most nuclear gas, rapidly depleting the reservoir.

quotes.quote: The NOEMA CO(1-0) observations of B2 0258+35 ( L _ 1.4 GHz =2.1 10 ^ 23 W Hz^ 1 ; Murthy et al. \ submitted) show a spectacular example of this and further confirms the predictions from simulations. We detect a highly turbulent molecular circumnuclear structure, where a fast (FWHM 350 km s ^ 1 ) jet-driven outflow of 2.6 10^6 \ is observed. This outflow comprises of 75 of the total gas in the nuclear region. Also in this case, the jet will deplete the kpc-scale molecular gas reservoir on a relatively short time scale (i.e. \ within 2 10^6 yr).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC1167 as an eCALIFA example S0 galaxy with an AGN, mostly quenched outskirts-only star formation, a central PCA peak, and visible rotation.

quotes.quote: IC391 and IC1151 are both spiral galaxies exhibiting SF, while NGC1167 is classified as an S0 galaxy with an AGN. In the PCA 0 maps, SF clumps are prominent features for IC391 and IC1151. Notably, IC391 exhibits more intense SF events compared to IC1151. In contrast, NGC1167 displays minimal SF, primarily at the periphery, and is predominantly quenched.

However, an intensity peak at the centre of NGC1167 in the PCA 1 map signals the presence of the AGN. Turning to PCA 3, which represents gas kinematics, we observe the rotational motion of the galaxies in IC391 and NGC1167.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1167 as a lenticular galaxy in a rich environment, hosting an obscured active nucleus with sub-parsec jets and an old central stellar population.

quotes.quote: NGC 1167 (S2) This lenticular galaxy resides in a rich environment, with several companions within a projected distance of 350 kpc Struve10 . The galaxy hosts an active nucleus with sub-parsec scale jets Nagar05,Giovannini01 , suspected to be obscured by a Compton-thick column of material Panessa06,Akylas09 . The stellar population within the half-light radius is fairly old, with a luminosity-weighted mean age of 6 Gyr GonzalezDelgado14 .

sources.summary: The paper describes B2 0258+35 as a low-luminosity radio source whose broad absorption likely traces jet-disturbed gas and turbulence rather than a fast outflow.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:fig2 shows the \ absorption detected against the kpc-scale jet of the low-luminosity radio source B2 0258+35. The width of the absorption ( 400 ) is too large to be explained by the rotation of the large scale \ disk known to be present in this object. The most likely hypothesis is that the jet enters the disk and, being trapped there, disturbs the kinematics of the gas without being able to produce a fast outflow, but injects energy increases the turbulence of the gas (Murthy et al. \ in prep).

gmw_00000651.name: SDSS J222648.38+170852.2
gmw_00000651.overview: SDSS J222648.38+170852.2 is a Balmer-strong absorption galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.1089 [arXiv:1904.10571]. It has a stellar mass of approximately 10^9.3 solar masses and hosted the nuclear transient AT2017eqx [arXiv:1904.10571].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J222648.38+170852.2 as the nuclear host of AT2017eqx, measures its redshift, and characterizes it as a faint Balmer-strong TDE host.

quotes.quote: The source was coincident with the centre of a galaxy catalogued in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey as SDSS J222648.38+170852.2, with an apparent magnitude of g=20.99 mag in Data Release 14 Abolfathi2018 . We determined the offset of AT2017eqx from the nucleus of this galaxy using a deep g band image of the transient obtained with LDSS3 on 2017-07-22 and an archival pre-disruption image from PanSTARRS DR1 Flewelling2016 . After transforming to a common coordinate grid and measuring the centroids of the transient and host galaxy, we find a relative offset of 0.06'' 0.08'' . Thus the origin of the source is fully consistent with the nucleus of the galaxy.

We also detect narrow absorption lines from the host galaxy, from which we measure the redshift z=0.1089 0.0005 . The host is a Balmer-strong absorption galaxy -- a rare type of galaxy that is greatly over-represented among TDE hosts Arcavi2014,French2016,LawSmith2017,Graur2018 .
This SMBH mass is consistent with the observed properties of the host galaxy, with SED fitting indicating a host stellar mass 10^ 9.3 .

gmw_00000652.name: Sextans A
gmw_00000652.overview: Sextans A is a low-surface-brightness dwarf irregular galaxy located at the edge of the Local Group [arXiv:2210.07726, arXiv:2403.10597]. It belongs to the NGC 3109 association [arXiv:2210.07726, arXiv:1712.06594] and has experienced a bursty star-formation history, including an increase in activity over the last 60 million years [arXiv:2210.07726, arXiv:2403.10597].

sources.summary: The LITTLE THINGS paper flags DDO 75 as difficult to image because of multiple uv datasets, striping, pinholes, and cleaning choices, while its VLA and single-aperture profiles agree.

quotes.quote: We had particular problems imaging DDO 75. There were 8 uv data sets including AB (when the configuration is in transition from A to B), B, C, and D array configurations. Six of the data sets were archival and two were obtained under project AO 215. The moment maps have low level diagonal striping, and the integrated \ maps have individual blanked pixels (``pinholes'') where there should be flux. The increased noise from the striping caused the algorithm that produced the moment maps ( xmom ) to calculate unreasonable velocity values, and so those pixels were blanked in the moment map.

We tried to identify the cause of the striping, but it appears at such a low level in the uv data that we could not isolate and remove it. We concluded by cleaning to 3 rms since cleaning to 2 rms looked overcleaned and produced many more pinholes. Information about the robust weighted and Natural-weighted map cubes are given in Table tab-obs , along with the channel separation of the observations and the final number of frequency channels in the cube. For each cube we give the beam full-width at half of maximum (FWHM) along the major and minor axes, the beam position angle, and the rms in the maps. The rms in a single channel varies from 0.46 to 1.1 mJy beam ^ 1 in our sample. The pixel size of the cubes is 1.5 , except for DDO 216 and SagDIG, which were observed only in C and D array configurations and were mapped with 3.5 \ pixels because of their significantly larger beam. The map sizes are usually 1024 1024 pixels. A few particularly large galaxies on the sky or galaxies with nearby Galactic emission were imaged at 2048 2048 pixels. For each galaxy we have measured the total mass in , determined the \ flux-velocity profile, and measured the \ surface density profile. These basic analysis products are presented here. To make these measurements, we used the Groningen Image Processing System (GIPSY, Vogelaar Terlouw 2001). The cube channel map units are in Jy beam ^ 1 . This is related to brightness temperature in degrees Kelvin through S( Jy beam^ 1 ) = 1.65 10^ 6 T_B (K) (Brinks, private communication), where and are the major and minor axis FWHM in arcseconds given in Table tab-obs . The integrated \ map is obtained after converting the channel maps to units of K km s ^ 1 using the above relation and summing along the velocity axis. The column density becomes N_ HI ( atoms cm^ 2 ) = 1.823 10^ 18 T( K ) V ( km \ s^ 1 ), which becomes N_ HI ( atoms cm^ 2 ) = 1.105 10^ 21 S( Jy beam^ 1 m s^ 1 ) / ( ) (Hibbard \ 2001). The \ mass profile is obtained by integrating the flux in each channel map over the area containing \ emission and dividing by the number of pixels per beam area to arrive at S expressed in Jy. The conversion to total mass is then M_ HI ( M_ ) = 235.6 D( Mpc )^2 S ( Jy ) V ( km \ s^ 1 ). The factors for converting fluxes to masses for our robust weighted integrated \ maps are given in Table tab-masses . HI masses To measure the total \ mass, we summed the flux over the primary-beam corrected integrated \ map made from both the Natural-weighted image cube and the robust weighted cube ( flux ). Since the integrated \ maps had been constructed from the blanked cubes and pixels without emission had values of zero, the flux was summed over the entire image. The flux was then converted to mass. The masses from the two maps agree well. The average of the absolute value of the differences between the masses measured from the robust weighted maps and those measured from the Natural-weighted maps is 11 , with the robust weighted masses often higher than the Natural-weighted masses. The fluxes and masses from the robust weighted data are given in Table tab-masses , and compared to single-dish measurements in Figure fig-mass . The single-dish values are taken from Bottinelli \ (1982), Schneider \ (1990; DDO 101), Huchtmeier \ (2003; DDO 53, DDO 216, F564-V3), and Springob \ (2005; CVnIdwA). Our VLA \ masses agree within 11 with those measured with single dish telescopes. However, there is some bias towards VLA interferometer masses being slightly lower than those measured with single-dish telescopes. One can see this in Figure fig-mass by comparing the best fit line to the data in red with the line of equal values in black. Velocity profiles and Single-dish comparison In general, we find good agreement between the VLA and single-dish measurements in the literature. We used spectra from the NASA Extragalactic Database, when available, and the telescopes that the single-dish measurements come from are given in Table tab-velprofilecomp . When comparing velocity profiles, we use our VLA robust maps as they generally recover more flux than the Natural-weighted maps, which were cleaned to 2.5 rms rather than 2 rms. The profiles are shown in Figure fig-velprof and details of the comparison of individual galaxies are listed in Table tab-velprofilecomp . Galaxies that show no significant difference between the interferometer and single-aperture measurements include CVnI dwA, DDO 43, DDO 46, DDO 47, DDO 52, DDO 70, DDO 75, DDO 133, DDO 155, DDO 210, DDO 216, SagDIG, and VII Zw 403.

sources.summary: The paper compares resolved and integrated Sextans A measurements, finding lower integrated H I surface density than star-forming-region values and significant dust-derived gas surface densities.

quotes.quote: Figure 1a contains the SFR surface density as a function of the total (solid symbols) or HI (open symbols) gas surface density for the auxiliary data (Sect. 2.2), plus the resolved observations for the XMPs Sextans A (red open and solid squares; Shi et al. 2014) and ESO 146-G14 (red open and solid circles; Shi et al. 2014), and the integrated measurements for the XMPs also contained in the DGS: SBS 0335-052, IZw 18 and VII Zw 403 (encircled brown open and solid triangles; Cormier et al. 2014). Figure 1b contains the SFR surface density as a function of the HI gas surface density, integrated over the optical radii, for the 43 sample XMP galaxies (blue open circles; Sect 2.1), plus the integrated values for the XMPs Sextans A (red open square; Sect. 2.1) and ESO 146-G14 (red open circle; Sect. 2.1), and for the metal-poor dIrr WLM (dark green open triangle; Sect. 2.2).

The adopted total HI masses of log(M _ HI /M _ ) 7.8 and 9.0 for Sextans A and ESO 146-G14, respectively (Filho et al. 2013 and references therein), are similar to the values quoted by Shi et al. (2014) for the disks of these two sources. However, the HI surface density is about an order of magnitude lower than the values provided for the individual star-forming regions (Shi et al. 2014). Similarly, the total HI mass for WLM (log(M _ HI /M _ ) 7.8; Hunter, Elmegreen Ludka 2010) is about two orders of magnitude above the estimated HI mass for the Northwestern component (Elmegreen et al. 2013). By averaging over the whole galaxy, although large-scale HI gas and HI gas from diffuse inter-star-forming regions are included, a significant part of the HI gas may be clumpy and concentrated in the star-forming regions. Hence, lower (by approximately an order of magnitude) integrated _ HI values (red open square and circle, and dark green open triangle, respectively; Fig. 1b) are obtained relative to the individual star-forming region values (red open squares and circles, and dark green open triangle, respectively; Fig. 1a).

The dust-derived total gas surface densities for Sextans A and ESO 146-G14 are high (Shi et al. 2014). Indeed, the disks of Sextans A and ESO 146-G14 (M _ dust /M _ 10 ^4 and 6 10 ^5 , respectively; Shi et al. 2014) and individual star-forming regions (M _ dust /M _ 1 -- 2 10 ^3 and 8 10 ^4 - 3 10 ^5 , respectively; Shi et al. 2014) contain significant dust masses, that exceed the total dust mass (M _ dust /M _ 450 -- 1800) determined in the prototypical XMP, IZw 18 (Fisher et al. 2014; Hunt et al. 2014).

sources.summary: The paper presents near-infrared observations of Sextans A, describing it as a metal-poor dwarf irregular with old and young populations, recent enhanced star formation, and dusty evolved stars.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: RA = 10 h 08 m 29 s .5 and Dec = -0^ 26 m 45 s for Sextans A

quotes.quote: Sextans A (DDO 75) is an extremely metal-poor ( [ Fe/H ] 1.85 to 1.40 ; Dolphin2003b ), dwarf irregular galaxy. Located at a distance of 1.45 Mpc McConnachie2012 , it is a member of the NGC 3109 association vandenBerghBook , which may or may not be bound to the Local Group. Photometric studies of Sextans A reveal that the galaxy contains an old stellar population (which formed 10-14 Gyrs ago), and a younger blue population which formed during the past 2.5 Gyr DohmPalmer2002 . The star formation rate of Sextans A has increased dramatically in the past few Gyr; from a quiescent period between 2.5 and 10 Gyr ago, to a sudden burst in star-formation starting 1--2.5 Gyr ago, with another considerable increase over the past 0.06 Gyr Dolphin2003b, Weisz2014, Camacho2016 . There has been little chemical enrichment within Sextans A over the last 10 Gyr: Sextans A is one of the lowest-metallicity objects in which variable AGB stars have been detected Boyer2015b, McQuinn2017 .

We identify 32 stars in Leo A and 101 stars in Sextans A with a DPR >10^ 11 M_ yr ^ 1 , confirming that metal-poor stars can form substantial amounts of dust. We also find tentative evidence for oxygen-rich dust formation at low metallicity, contradicting previous models that suggest oxygen-rich dust production is inhibited in metal-poor environments. The total rates of dust injection into the interstellar medium of Leo A and Sextans A are (8.2 1.8) 10^ 9 M_ yr ^ 1 and (6.2 0.2) 10^ 7 M_ yr ^ 1 , respectively.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Sextans A as a Local Group dIrr galaxy; KAT-7 finds smaller H i extent and flux than Effelsberg, a declining rotation curve, and a pseudo-isothermal dark halo fit.

quotes.quote: Sextans A (DDO 75, UGC 205) is a dIrr galaxy at a distance of 1.3 Mpc 1991rc3..book.....D , which places it in the Local Group of galaxies, lying between Sextans B and NGC 3109. Sextans A is classified as an IBm galaxy 1991rc3..book.....D , having an absolute magnitude of M _ V 14 2005AJ....130.1558K . The stellar distribution in Sextans A has been observed to have a square-like distribution 1996ApJ...462..732H .

We derive H i extends of 30 ^ and 20 ^ and total H i fluxes of 181 2.0 Jy.km.s ^ 1 and 105 1.4 Jy.km.s ^ 1 for Sextans A and B respectively. This result shows clearly the overestimate of the H i extent and total flux of 54 ^ and 264 Jy.km.s ^ 1 reported for Sextans A using the Effelsberg observations. Tilted ring models allow us to derive the rotation curves (RCs) of Sextans A and B out to 550 ^ ( 3.5 kpc) and 650 ^ ( 4 kpc) respectively. The RCs of the two galaxies are seen to decline in the outer parts. The dark matter distribution in Sextans A is better described by the pseudo-isothermal halo model when a M/L ratio of 0.2 is used.

sources.summary: The paper describes Sextans A as a low-surface-brightness dwarf irregular with a peculiar shape, measured distance, and bursty star-formation history traced by its stellar populations.

quotes.quote: This galaxy is seen nearly face-on with an inclination of i 36^o Skillman1988 . used Cepheids, red clump stars, and the tip of the red giant branch (RGB) to measure the distance, which was derived to be 1.32 0.04 Mpc. Later this value was updated by to 1.42 0.08 Mpc. This value puts Sextans A right at the edge of the Local Group.

Studies of existing stellar populations in Sextans A show that the star formation (SF) was not continuous but bursty. It had a significant SF in ancient times, >5-10 Gyr ago, with a break at intermediate ages (1 - 5 Gyr ago), and a bursty episode that started between 1 and 2.5 Gyr ago Mateo1998 . It has continuously formed stars since then. In particular, the SF rate over the past 0.06 Gyr is a factor of 20 larger than the average over the whole lifetime of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper presents NTT spectroscopy for Sextans A, deriving HII-region abundances, finding homogeneous current metallicity, and identifying a Type I planetary nebula whose abundances imply lower past ISM metallicity.

quotes.quote: Sextans A is a dIrr at a distance of 1.32 Mpc for which deep HST images have permitted the measurements of stars to a limiting absolute magnitude of M_ V +1.9 . The gas abundance of heavy elements for Sextans A was first determined by . This study presented only an empirical estimate of O/H, which has a characteristic rms uncertainty of 50 . While this estimate was successfully used in various statistical studies to combine the data on O/H with the CMD-derived SF history and metallicities, such accuracy is not sufficient. We have derived an average value of 12+log(O/H)=7.57 from three HII regions in Sextans A.

Chemical abundances derived for the PN in Sextans A show that this is a Type I object (see Figure 1), with a highly elevated nitrogen abundance: N/O 2. Its 12+log(O/H) = 8.02 is about a factor of 3 higher than in the HII regions, which implies significant self-pollution by the PN progenitor. Since the abundances of S and Ar should not be altered in a PN progenitor (see discussion above), their values indicate that the ISM metallicity was 0.5 dex lower at the epoch of the PN progenitor's formation, compared to the current metallicity in HII regions and A-supergiants.

sources.summary: The paper treats Sextans A as a dwarf irregular galaxy, measuring H II-region and planetary-nebula abundances and linking its metallicity and star-formation history to recent chemical enrichment.

quotes.quote: In Sextans A we obtained the element abundances in its only known PN and in three H ii regions with the classical T _ e method. The oxygen abundances in these three H ii regions of Sextans A are all consistent within the individual rms uncertainties, with the average 12+ (O/H) = 7.54 0.06. The oxygen abundance of the PN in Sextans A is, however, significantly higher: 12+ (O/H) = 8.02 0.05. This PN is even more enriched in nitrogen and helium, suggesting a classification as a PN of Type I. The PN abundances of S and Ar, which are presumably unaffected by nucleosynthesis in the progenitor star, are well below those in the H ii regions, indicating lower metallicity at the epoch of the PN progenitor formation ( 1.5 Gyr ago, according to our estimates based on the PN parameters).

Sextans A is at a distance of 1.32 Mpc Dolphin03a . Deep HST imaging permitted photometric derivations of the recent and intermediate-age star formation and enrichment histories, but for ages of several Gyr the uncertainties become large Dolphin03b . The best fit is obtained for the assumption of a roughly constant metallicity of 1.4 dex (with an rms scatter of 0.15 dex at all ages) throughout the lifetime of Sextans A Dolphin03b .

sources.summary: The paper uses GBT mapping to test reports of an extended gas envelope around the dwarf irregular Sextans A, finding no large skirt and an extent high among irregulars but not abnormal.

quotes.quote: One galaxy with a potentially large smooth extension of gas is the dwarf irregular Sextans A. Single dish maps of Sextans A by Huchtmeier \ (1981) with the Effelsberg 100 m telescope showed \ extending to a diameter of 54 \ at a column density of 10 ^ 19 . Thus, \ was detected to 8.7 times the galaxy's optically-defined Holmberg diameter.
It is immediately clear from Figure fig-sexam0 that there is no large extended skirt of \ around Sextans A.

Sextans A was then thought to have the third highest ratio with a value of 5.8, with the values of R_ HI and R_ Holm used at that time. With our new data, we find that Sextans A has an R_ HI at 10^ 19 \ of 10.2 , and the ratio R_ HI /R_ Holm is 3.3. (Our R_ Holm are taken from Hunter Elmgreen [ 2006 ] and given in Table tab-sample ). This now puts Sextans A at the high end of the distribution of the bulk of other irregular galaxies and an \ extent that is not particularly abnormal.
